description: This benchmark tests VLMs' ability to act as phoneticians by interpreting spectrograms and waveforms of speech through a multiple-choice task. It contains 4k+ English words with stylistically consistent spectrogram and waveform figures, requiring specialized parametric knowledge for interpretation.
paper_title: Seeing isn't Hearing: Benchmarking Vision Language Models at Interpreting Spectrograms

description: UNICBench is a unified multimodal counting benchmark evaluating MLLMs across image, text, and audio modalities. It comprises 5,300 images, 872 documents, and 2,069 audio clips with a three-level capability taxonomy and standardized evaluation protocol.
paper_title: UNICBench: UNIfied Counting Benchmark for MLLM

description: EgoSound is the first benchmark for evaluating egocentric sound understanding in MLLMs, unifying data from Ego4D and EgoBlind. It features a seven-task taxonomy spanning intrinsic sound perception, spatial localization, causal inference, and cross-modal reasoning specifically in egocentric settings.
paper_title: EgoSound: Benchmarking Sound Understanding in Egocentric Videos

description: An audio-visual benchmark designed for agentic multi-speaker understanding requiring models to decompose complex interactions into planning, grounding, and reflection steps. Evaluates across three modes and six task families including spatio-temporal speaker grounding and multimodal dialogue summarization.
paper_title: AMUSE: Audio-Visual Benchmark and Alignment Framework for Agentic Multi-Speaker Understanding

description: Real-world benchmark for audio-video understanding across 13 conversational domains with demographic metadata. Evaluates temporal localization with supporting rationales and reveals persistent disparities in model behavior across demographic groups.
paper_title: SONIC-O1: A Real-World Benchmark for Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models on Audio-Video Understanding

description: AVMeme Exam evaluates understanding of iconic Internet audio-visual memes spanning speech, songs, music, and sound effects. It uniquely assesses levels from surface content to contextual, emotional, and cultural understanding with time-varying sound and motion signals beyond text representation.
paper_title: AVMeme Exam: A Multimodal Multilingual Multicultural Benchmark for LLMs' Contextual and Cultural Knowledge and Thinking

description: AvaMERG introduces multimodal empathetic response generation by extending traditional text-based empathy with authentic human speech audio and dynamic talking-face avatar videos. It's the first benchmark to evaluate empathetic responses across text, speech, and facial vision modalities in avatar-based interactions.
paper_title: Towards Multimodal Empathetic Response Generation: A Rich Text-Speech-Vision Avatar-based Benchmark

description: AV-SpeakerBench focuses on speaker-centric audiovisual reasoning in real-world videos with 3,212 multiple-choice questions. It uniquely treats speakers as the core reasoning unit and embeds audiovisual dependencies into question semantics, requiring fine-grained alignment of who speaks, what is said, and when it occurs.
paper_title: See, Hear, and Understand: Benchmarking Audiovisual Human Speech Understanding in Multimodal Large Language Models

description: AVUT provides an audio-centric video understanding benchmark that focuses specifically on auditory information rather than treating audio as auxiliary. It addresses the text shortcut problem through answer permutation-based filtering and tests both audio content understanding and audio-visual interactions.
paper_title: Audio-centric Video Understanding Benchmark without Text Shortcut

description: A manually-annotated clue-grounded counting benchmark with 1,027 multimodal questions and 5,845 annotated clues over 497 long videos. Addresses limitations of existing counting benchmarks by providing longer videos, open-set queries, detailed clue annotations, and strong multimodal coverage with both audio and visual elements.
paper_title: AV-Reasoner: Improving and Benchmarking Clue-Grounded Audio-Visual Counting for MLLMs

description: Benchmark for evaluating context-grounded acoustic control in omni-modal models comprising 3,559 verified instances covering six acoustic features. Models must read scripts aloud with appropriate tone and manner given spoken instruction, text script, and image.
paper_title: OmniACBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Context-Grounded Acoustic Control in Omni-Modal Models

description: CoP Benchmark provides detailed multimodal annotations for video-guided piano music generation with Chain-of-Perform guidance enabling precise semantic and temporal alignment. It offers a versatile evaluation framework for both general-purpose and specialized video-to-piano generation tasks with step-by-step performance guidance.
paper_title: Towards Video to Piano Music Generation with Chain-of-Perform Support Benchmarks

description: MultiVox is the first omni voice assistant benchmark designed to evaluate integration of spoken and visual cues including paralinguistic speech features. It includes 1000 human-annotated speech dialogues encompassing diverse paralinguistic features and visual cues, addressing gaps in evaluating context-aware responses from fine-grained speech characteristics.
paper_title: MultiVox: A Benchmark for Evaluating Voice Assistants for Multimodal Interactions

description: First comprehensive benchmark specifically designed to evaluate perception and comprehension capabilities of audio-visual LLMs. Includes tests for hallucinations, cross-modal matching, and reasoning abilities between audio and visual signals.
paper_title: AVHBench: A Cross-Modal Hallucination Benchmark for Audio-Visual Large Language Models

description: Omni-Cloze is a novel cloze-style evaluation benchmark for detailed audio, visual, and audio-visual captioning that ensures stable, efficient, and reliable assessment. It uniquely addresses the gap in dedicated benchmarks for omni detailed perception by providing comprehensive evaluation of fine-grained multimodal understanding.
paper_title: Omni-Captioner: Data Pipeline, Models, and Benchmark for Omni Detailed Perception

description: OmniVideoBench assesses synergistic audio-visual understanding in MLLMs with 1000 QA pairs from 628 diverse videos, emphasizing modality complementarity and logical consistency with step-by-step reasoning traces.
paper_title: OmniVideoBench: Towards Audio-Visual Understanding Evaluation for Omni MLLMs

description: Unified benchmark for multimodal audio-visual evaluation requiring tight integration of video and audio information. Features 2,556 questions from 700 videos across multiple-choice and open-ended formats with human performance baselines.
paper_title: MAVERIX: Multimodal Audio-Visual Evaluation and Recognition IndeX

description: First benchmark to assess multi-modal video understanding with simultaneous visual, audio, and text inputs. Features 1,662 audio-visual synchronized videos across 8 domains and 67 subcategories with 3,172 QA pairs across 26 distinct tasks.
paper_title: WorldSense: Evaluating Real-world Omnimodal Understanding for Multimodal LLMs

description: Presents MaViLS, a benchmark dataset for aligning lecture videos with corresponding slides using a novel multimodal algorithm leveraging speech, OCR, and visual features. The benchmark demonstrates that OCR features contribute most to matching accuracy, followed by image features.
paper_title: MaViLS, a Benchmark Dataset for Video-to-Slide Alignment, Assessing Baseline Accuracy with a Multimodal Alignment Algorithm Leveraging Speech, OCR, and Visual Features

description: Medium-scale benchmark containing real-world videos with synchronized audio across ten diverse anomaly classes for audio-visual anomaly recognition. Addresses limitations of visual-only approaches under challenging conditions like occlusion and low illumination.
paper_title: AVAR-Net: A Lightweight Audio-Visual Anomaly Recognition Framework with a Benchmark Dataset

description: A fine-grained benchmark for evaluating cross-modal reasoning capabilities of Audio-Visual models across six challenging cognitive domains. Uniquely designed with questions that are explicitly unanswerable from a single modality, forcing genuine cross-modal reasoning rather than allowing uni-modal shortcuts.
paper_title: AURA: A Fine-Grained Benchmark and Decomposed Metric for Audio-Visual Reasoning

description: A comprehensive benchmark framework for evaluating synchronous audio-video generation across three task types (T2AV, I2AV, stereo AV). Establishes evaluation modules covering 15 dimensions including audio-video synchronization, lip-speech consistency, and cross-modal QA pairs across seven content categories.
paper_title: VABench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Audio-Video Generation

description: Introduces a multi-modal benchmark for evaluating joint generation of whole-body talking avatars and speech. The benchmark comprises 10,000 unique identities across 2 million video samples with evaluation protocols for assessing both visual and audio quality of whole-body animatable avatars.
paper_title: JWB-DH-V1: Benchmark for Joint Whole-Body Talking Avatar and Speech Generation Version 1

description: First benchmark for Spatially Aligned Audio-Video Generation (SAVG) task, featuring a curated dataset where audio and video data are specifically selected based on whether sound events are onscreen or not. Introduces new alignment metrics to evaluate spatial correspondence between audio and visual elements.
paper_title: SAVGBench: Benchmarking Spatially Aligned Audio-Video Generation

description: Comprehensive Text to Audible-Video Generation benchmark with over 1.7 million clips totaling 11.8 thousand hours. Features automatic annotation pipeline for detailed audio-video descriptions and introduces AVHScore metric for quantitative audio-visual alignment measurement.
paper_title: TAVGBench: Benchmarking Text to Audible-Video Generation

description: MTAVG-Bench is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating multi-speaker dialogue generation in audio-visual videos. It consists of 1.8k videos generated using multiple popular models with carefully designed prompts, yielding 2.4k manually annotated QA pairs.
paper_title: MTAVG-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating Multi-Talker Dialogue-Centric Audio-Video Generation

description: AVHaystacks introduces a novel audio-visual benchmark for the AV-HaystacksQA task, where models must identify salient segments across different videos in response to a query and link them together for comprehensive answers. The benchmark comprises 3100 annotated QA pairs designed to assess multi-video retrieval and temporal grounding capabilities in audio-visual contexts.
paper_title: MAGNET: A Multi-agent Framework for Finding Audio-Visual Needles by Reasoning over Multi-Video Haystacks

description: An audio-visual evaluation benchmark comprising six representative single-modal tasks with five cross-modal tasks reflecting audio-visual co-reasoning abilities. It evaluates fine-grained combination of audio and visual streams for multimodal LLMs.
paper_title: Fine-grained Audio-Visual Joint Representations for Multimodal Large Language Models

description: A new benchmark that introduces two critical, previously missing tasks: composed audio retrieval and audio-visual retrieval. ACM comprehensively evaluates models' omni-modal embedding capacity across text, vision, and audio modalities.
paper_title: Efficient and High-Fidelity Omni Modality Retrieval

description: MAVL is the first multilingual, multimodal benchmark for singable lyrics translation in animated musicals, integrating text, audio, and video modalities. It uniquely addresses the challenge of maintaining musical rhythm, syllabic structure, and visual-auditory alignment in lyrics translation.
paper_title: MAVL: A Multilingual Audio-Video Lyrics Dataset for Animated Song Translation

description: TIMIT-TTS is a synthetic speech dataset containing cutting-edge Text-to-Speech methods designed for multimodal synthetic media detection. It uses a pipeline combining TTS and Dynamic Time Warping techniques to generate realistic speech tracks from real or fake videos, specifically addressing the need for audio-visual deepfake detection datasets.
paper_title: TIMIT-TTS: a Text-to-Speech Dataset for Multimodal Synthetic Media Detection

description: PACS is the first audiovisual benchmark annotated for physical commonsense reasoning attributes, focusing on understanding physical properties and affordances through both vision and acoustics. It brings audio as a core component to multimodal physical reasoning tasks.
paper_title: PACS: A Dataset for Physical Audiovisual CommonSense Reasoning

description: T4-Deception is a multicultural deception detection dataset based on the "To Tell The Truth" television format implemented across four countries, making it the largest non-laboratory deception detection dataset. It includes structured cue-level descriptions and reasoning chains for auditable deception detection.
paper_title: DecepGPT: Schema-Driven Deception Detection with Multicultural Datasets and Robust Multimodal Learning

description: VALOR-1M is a large-scale high-quality tri-modality dataset containing audiable videos with human annotated audiovisual captions. It supports vision-audio-language pretraining research and enables various downstream tasks with different input modality combinations.
paper_title: VALOR: Vision-Audio-Language Omni-Perception Pretraining Model and Dataset

num_samples: 6072 segments over 43 hours of video from 20 movies
description: MObyGaze introduces the first dataset for characterizing and quantifying multimodal objectification in films, with dense expert annotations for objectification levels across visual, speech, and audio modalities. It enables AI analysis of complex multimodal temporal patterns producing objectification in audiovisual content.
paper_title: MObyGaze: a film dataset of multimodal objectification densely annotated by experts

description: Large-scale geometry-acoustics alignment dataset that bridges causal chain from 3D geometry to acoustic signals through physical properties and modal parameters. Enables physically consistent multimodal learning with explicit causal relationships.
paper_title: VibraVerse: A Large-Scale Geometry-Acoustics Alignment Dataset for Physically-Consistent Multimodal Learning

description: PianoVAM is a comprehensive piano performance dataset combining videos, audio, MIDI, hand landmarks, and fingering labels recorded with a Disklavier piano. It enables benchmarking for both audio-only and audio-visual piano transcription while providing synchronized multimodal data for music information retrieval research.
paper_title: PianoVAM: A Multimodal Piano Performance Dataset

num_samples: 20M trailer clips with visual captions, 2M high-quality clips with multimodal captions
description: Presents MMTrail, a large-scale multi-modality video-language dataset incorporating trailer clips with visual captions and high-quality clips with multimodal captions including music descriptions. The dataset explores audio-visual correlation beyond typical video-language datasets.
paper_title: MMTrail: A Multimodal Trailer Video Dataset with Language and Music Descriptions

num_samples: 743 target words and over 414,000 video samples
description: Introduces LRW-Persian, the largest in-the-wild Persian word-level lipreading dataset with wide regional and dialectal coverage. Features speaker-disjoint splits and rich metadata including head pose, age, and gender.
paper_title: LRW-Persian: Lip-reading in the Wild Dataset for Persian Language

description: Introduces SlideAVSR, a new audio-visual speech recognition dataset using scientific paper explanation videos where models must transcribe speech while referencing textual information on presentation slides. The dataset focuses on technical terminology transcription challenges and provides a new benchmark for evaluating AVSR models in academic presentation contexts.
paper_title: SlideAVSR: A Dataset of Paper Explanation Videos for Audio-Visual Speech Recognition

description: iMiGUE-Speech extends the original iMiGUE dataset to provide a spontaneous affective speech corpus with enriched metadata including transcripts, speaker-role separation, and word-level alignments. Unlike acted emotional speech datasets, it captures spontaneous affect from real match outcomes, offering benchmarks for speech emotion recognition and transcript-based sentiment analysis.
paper_title: iMiGUE-Speech: A Spontaneous Speech Dataset for Affective Analysis

description: DOLOS is the largest gameshow deception detection dataset featuring 1,675 video clips with 213 subjects and rich deceptive conversations. It provides audio-visual feature annotations and multiple evaluation protocols (train-test, duration, gender) for benchmarking multimodal deception detection approaches.
paper_title: Audio-Visual Deception Detection: DOLOS Dataset and Parameter-Efficient Crossmodal Learning

description: MUSIC-AVQA v2.0 addresses pronounced answer biases in the original dataset by carefully curating complementary videos and questions to ensure balanced answer distributions. For binary questions, it achieves near-uniform distribution across answer categories, creating a more challenging and unbiased benchmark for audio-visual question answering.
paper_title: Tackling Data Bias in MUSIC-AVQA: Crafting a Balanced Dataset for Unbiased Question-Answering

description: A task-driven benchmark for Text-to-Audio-Video generation featuring high-quality prompts across 11 real-world categories. Introduces a multi-granular evaluation framework combining lightweight specialist models with MLLMs to assess both perceptual quality and semantic controllability.
paper_title: AVGen-Bench: A Task-Driven Benchmark for Multi-Granular Evaluation of Text-to-Audio-Video Generation

description: A benchmark of over 2,700 conflict stimuli spanning emotional prosody, background sounds, and speaker identity to evaluate acoustic faithfulness in Audio MLLMs. Uses controlled multi-level evaluation framework to distinguish genuine acoustic processing from text-based semantic inference.
paper_title: DEAF: A Benchmark for Diagnostic Evaluation of Acoustic Faithfulness in Audio Language Models

description: A benchmark for dialogue-conditioned background music recommendation featuring open-domain daily dialogues paired with four candidate music clips and human preference rankings. Evaluates models on contextual relevance, non-intrusiveness, and consistency for BGM selection.
paper_title: DialBGM: A Benchmark for Background Music Recommendation from Everyday Multi-Turn Dialogues

description: A customizable 4D environment for assessing selective cross-modal perception and time awareness in Omni models. Incorporates trigger-based auditory sources, temporally transient evidence, and location-dependent cues requiring spatio-temporal reasoning under time constraints.
paper_title: Evaluating Time Awareness and Cross-modal Active Perception of Large Models via 4D Escape Room Task

description: General Meeting Understanding and Generation Benchmark with AliMeeting4MUG Corpus containing 654 recorded Mandarin meeting sessions. Supports topic segmentation, summarization, keyphrase extraction, and action item detection tasks.
paper_title: MUG: A General Meeting Understanding and Generation Benchmark

description: CCFQA is a cross-lingual and cross-modal factuality benchmark containing parallel speech-text factual questions across 8 languages. It systematically evaluates MLLMs' cross-lingual and cross-modal factuality capabilities in multilingual spoken question answering.
paper_title: CCFQA: A Benchmark for Cross-Lingual and Cross-Modal Speech and Text Factuality Evaluation

description: A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating audio language models' emotional intelligence using real-recorded human dialogues. Reformulates emotional tracking and causal reasoning into multiple-choice questions with adversarial distractors.
paper_title: HumDial-EIBench: A Human-Recorded Multi-Turn Emotional Intelligence Benchmark for Audio Language Models

description: A multi-task benchmark for Video-to-Audio generation evaluation across four audio categories (sound effects, music, speech, singing). Features task-specific, reference-free metrics validated through subjective studies.
paper_title: VidAudio-Bench: Benchmarking V2A and VT2A Generation across Four Audio Categories

description: The first large-scale benchmark for audio-visual inconsistency understanding in videos with 11.2K long videos and 39.4K annotated inconsistency events. It evaluates cross-modal conflict perception through detection, temporal grounding, classification, and reasoning across 8 fine-grained inconsistency categories.
paper_title: AVID: A Benchmark for Omni-Modal Audio-Visual Inconsistency Understanding via Agent-Driven Construction

description: A comprehensive evaluation benchmark for VLMs in chart grounding tasks including extracting tabular data, localizing visualization elements, and recognizing attributes. Features a two-stage inference workflow to evaluate alignment capabilities across multiple charts.
paper_title: ChartAB: A Benchmark for Chart Grounding & Dense Alignment

description: First benchmark for complex multimodal table reasoning integrating semi-structured data with visual elements like charts and maps. Evaluates across four question types, five reasoning categories, and eight table types from diverse real-world sources requiring visual-based reasoning and multi-step inference.
paper_title: MMTBENCH: A Unified Benchmark for Complex Multimodal Table Reasoning

description: FinMTM is a multi-turn multimodal benchmark specifically designed for financial reasoning and agent evaluation. It expands diversity along both data and task dimensions with bilingual financial QA pairs grounded in specialized financial visuals like candlestick charts and statistical plots.
paper_title: FinMTM: A Multi-Turn Multimodal Benchmark for Financial Reasoning and Agent Evaluation

description: InterChart is a diagnostic benchmark that evaluates how well VLMs reason across multiple related charts, challenging models with diverse question types from entity inference to abstract multi-step reasoning. It organizes evaluation into three tiers of increasing difficulty based on chart complexity and integration requirements.
paper_title: InterChart: Benchmarking Visual Reasoning Across Decomposed and Distributed Chart Information

description: A synthetic benchmark with 2,076 QA pairs providing systematic coverage of six visual encoding channels (position, length, area, color, shape) and eight analytic tasks for chart understanding. Informed by visualization literature for balanced coverage.
paper_title: EncQA: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models on Visual Encodings for Charts

description: A large-scale, open-domain multimodal dataset with 2.5k richly structured LaTeX-rendered tables and 6k reasoning-intensive QA pairs designed for visual reasoning over complex tabular data. It uniquely employs a modular, scalable autonomous generation pipeline with multi-model collaborative data generation.
paper_title: Visual-TableQA: Open-Domain Benchmark for Reasoning over Table Images

description: This work provides the first systematic evaluation of VLMs on standardized visualization literacy tests (VLAT and CALVI) with controlled methodology. It reveals distinct performance patterns in chart interpretation versus misleading visualization detection, establishing reliable benchmarks for visualization understanding.
paper_title: Benchmarking Visual Language Models on Standardized Visualization Literacy Tests

description: EvoChart-QA contains 650 distinct real-world charts from 140 websites with 1,250 expert-curated questions focusing on chart comprehension in real-world scenarios. It provides a challenging benchmark where even GPT-4o achieves only 49.8% accuracy, highlighting gaps in current chart understanding capabilities.
paper_title: EvoChart: A Benchmark and a Self-Training Approach Towards Real-World Chart Understanding

description: The first large-scale benchmark for cross-chart comparative summarization consisting of chart pairs with LLM-generated and human-verified summaries describing differences in trends, fluctuations, and anomalies. It addresses the gap in comparative reasoning across multiple charts.
paper_title: ChartDiff: A Large-Scale Benchmark for Comprehending Pairs of Charts

description: Benchmark with 1,000 multiple-choice questions for cross-modal reasoning over tables and charts from 4,000 Wikipedia pages. Emphasizes complex reasoning requiring synthesis of information from multiple modalities and long-context processing.
paper_title: WikiMixQA: A Multimodal Benchmark for Question Answering over Tables and Charts

description: More diverse and challenging chart QA benchmark with 1,341 charts from 157 sources and 1,948 questions including multiple-choice, conversational, hypothetical, and unanswerable questions. Addresses limitations of existing benchmarks with real-world diversity.
paper_title: ChartQAPro: A More Diverse and Challenging Benchmark for Chart Question Answering

description: Comprehensive benchmark for complex chart question answering in real-world settings covering seven task categories with multilingual contexts and open-domain textual outputs. Bridges gap between real-world applications and traditional academic benchmarks with diverse chart formats.
paper_title: ChartMind: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Complex Real-world Multimodal Chart Question Answering

description: Chart-to-Experience evaluates MLLMs' ability to predict the perceptual and emotional impact of charts on seven experiential factors. The benchmark uses crowdsourced human evaluations as ground truth and tests MLLMs on both direct prediction and pairwise comparison tasks for chart impact assessment.
paper_title: Chart-to-Experience: Benchmarking Multimodal LLMs for Predicting Experiential Impact of Charts

description: ChartBench assesses chart comprehension through complex visual reasoning with 42 categories, 66.6k charts, and 600k QA pairs. Many charts lack data point annotations, requiring MLLMs to derive values using inherent chart elements like colors, legends, and coordinates, similar to human understanding.
paper_title: ChartBench: A Benchmark for Complex Visual Reasoning in Charts

description: Chart-HQA introduces a novel Chart Hypothetical Question Answering task that uses counterfactual reasoning to test genuine chart understanding rather than parametric memory. It uses a human-AI interactive synthesis approach to generate diverse HQA data at low cost.
paper_title: Chart-HQA: A Benchmark for Hypothetical Question Answering in Charts

description: InfoChartQA evaluates MLLMs on infographic chart understanding with 5,642 pairs of infographic and plain charts sharing the same data but different visual presentations. It includes visual-element-based questions to capture unique visual designs and communicative intent.
paper_title: InfoChartQA: A Benchmark for Multimodal Question Answering on Infographic Charts

description: MultiChartQA addresses the gap in multi-chart scenarios by evaluating MLLMs on four key areas: direct QA, parallel QA, comparative reasoning, and sequential reasoning. It requires multi-hop reasoning to extract and integrate information from multiple charts.
paper_title: MultiChartQA: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models on Multi-Chart Problems

description: CFBenchmark-MM is a Chinese multimodal financial benchmark featuring over 9,000 image-question pairs with tables, histogram charts, line charts, pie charts, and structural diagrams. It includes a staged evaluation system to assess MLLMs' handling of multimodal financial information.
paper_title: CFBenchmark-MM: Chinese Financial Assistant Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Model

description: Introduces the first large-scale multimodal dataset to evaluate MLLMs' ability to detect and interpret misleading chart visualizations. The benchmark contains 3,026 curated examples spanning 21 misleader types and 10 chart types with standardized chart code, CSV data, multiple-choice questions, and labeled explanations.
paper_title: Unmasking Deceptive Visuals: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models on Misleading Chart Question Answering

description: Introduces a comprehensive financial multimodal reasoning evaluation dataset with more than 11,000 high-quality samples across 18 financial domains and 6 asset classes. Features 10 major chart types and develops FinScore evaluation system with hallucination penalties and multi-dimensional capability assessment.
paper_title: FinMME: Benchmark Dataset for Financial Multi-Modal Reasoning Evaluation

description: ChartM3 introduces a novel paradigm for multimodal chart editing combining natural language with visual indicators to explicitly highlight elements for modification. It provides multi-level complexity evaluation and multi-perspective assessment of both visual appearance and code correctness.
paper_title: ChartM$^3$: Benchmarking Chart Editing with Multimodal Instructions

description: IGenBench is the first benchmark for evaluating text-to-infographic generation reliability, comprising 600 curated test cases across 30 infographic types. It uniquely decomposes reliability verification into atomic yes/no questions using a taxonomy of 10 question types, focusing on easily overlooked issues like distorted data encoding.
paper_title: IGenBench: Benchmarking the Reliability of Text-to-Infographic Generation

description: CHARTOM evaluates multimodal large language models' theory-of-mind capabilities by testing their ability to understand misleading data visualizations in charts. It uniquely assesses both factual comprehension (FACT questions) and the ability to judge whether charts will mislead human readers (MIND questions), calibrated against human performance.
paper_title: CHARTOM: A Visual Theory-of-Mind Benchmark for LLMs on Misleading Charts

description: ChartX is a comprehensive multi-modal evaluation benchmark covering 18 chart types, 7 chart tasks, and 22 disciplinary topics for evaluating MLLMs' chart reasoning capabilities. It provides high-quality chart data with a focus on interpretable pattern recognition in chart-related tasks.
paper_title: ChartX & ChartVLM: A Versatile Benchmark and Foundation Model for Complicated Chart Reasoning

description: Plot2Code is a comprehensive visual coding benchmark with 132 manually selected high-quality matplotlib plots across six plot types. It evaluates MLLMs' capabilities in turning visual figures into executable code, featuring automatic evaluation metrics including code pass rate, text-match ratio, and GPT-4V overall rating.
paper_title: Plot2Code: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating Multi-modal Large Language Models in Code Generation from Scientific Plots

description: ChartE³ is an End-to-End Chart Editing benchmark that directly evaluates models without relying on intermediate representations. It focuses on local editing (fine-grained appearance changes) and global editing (holistic data-centric transformations) with over 1,200 high-quality samples provided as triplets of chart image, code, and multimodal editing instruction.
paper_title: ChartE$^{3}$: A Comprehensive Benchmark for End-to-End Chart Editing

description: ChemTable is a large-scale benchmark of chemical tables from real-world literature with expert-annotated cell layouts, logical structures, and domain-specific labels. It uniquely focuses on the complex multimodal challenge of understanding scientific tables that combine structured data with visual symbols like molecular structures and chemical formulas.
paper_title: Benchmarking Multimodal LLMs on Recognition and Understanding over Chemical Tables

description: ViInfographicVQA is the first Vietnamese InfographicVQA benchmark featuring both single-image and multi-image evaluation settings. It requires integration of OCR, layout understanding, and numerical reasoning on data-rich visuals, with the multi-image task being the first Vietnamese evaluation of cross-image reasoning in VQA.
paper_title: ViInfographicVQA: A Benchmark for Single and Multi-image Visual Question Answering on Vietnamese Infographics

description: LiveXiv is a scalable evolving live benchmark based on scientific ArXiv papers that automatically generates visual question-answer pairs from multi-modal manuscript content like graphs, charts, and tables. It safeguards against test data contamination by accessing domain-specific manuscripts at any timestamp and introduces efficient evaluation approaches to reduce overall cost.
paper_title: LiveXiv -- A Multi-Modal Live Benchmark Based on Arxiv Papers Content

description: Chart-MRAG Bench is a comprehensive benchmark for chart-based multimodal RAG evaluation featuring 4,738 question-answering pairs across 8 domains from real-world documents. It introduces the novel Chart-based MRAG task using the CHARGE framework for semi-automatic generation of high-quality evaluation samples.
paper_title: Benchmarking Multimodal RAG through a Chart-based Document Question-Answering Generation Framework

description: Chart2Code is a hierarchical benchmark with three levels of increasing difficulty: chart reproduction, chart editing, and long-table to chart generation. It contains 2,023 tasks across 22 chart types with multi-level evaluation metrics assessing both code correctness and visual fidelity of rendered charts.
paper_title: From Charts to Code: A Hierarchical Benchmark for Multimodal Models

description: GRAFT is a structured multimodal benchmark that evaluates LLMs on instruction following, visual reasoning, and visual-textual alignment tasks using programmatically generated charts and synthetically rendered tables. It uniquely focuses on structured outputs (JSON/YAML) and introduces a comprehensive taxonomy of reasoning operations including comparison, trend identification, ranking, and anomaly detection.
paper_title: GRAFT: GRaPH and Table Reasoning for Textual Alignment -- A Benchmark for Structured Instruction Following and Visual Reasoning

description: RealCQA-V2 reformulates chart question answering as Visual Premise Proving (VPP), a structured logical entailment task over chart-grounded visual predicates. It uniquely enables verification at the level of individual visual statements and complete reasoning sequences, providing atomic premise evaluation rather than just final-answer correctness.
paper_title: RealCQA-V2: A Diagnostic Benchmark for Structured Visual Entailment over Scientific Charts

description: The first large-scale benchmark to evaluate chart generation from raw data and assess iterative code refinement in multi-turn conversational settings. Features over 2,800 instances with authentic datasets and complex multi-panel visualizations.
paper_title: RealChart2Code: Advancing Chart-to-Code Generation with Real Data and Multi-Task Evaluation

description: Comprehensive benchmark for evaluating plot-to-code generation under realistic and complex visualization scenarios. Spans 9 major categories, 30 subcategories, and 3 core tasks (plot replication, transformation, multi-library generation) covering 2D, 3D and animated plots across 5 visualization libraries.
paper_title: PlotGen-Bench: Evaluating VLMs on Generating Visualization Code from Diverse Plots across Multiple Libraries

description: Benchmark combining real-world visualizations with human-authored misleading captions designed to elicit specific reasoning and visualization error types. Enables controlled analysis of VLM ability to detect deceptive data representations across error categories.
paper_title: When Visuals Aren't the Problem: Evaluating Vision-Language Models on Misleading Data Visualizations

description: First comprehensive evaluation method for MLLMs on flowchart-related tasks across multiple dimensions including reasoning, localization recognition, information extraction, logical verification, and summarization. Tests specific flowchart comprehension abilities.
paper_title: First Multi-Dimensional Evaluation of Flowchart Comprehension for Multimodal Large Language Models

description: FCMR evaluates financial cross-modal multi-hop reasoning by requiring MLLMs to integrate information from textual reports, tables, and charts within the financial domain. It uniquely addresses data contamination issues and tests complex three-hop reasoning across three modalities with three difficulty levels.
paper_title: FCMR: Robust Evaluation of Financial Cross-Modal Multi-Hop Reasoning

description: ChartInsights evaluates MLLMs on low-level ChartQA tasks covering 10 data analysis tasks across 7 chart types, focusing on correlation identification and detailed chart analysis rather than high-level captioning.
paper_title: ChartInsights: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models for Low-Level Chart Question Answering

description: Novel benchmark for chart editing tasks featuring 1,405 diverse editing instructions applied to 233 real-world charts, manually annotated and validated. Evaluates MLLMs' capability to integrate chart understanding and reasoning for code-based chart modification, revealing limitations in precise editing according to instructions.
paper_title: ChartEdit: How Far Are MLLMs From Automating Chart Analysis? Evaluating MLLMs' Capability via Chart Editing

description: Human-centered evaluation using six data visualization literacy assessments designed for humans, comparing vision-language model responses to human participants. Evaluates data visualization understanding using measures typically used to assess these abilities in humans rather than conventional ML metrics.
paper_title: CHART-6: Human-Centered Evaluation of Data Visualization Understanding in Vision-Language Models

description: Evaluates large vision-language models on stock chart captioning to generate informative descriptions and market sentiment analysis. Focuses on technical analysis interpretation for financial charts to assist non-expert investors.
paper_title: StockGenChaR: A Study on the Evaluation of Large Vision-Language Models on Stock Chart Captioning

description: Introduces OmniSch, the first comprehensive benchmark for assessing LMMs on PCB schematic understanding and spatial netlist graph construction. The benchmark includes four tasks: visual grounding, diagram-to-graph reasoning, geometric reasoning, and tool-augmented agentic reasoning.
paper_title: OmniSch: A Multimodal PCB Schematic Benchmark For Structured Diagram Visual Reasoning

num_samples: 22,606 charts and 26,151 QA pairs across 10 languages
description: Introduces PolyChartQA, the first large-scale multilingual benchmark for chart question answering across 10 diverse languages. The benchmark addresses the English-centric limitation of existing chart understanding benchmarks and reveals significant performance gaps between English and other languages.
paper_title: POLYCHARTQA: Benchmarking Large Vision-Language Models with Multilingual Chart Question Answering

description: Introduces FinChart-Bench, the first benchmark specifically focused on real-world financial charts. The benchmark comprises financial chart images from 2015-2024 with True/False, Multiple Choice, and QA questions, revealing critical limitations in current LVLM capabilities for financial chart understanding.
paper_title: FinChart-Bench: Benchmarking Financial Chart Comprehension in Vision-Language Models

description: Introduces SCI-CQA (Scientific Chart QA), a comprehensive benchmark emphasizing flowcharts and complex scientific charts from top-tier computer science conferences. The benchmark addresses limitations in chart variety and simplistic visual elements with a novel evaluation framework inspired by human exams.
paper_title: Rethinking Comprehensive Benchmark for Chart Understanding: A Perspective from Scientific Literature

description: DashboardQA is the first benchmark designed for interactive dashboard reasoning, evaluating vision-language GUI agents on real-world Tableau dashboards with rich interactivity. It spans five question categories and tests agents' ability to comprehend, interact with, and reason about dynamic visualization interfaces.
paper_title: DashboardQA: Benchmarking Multimodal Agents for Question Answering on Interactive Dashboards

description: Benchmark for generating multimodal visualizations from text covering 20+ chart types and diverse data science queries including trend analysis and predictive analytics. Tests complex reasoning, conversational turns, and dynamic data retrieval for visualization generation.
paper_title: Text2Vis: A Challenging and Diverse Benchmark for Generating Multimodal Visualizations from Text

description: ChartHal provides a fine-grained taxonomy of hallucination scenarios specifically in chart understanding tasks. The benchmark evaluates how LVLMs handle information that is absent from or contradictory to chart content, addressing a critical gap in chart-specific hallucination evaluation.
paper_title: ChartHal: A Fine-grained Framework Evaluating Hallucination of Large Vision Language Models in Chart Understanding

description: RADAR is a comprehensive benchmark dataset with 17,819 diverse samples featuring charts, questions, reasoning steps, and attribution annotations for explainable visual data analysis. It evaluates models' ability to highlight specific regions in charts and graphs that justify their answers, focusing on attribution accuracy and interpretability.
paper_title: RADAR: A Reasoning-Guided Attribution Framework for Explainable Visual Data Analysis

description: A fully-automated pipeline for code-guided synthetic chart generation that creates chart-image code pairs from seed images. Provides a curated held-out evaluation subset of 4.3K chart image-code pairs covering 27 chart types, 11 plotting libraries, and multiple data modalities for chart-to-code reconstruction evaluation.
paper_title: ChartGen: Scaling Chart Understanding Via Code-Guided Synthetic Chart Generation

description: Taxonomy-based chart dataset covering thirty different chart types based on visualization community taxonomy, addressing limitations of existing datasets that cover only small sets of chart types. Provides classified chart images without learning signals.
paper_title: ChartComplete: A Taxonomy-based Inclusive Chart Dataset

description: ChartNet is a million-scale multimodal dataset for chart understanding with 1.5 million diverse chart samples spanning 24 chart types and 6 plotting libraries. Each sample includes five aligned components: plotting code, rendered chart image, data table, natural language summary, and QA with reasoning.
paper_title: ChartNet: A Million-Scale, High-Quality Multimodal Dataset for Robust Chart Understanding

description: A dataset for understanding and generating infographic charts that combines visual elements with textual information, addressing the gap between plain charts and complex infographic visualizations. It captures the visual and structural complexity of real design through 75 chart types, 440 chart variations, and 68 layout templates.
paper_title: ChartGalaxy: A Dataset for Infographic Chart Understanding and Generation

description: A VQA benchmark for scientific charts focusing on distribution-based questions where there is no 1-to-1 correspondence between chart marks and underlying data. It generates synthetic histogram charts with ground truth data to test reasoning that requires access to underlying distributions rather than just visual elements.
paper_title: What Lies Beneath: A Call for Distribution-based Visual Question & Answer Datasets

description: PUB presents a synthetic benchmark dataset for evaluating LLM proficiency in interpreting data visualizations including time series, histograms, violins, boxplots, and clusters. Generated automatically with controlled parameters to ensure comprehensive coverage and eliminate pre-learned responses, it provides unbiased evaluation of visual interpretative abilities with quantitative metrics for robust assessment.
paper_title: PUB: Plot Understanding Benchmark and Dataset for Evaluating Large Language Models on Synthetic Visual Data Interpretation

description: A multi-scale candlestick charts dataset with standardized evaluation framework for assessing VLMs' ability to integrate short-term and long-term visual market dynamics. Evaluates stock price forecasting using confusion-matrix-based diagnostics and information coefficient time series metrics.
paper_title: Do VLMs Truly "Read" Candlesticks? A Multi-Scale Benchmark for Visual Stock Price Forecasting

description: A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating VLMs on long, visually complex documents ranging from 5 to 200 pages. Strategically inserts text or multimodal 'needles' at various depths to challenge retrieval capabilities.
paper_title: Document Haystack: A Long Context Multimodal Image/Document Understanding Vision LLM Benchmark

description: This benchmark evaluates VLMs on OCR tasks in dynamic video environments across diverse domains including code editors, news broadcasts, and advertisements. It uniquely focuses on video-based OCR challenges and compares VLMs against traditional OCR systems.
paper_title: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models on Optical Character Recognition in Dynamic Video Environments

description: AncientDoc is the first benchmark for Chinese ancient documents, assessing VLMs from OCR to knowledge reasoning across five tasks including vernacular translation and reasoning-based QA. It uniquely addresses the gap in evaluating VLMs on historical Chinese documents with complex visual and linguistic characteristics.
paper_title: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models on Chinese Ancient Documents: From OCR to Knowledge Reasoning

description: Image2Struct evaluates VLMs on extracting underlying structure from images through round-trip evaluation, where generated structure is rendered and compared against input images. It uniquely provides fully automatic evaluation without human judgment using a renewable stream of fresh data across webpages, LaTeX, and musical scores.
paper_title: Image2Struct: Benchmarking Structure Extraction for Vision-Language Models

description: ThaiOCRBench is the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating VLMs on Thai text-rich visual understanding across 13 task categories. It addresses the underrepresentation of Thai language in existing benchmarks, particularly for document structure understanding tasks.
paper_title: ThaiOCRBench: A Task-Diverse Benchmark for Vision-Language Understanding in Thai

description: ExStrucTiny is a benchmark for schema-variable structured information extraction from document images, unifying aspects of Key Entity Extraction, Relation Extraction, and Visual Question Answering. It covers more varied document types and extraction scenarios than existing datasets through a novel pipeline combining manual and synthetic human-validated samples.
paper_title: ExStrucTiny: A Benchmark for Schema-Variable Structured Information Extraction from Document Images

description: A large-scale bilingual (Chinese and English) benchmark for Visually Rich Document Understanding with 72K images and 600K+ QA pairs. Features complex layouts from newspapers and magazines with comprehensive multi-task annotations (OCR, VQA, reading order, localization).
paper_title: MosaicDoc: A Large-Scale Bilingual Benchmark for Visually Rich Document Understanding

description: KRETA is the first comprehensive benchmark for Korean text-rich VQA, addressing the gap for low-resource languages. It enables evaluation of both visual text understanding and reasoning capabilities across 15 domains and 26 image types, with a semi-automated generation pipeline optimized for text-rich settings.
paper_title: KRETA: A Benchmark for Korean Reading and Reasoning in Text-Rich VQA Attuned to Diverse Visual Contexts

description: HCT-QA addresses question answering on human-centric tables with complex structural and semantic layouts found in PDFs and web pages. It includes 1,880 real-world HCTs with 9,835 QA pairs plus 4,679 synthetic HCTs with 67.7K QA pairs, providing comprehensive metadata for deeper analysis.
paper_title: HCT-QA: A Benchmark for Question Answering on Human-Centric Tables

description: A French-focused benchmark for PDF-to-Markdown conversion using challenging pages selected via model-disagreement sampling from 60,000 documents. It uses unit-test-style checks targeting concrete failure modes with category-specific normalization to discount presentation-only variance.
paper_title: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models for French PDF-to-Markdown Conversion

task_types: Dense Scene Text Visual Question Answering
description: A diagnostic benchmark for Japanese scene text understanding with 3,241 instances from 2,961 newly captured images, featuring 1.12 million annotated characters. It uniquely addresses Japanese-specific challenges including mixed scripts, vertical writing, and large character inventory through three complementary tasks.
paper_title: JaWildText: A Benchmark for Vision-Language Models on Japanese Scene Text Understanding

description: The first benchmark performing full-scale, one-to-one physical reconstruction of the entire OmniDocBench v1.5 across five critical real-world scenarios: Scanning, Warping, Screen-Photography, Illumination, and Skew. It uniquely enables rigorous factor-wise attribution of performance degradation with complete ground-truth mapping.
paper_title: Real5-OmniDocBench: A Full-Scale Physical Reconstruction Benchmark for Robust Document Parsing in the Wild

description: NoTeS-Bank is an evaluation benchmark for Neural Transcription and Search in note-based question answering with complex handwritten notes across multiple domains. It requires vision-language fusion and multimodal reasoning beyond traditional OCR-based approaches.
paper_title: NoTeS-Bank: Benchmarking Neural Transcription and Search for Scientific Notes Understanding

description: FinCriticalED provides 500 image-HTML pairs with expert-annotated financial facts covering over 700 numerical and temporal facts. It establishes the first fact-level evaluation benchmark for financial document understanding, focusing on domain-critical factual correctness rather than lexical similarity.
paper_title: FinCriticalED: A Visual Benchmark for Financial Fact-Level OCR Evaluation

description: FCMBench is the first large-scale financial credit multimodal benchmark covering 26 certificate types with privacy-compliant synthetic data. It evaluates models on perception and reasoning tasks specific to financial credit workflows, including robustness testing under real-world challenges.
paper_title: FCMBench: The First Large-scale Financial Credit Multimodal Benchmark for Real-world Applications

description: PosterSum is a novel benchmark for scientific poster summarization that challenges MLLMs to understand and summarize visually complex scientific posters into research paper abstracts. It features diverse visual challenges including complex layouts, dense text, tables, and figures.
paper_title: PosterSum: A Multimodal Benchmark for Scientific Poster Summarization

description: AECV-Bench evaluates multimodal models on architectural and engineering drawings through object counting on 120 floor plans and drawing-grounded document QA spanning 192 question-answer pairs. It tests interpretation of technical drawings with complex layouts, symbols, and dense annotations.
paper_title: AECV-Bench: Benchmarking Multimodal Models on Architectural and Engineering Drawings Understanding

description: A novel benchmark constructed from real-world reports requiring reasoning across heterogeneous visual and textual elements under strict non-disclosure policies. It addresses the Reasoning-Induced Safety Gap where models leak sensitive information during complex synthesis across modalities.
paper_title: Doc-PP: Document Policy Preservation Benchmark for Large Vision-Language Models

description: Extends the MIRACL dataset to create a multilingual visual document retrieval benchmark covering 18 languages. It introduces a method for eliminating 'easy' negatives and focuses on evaluating retrieval pipelines for documents with complex visual layouts.
paper_title: MIRACL-VISION: A Large, multilingual, visual document retrieval benchmark

description: Comprehensive multimodal long document benchmark integrating Understanding, Reasoning, and Locating tasks with 2,325 QA pairs covering over 33,000 pages. Features 20 sub-tasks across three primary categories for comprehensive document analysis.
paper_title: LongDocURL: a Comprehensive Multimodal Long Document Benchmark Integrating Understanding, Reasoning, and Locating

description: Introduces a benchmarking framework for mathematical formula extraction from PDFs using synthetically generated documents with precise LaTeX ground truth. Pioneers LLM-as-a-judge for semantic formula assessment with robust two-stage matching pipeline to handle parser output inconsistencies.
paper_title: Benchmarking Document Parsers on Mathematical Formula Extraction from PDFs

description: Comprehensive document parsing benchmark with high-quality annotations across nine document sources including challenging cases like handwritten notes and densely typeset newspapers. Supports flexible multi-level evaluation with 19 layout categories and 15 attribute labels.
paper_title: OmniDocBench: Benchmarking Diverse PDF Document Parsing with Comprehensive Annotations

description: MMDocIR introduces the first comprehensive benchmark for multimodal document retrieval with two distinct tasks: page-level and layout-level retrieval. It features 1,685 expert-annotated questions and 173,843 bootstrapped questions, focusing on retrieving specific document elements like figures, tables, and charts from extensive documents.
paper_title: MMDocIR: Benchmarking Multimodal Retrieval for Long Documents

description: MMDocBench provides comprehensive evaluation of fine-grained visual document understanding with 15 main tasks, 4,338 QA pairs, and 11,353 supporting regions across diverse document types (research papers, receipts, financial reports, Wikipedia tables, charts, infographics). It focuses specifically on OCR-free document understanding capabilities.
paper_title: MMDocBench: Benchmarking Large Vision-Language Models for Fine-Grained Visual Document Understanding

description: NL-DIR introduces Natural Language-based Document Image Retrieval with 41K authentic document images, each paired with five high-quality, fine-grained semantic queries. It bridges the gap between existing image-based DIR methods and real-world scenarios where textual queries with fine-grained semantics are provided, using LLMs for query generation and manual verification.
paper_title: Towards Natural Language-Based Document Image Retrieval: New Dataset and Benchmark

description: EssayJudge is the first multimodal benchmark for Automated Essay Scoring, evaluating capabilities across lexical-, sentence-, and discourse-level traits. It leverages MLLMs for trait-specific scoring and multimodal context understanding without manual feature engineering.
paper_title: EssayJudge: A Multi-Granular Benchmark for Assessing Automated Essay Scoring Capabilities of Multimodal Large Language Models

description: KazakhOCR is a synthetic OCR dataset for low-resource Kazakh scripts using Arabic, Cyrillic, and Latin writing systems. It contains 7,219 images with font, color, and noise variations to evaluate MLLM performance on multilingual OCR and language identification tasks.
paper_title: KazakhOCR: A Synthetic Benchmark for Evaluating Multimodal Models in Low-Resource Kazakh Script OCR

description: Introduces the first large-scale Chinese benchmark spanning full front-middle-back office lifecycle of financial tasks. Comprises 15,848 QA pairs across eight financial image modalities organized into three hierarchical scenario depths, evaluating comprehensive financial understanding capabilities.
paper_title: VisFinEval: A Scenario-Driven Chinese Multimodal Benchmark for Holistic Financial Understanding

description: A benchmark specifically designed for evaluating text-rich visual comprehension of MLLMs with 2.3K questions spanning Charts, Maps, and Webs categories. Unique for focusing exclusively on text-rich scenarios ubiquitous in real-world applications, addressing a gap in existing MLLM benchmarks.
paper_title: SEED-Bench-2-Plus: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models with Text-Rich Visual Comprehension

description: A benchmark evaluating MLLMs' understanding of engineering documentation combining textual design requirements, CAD images, and engineering drawings from Formula SAE competition. Unique for its document-grounded visual questions where input image and document come from different sources, testing real-world engineering design comprehension.
paper_title: DesignQA: A Multimodal Benchmark for Evaluating Large Language Models' Understanding of Engineering Documentation

description: Comprehensive benchmark for One-Image Guide understanding across diverse domains. One-Image Guides combine text, imagery, and symbols in structured format designed for human viewing, embodying characteristics of human perception and understanding.
paper_title: OIG-Bench: A Multi-Agent Annotated Benchmark for Multimodal One-Image Guides Understanding

description: A bilingual multimodal benchmark for financial numerical reasoning with 837 documents averaging 50.8 pages. Features scenario awareness with 12 implicit financial scenario types, extensive document understanding, and multi-step computation requiring 11-step reasoning on average.
paper_title: FinMMDocR: Benchmarking Financial Multimodal Reasoning with Scenario Awareness, Document Understanding, and Multi-Step Computation

description: A comprehensive benchmark for photographed document parsing and translation addressing real-world capture conditions. Includes over 1,300 high-resolution photographed documents with geometric distortions and photometric variations, filling the gap left by pristine digital document benchmarks.
paper_title: DocPTBench: Benchmarking End-to-End Photographed Document Parsing and Translation

description: A scan-oriented academic paper reasoning benchmark requiring models to read and cross-check entire papers like human researchers. Features 1,800 questions across nine error categories and 13 domains with detailed annotations for evidence localization and reasoning traces, moving beyond search-oriented paradigms.
paper_title: Not Search, But Scan: Benchmarking MLLMs on Scan-Oriented Academic Paper Reasoning

description: Focuses on mind map structure recognition and analysis with five types of structured understanding tasks. Addresses complex interactions between elements in structured documents like mind maps and flowcharts beyond simple text extraction.
paper_title: MindBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Mind Map Structure Recognition and Analysis

description: Diagnostic benchmark for markup-driven table understanding that decomposes evaluation into five task families. Distinguishes between models that fail to see markup versus fail to reason with it in table-centric documents.
paper_title: HighlightBench: Benchmarking Markup-Driven Table Reasoning in Scientific Documents

description: OCR-Reasoning systematically evaluates MLLMs on text-rich image reasoning across 6 core abilities and 18 practical tasks. Unlike other benchmarks that only annotate final answers, it uniquely annotates the reasoning process simultaneously, enabling holistic analysis of problem-solving abilities in text-rich scenarios.
paper_title: OCR-Reasoning Benchmark: Unveiling the True Capabilities of MLLMs in Complex Text-Rich Image Reasoning

description: FinRAGBench-V is a comprehensive visual RAG benchmark for finance that integrates multimodal data with visual citation for traceability. It uniquely addresses the gap in financial RAG research by incorporating rich visual content from financial documents beyond textual data.
paper_title: FinRAGBench-V: A Benchmark for Multimodal RAG with Visual Citation in the Financial Domain

description: GDI-Bench decouples visual complexity and reasoning complexity in document intelligence evaluation, featuring 2.3k images across 9 scenarios and 19 document-specific tasks. It structures graded tasks that allow performance assessment by difficulty, enabling identification of model weaknesses and optimization guidance.
paper_title: GDI-Bench: A Benchmark for General Document Intelligence with Vision and Reasoning Decoupling

description: The first multilingual text-centric VQA benchmark with high-quality human expert annotations across 9 diverse languages. Addresses visual-textual misalignment problems in translation-based approaches by focusing on text-centric visual environments and providing culturally appropriate content.
paper_title: MTVQA: Benchmarking Multilingual Text-Centric Visual Question Answering

description: MMFCTUB focuses on credit table understanding for financial credit review, encompassing 5 table types with capacity-driven questions and mask-and-recovery evaluation strategy. It evaluates models' cross-table structure perception, domain knowledge utilization, and numerical calculation capabilities using a minimally supervised pipeline that maintains data consistency.
paper_title: MMFCTUB: Multi-Modal Financial Credit Table Understanding Benchmark

description: TableVQA-Bench establishes a visual question answering benchmark specifically for table understanding across multiple domains. It uniquely addresses the gap in existing table datasets by incorporating both images (via stylesheet application or table rendering) and QA pairs generated through LLMs, enabling comprehensive evaluation of MLLMs on table visual comprehension.
paper_title: TableVQA-Bench: A Visual Question Answering Benchmark on Multiple Table Domains

description: Q-Doc introduces a three-tiered evaluation framework for Document Image Quality Assessment (DIQA) capabilities in MLLMs, spanning coarse (quality scoring), middle (distortion identification), and fine (severity assessment) granularity levels. It uniquely probes document-specific quality perception and demonstrates that Chain-of-Thought prompting substantially enhances performance across all evaluation levels.
paper_title: Q-Doc: Benchmarking Document Image Quality Assessment Capabilities in Multi-modal Large Language Models

description: OIDA-QA is a multimodal benchmark for analyzing the Opioid Industry Documents Archive with 400k training documents and 10k testing documents. It uniquely focuses on complex healthcare-related legal and corporate documents, extracting rich multimodal information including textual content, visual elements, and layout structures to generate 360k training QA pairs.
paper_title: OIDA-QA: A Multimodal Benchmark for Analyzing the Opioid Industry Documents Archive

description: MathDoc is the first benchmark for document-level information extraction from authentic high school mathematics exam papers with real-world visual noise. It uniquely evaluates active refusal behavior on unrecognizable samples alongside structured extraction capabilities, addressing a critical gap in current MLLMs' reliability under degraded document conditions.
paper_title: MathDoc: Benchmarking Structured Extraction and Active Refusal on Noisy Mathematics Exam Papers

description: A Vietnamese OCR-VQA dataset consisting of 28,000+ images and 120,000+ question-answer pairs, addressing the limited availability of OCR-VQA resources in low-resource languages. All images contain text and questions about information relevant to the text, specifically designed for Vietnamese language understanding in visual contexts.
paper_title: ViOCRVQA: Novel Benchmark Dataset and Vision Reader for Visual Question Answering by Understanding Vietnamese Text in Images

description: HW-MLVQA is the first benchmark for multilingual handwritten document understanding, featuring 1,600 handwritten pages with 2,400 question-answers. It provides evaluation across three modalities (text, image, integrated) and includes OCR model assessment to simulate real-world contexts without ground truth transcriptions.
paper_title: HW-MLVQA: Elucidating Multilingual Handwritten Document Understanding with a Comprehensive VQA Benchmark

description: This is the first benchmark for Urdu Text VQA, comprising over 1000 natural scene images with fine-grained annotations for arbitrary-shaped texts. It addresses the gap in Urdu scene text understanding with multi-task capabilities for detection, recognition, and VQA in a low-resource language setting.
paper_title: Dataset and Benchmark for Urdu Natural Scenes Text Detection, Recognition and Visual Question Answering

description: ViSignVQA is the first large-scale Vietnamese dataset for signboard-oriented VQA, comprising 10,762 images and 25,573 question-answer pairs. It captures diverse linguistic, cultural, and visual characteristics of Vietnamese signboards including bilingual text, informal phrasing, and visual elements like color and layout.
paper_title: Towards Signboard-Oriented Visual Question Answering: ViSignVQA Dataset, Method and Benchmark

description: UniDoc-Bench is the first large-scale, realistic benchmark for multimodal retrieval-augmented generation built from real-world PDF pages. It supports evaluation of four different paradigms (text-only, image-only, multimodal fusion, and joint retrieval) under a unified protocol.
paper_title: UNIDOC-BENCH: A Unified Benchmark for Document-Centric Multimodal RAG

description: MMDocRAG is a comprehensive benchmark for multimodal document retrieval-augmented generation featuring expert-annotated QA pairs with multi-page, cross-modal evidence chains. It introduces innovative metrics for evaluating multimodal quote selection and enables answers that interleave text with visual elements.
paper_title: Benchmarking Retrieval-Augmented Multimodal Generation for Document Question Answering

num_samples: 15234 document parsing pages, 7496 TEC-VQA QA pairs
description: SEA-Vision jointly evaluates Document Parsing and Text-Centric VQA across 11 Southeast Asian languages. It addresses multilingual document understanding with hierarchical annotations and probes multiple reasoning capabilities including spatial understanding and numerical calculation.
paper_title: SEA-Vision: A Multilingual Benchmark for Comprehensive Document and Scene Text Understanding in Southeast Asia

description: VRD-UQA evaluates VLLM resilience to plausible yet unanswerable questions on multi-page Visually Rich Documents by automatically altering existing VQA datasets with subtle corruptions. It tests robustness across multiple dimensions including NLP entity, document element, and layout corruptions, providing comprehensive evaluation at both page and document levels.
paper_title: Benchmarking Visual LLMs Resilience to Unanswerable Questions on Visually Rich Documents

description: Benchmark for multi-hop reasoning over long scientific papers requiring evidence integration across text, tables, and figures. Provides explicit multi-hop reasoning annotations for step-level evaluation beyond answer accuracy.
paper_title: BRIDGE: Benchmark for multi-hop Reasoning In long multimodal Documents with Grounded Evidence

description: First large-scale public benchmark for Korean visual document retrieval using real-world official publications. Features complex visual elements and dual-task evaluation revealing cross-modal reasoning challenges.
paper_title: SDS KoPub VDR: A Benchmark Dataset for Visual Document Retrieval in Korean Public Documents

description: LED is a benchmark that evaluates structural reasoning in Document Layout Analysis beyond surface-level accuracy. It defines eight standardized error types and provides quantitative rules for realistic error simulation, enabling fine-grained assessment of structural understanding through three evaluation tasks.
paper_title: LED: A Benchmark for Evaluating Layout Error Detection in Document Analysis

description: DuwatBench is a benchmark for Arabic calligraphy recognition featuring 1,272 curated samples across six classical and modern calligraphic styles with sentence-level detection annotations. It addresses the gap in Arabic script processing, especially artistic and stylized calligraphic forms that challenge standard text recognition systems.
paper_title: DuwatBench: Bridging Language and Visual Heritage through an Arabic Calligraphy Benchmark for Multimodal Understanding

description: MMESGBench is the first benchmark for multimodal understanding and complex reasoning across ESG documents, comprising 933 validated QA pairs from 45 ESG documents. It addresses the challenge of document-level reasoning in lengthy, structurally diverse, and multimodal ESG reports through human-AI collaborative construction with expert validation.
paper_title: MMESGBench: Pioneering Multimodal Understanding and Complex Reasoning Benchmark for ESG Tasks

description: First multimodal benchmark for LLM-based peer review automation spanning 17 research domains across 4 major academic disciplines. Evaluates review generation, outcome formulation, alignment with human preferences, and robustness to adversarial manipulation.
paper_title: MMReview: A Multidisciplinary and Multimodal Benchmark for LLM-Based Peer Review Automation

description: First comprehensive benchmark for document packet splitting comprising five datasets of varying complexity. Addresses real-world challenges including out-of-order pages, interleaved documents, and documents lacking clear demarcations.
paper_title: DocSplit: A Comprehensive Benchmark Dataset and Evaluation Approach for Document Packet Recognition and Splitting

description: SciMDR introduces a large-scale training dataset and evaluation benchmark (SciMDR-Eval) for scientific multimodal document reasoning. It uniquely uses a synthesize-and-reground framework to generate faithful QA pairs with explicit reasoning chains across 20K scientific papers, focusing on cross-modal comprehension within full-length scientific workflows.
paper_title: SciMDR: Benchmarking and Advancing Scientific Multimodal Document Reasoning

description: Introduces a novel benchmark to evaluate structural robustness of document layout predictions by defining eight standardized error types and three tasks: error existence detection, error type classification, and element-wise error type classification. Creates LED-Dataset with synthetic structural errors based on empirical distributions from DLA models to test structural understanding capabilities.
paper_title: LED Benchmark: Diagnosing Structural Layout Errors for Document Layout Analysis

description: Introduces a benchmark of 851 samples for evaluating large multimodal models on multimodal super-long document understanding with hundreds of pages. Requires open-ended solutions rather than extractive answers and includes a retrieval-aware tuning framework for efficient document reading.
paper_title: M-Longdoc: A Benchmark For Multimodal Super-Long Document Understanding And A Retrieval-Aware Tuning Framework

description: VLM-SlideEval evaluates VLMs on structured comprehension of presentation slides through element-level extraction, robustness to geometric/stylistic perturbations, and higher-level narrative understanding. It uses standardized ground-truth from PowerPoint XML and live renderings for comprehensive slide analysis.
paper_title: VLM-SlideEval: Evaluating VLMs on Structured Comprehension and Perturbation Sensitivity in PPT

description: DISCO evaluates OCR pipelines and VLMs separately on parsing and question answering across diverse document types including handwritten text, multilingual scripts, medical forms, and infographics. It provides complexity-aware evaluation to guide optimal approach selection based on document characteristics.
paper_title: DISCO: Document Intelligence Suite for COmparative Evaluation

description: First multimodal benchmark for evaluating French financial document understanding. Contains 1,204 expert-validated questions spanning text extraction, table comprehension, chart interpretation, and multi-turn conversational reasoning from real investment prospectuses, KIDs, and PRIIPs.
paper_title: When Tables Go Crazy: Evaluating Multimodal Models on French Financial Documents

description: Novel benchmark specifically designed for multi-tabular visual question answering. Comprises 3,745 complex question-answer pairs that necessitate multi-hop reasoning across several visually rendered table images, addressing gap in evaluating diverse table image parsing and cross-table correlation.
paper_title: MTabVQA: Evaluating Multi-Tabular Reasoning of Language Models in Visual Space

description: Provides fine-grained error detection and analysis for document parsing quality assessment using VLM-as-a-Judge. Identifies 28 predefined error types with hierarchical Chain-of-Checklist reasoning paradigm for comprehensive document parsing evaluation.
paper_title: DOCR-Inspector: Fine-Grained and Automated Evaluation of Document Parsing with VLM

description: Reference-free evaluation framework for flowchart image-to-code generation using OCR-based content coverage and Visual Entailment for hallucination detection. Enables quality monitoring in production settings without ground-truth references.
paper_title: An Online Reference-Free Evaluation Framework for Flowchart Image-to-Code Generation

description: This work creates synthetic and real-world evaluation datasets specifically for vertically written Japanese text, addressing a significant gap in MLLM evaluation. It uniquely focuses on vertical writing support in Japanese documents, which is essential for comprehensive document understanding in Japanese.
paper_title: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models on Vertically Written Japanese Text

description: Comprehensive multimodal benchmark for evaluating LLMs on PowerPoint-related tasks, leveraging 958 PPTX files across four categories: Detection, Understanding, Modification, and Generation. Focuses on layout-centric challenges central to real-world slide creation and editing, revealing gaps between semantic understanding and visual-layout reasoning.
paper_title: PPTBench: Towards Holistic Evaluation of Large Language Models for PowerPoint Layout and Design Understanding

description: A dataset of authentic university-level STEM student handwritten solutions with expert-verified transcriptions and grading reports. It simultaneously evaluates MLLMs' upstream recognition fidelity and downstream auto-grading performance on real handwritten mathematical content.
paper_title: EDU-CIRCUIT-HW: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models on Real-World University-Level STEM Student Handwritten Solutions

description: Designed for comprehensive evaluation of video text understanding with multilingual content. Features hierarchical evaluation framework with video-level, clip-level, and instance-level tasks, plus paired perception reasoning tasks between textual and visual information.
paper_title: VidText: Towards Comprehensive Evaluation for Video Text Understanding

description: Investigates LLM-based OCR capabilities for low-resource scripts including Urdu, Albanian, and Tajik. The study uses a meticulously curated dataset with controlled variations to simulate real-world challenges and highlights the need for annotated datasets and fine-tuned models for underserved languages.
paper_title: Deciphering the Underserved: Benchmarking LLM OCR for Low-Resource Scripts

description: Introduces CC-OCR, a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LMMs in literacy with rich structure and fine-grained visual challenges. The benchmark comprises four OCR-centric tracks across 39 subsets, with 41% sourced from real applications for the first time.
paper_title: CC-OCR: A Comprehensive and Challenging OCR Benchmark for Evaluating Large Multimodal Models in Literacy

description: Introduces PsOCR, a synthetic Pashto OCR dataset for evaluating LMMs on low-resource language OCR. The benchmark covers 1,000 unique font families with variations in colors, image sizes, and layouts, establishing a foundation for research in Pashto and similar scripts.
paper_title: PsOCR: Benchmarking Large Multimodal Models for Optical Character Recognition in Low-resource Pashto Language

description: Introduces IRPAPERS, a visual document benchmark comparing image-based and text-based retrieval and QA systems on scientific papers. The benchmark reveals complementary failures between modalities and shows that multimodal hybrid search outperforms either modality alone.
paper_title: IRPAPERS: A Visual Document Benchmark for Scientific Retrieval and Question Answering

num_samples: 10,000 human-verified QA pairs, 1,500 manually annotated test images
description: Introduces OCRBench v2, a large-scale bilingual text-centric benchmark with 4x more tasks than previous benchmarks and the widest coverage of scenarios (31 diverse scenarios). The benchmark focuses on challenging tasks like text localization, handwritten content extraction, and logical reasoning.
paper_title: OCRBench v2: An Improved Benchmark for Evaluating Large Multimodal Models on Visual Text Localization and Reasoning

description: DocGenome provides comprehensive multi-modal scientific document understanding with 13 layout attributes and LaTeX source codes, featuring logical relationships between entities and diverse document tasks. It addresses multi-page scientific document extraction and within-document format processing like charts and equations.
paper_title: DocGenome: An Open Large-scale Scientific Document Benchmark for Training and Testing Multi-modal Large Language Models

description: Synthetic benchmark for document-to-table extraction using reverse Table2Doc paradigm with multi-agent synthesis workflow. Adopts two-level taxonomy covering 5 major categories and 13 subcategories of Doc2Table capabilities, providing comprehensive testbed for evaluating reasoning, faithfulness, and conflict resolution in structured table extraction.
paper_title: DTBench: A Synthetic Benchmark for Document-to-Table Extraction

description: A benchmark for document reading order recovery that addresses the lack of robust evaluation benchmarks for complex layouts. Specifically designed to test layout ordering methods on both simple and complex document structures, serving as a foundation for RAG and LLM preprocessing evaluation.
paper_title: XY-Cut++: Advanced Layout Ordering via Hierarchical Mask Mechanism on a Novel Benchmark

description: Combines 55K high-fidelity synthetic scanned document parsing data with expert-filtered real-world documents for end-to-end reinforcement learning framework. Focuses on layout-aware document parsing with OCR, table and formula extraction, and reading order detection.
paper_title: Infinity Parser: Layout Aware Reinforcement Learning for Scanned Document Parsing

description: Consists of 324 full-length research articles and 1,688 complex structured JSON files for multimodal information extraction from scientific literature. Focuses on materials science with data extracted from text, tables, and figures, including curve similarity evaluation.
