It enables training Prometheus-Vision, the first open-source VLM evaluator that can understand user-defined score criteria during evaluation.
paper_title: Prometheus-Vision: Vision-Language Model as a Judge for Fine-Grained Evaluation

description: Multimodal RewardBench is an expert-annotated benchmark with 5,211 (prompt, chosen response, rejected response) triplets covering six domains: general correctness, preference, knowledge, reasoning, safety, and visual question-answering. It provides comprehensive evaluation of multimodal reward models for VLMs with challenging testbeds across multiple domains.
paper_title: Multimodal RewardBench: Holistic Evaluation of Reward Models for Vision Language Models

description: VMCBench transforms 20 existing VQA datasets into unified multiple-choice format using AutoConverter framework, totaling 9,018 questions. It enables objective evaluation while reducing costly multiple-choice question creation, providing scalable, consistent, and reproducible VLM evaluation across diverse tasks.
paper_title: Automated Generation of Challenging Multiple-Choice Questions for Vision Language Model Evaluation

description: FIRE-Bench comprehensively evaluates VLMs' feedback-refining capabilities through multi-turn conversations across diverse tasks. It uniquely tests models' ability to spontaneously improve responses based on user feedback, with evaluation settings and automated feedback provision.
paper_title: FIRE: A Dataset for Feedback Integration and Refinement Evaluation of Multimodal Models

description: WV-Bench evaluates VLMs using human preferences from real-world multimodal interactions, curated from 8,000 user submissions in WildVision-Arena. It provides comprehensive analysis of VLM failure cases in authentic user scenarios with high correlation to human preferences.
paper_title: WildVision: Evaluating Vision-Language Models in the Wild with Human Preferences

description: RICE-VL evaluates VLM cultural understanding across 11 ASEAN countries through culturally informed expert annotations. It includes diverse VQA formats and visual grounding tasks, introducing SEA-LAVE metric for assessing textual accuracy, cultural alignment, and country identification.
paper_title: Rice-VL: Evaluating Vision-Language Models for Cultural Understanding Across ASEAN Countries

description: First benchmark specifically designed for Chinese visual contexts to evaluate alignment capabilities of VLMs. Encompasses 1,054 images and 4,978 question-answer pairs across thirteen specific tasks in three categories, including both single-turn and multi-turn dialogue scenarios with real-world Chinese visual contexts.
paper_title: AlignMMBench: Evaluating Chinese Multimodal Alignment in Large Vision-Language Models

description: Introduces MMHE benchmark comprising 18,000 expert human judgments across four multi-modal tasks to assess the generalizability of automatic evaluation metrics in multi-task scenarios. Uniquely focuses on criterion-wise evaluation across different modalities and tasks.
paper_title: Multi-modal, Multi-task, Multi-criteria Automatic Evaluation with Vision Language Models

description: Novel German-language benchmark dataset drawing from real middle school curricula across nine domains including mathematics, history, biology, and religion. Includes over 2,000 open-ended questions grounded in 486 images, ensuring integration of visual interpretation with factual reasoning.
paper_title: VLM@school -- Evaluation of AI image understanding on German middle school knowledge

description: DatBench curates and transforms existing benchmarks to maximize fidelity and discriminability by converting multiple-choice to generative tasks and filtering blindly solvable/mislabeled samples. It provides both DatBench-Full (33 datasets) and a discriminative subset achieving 13x speedup while maintaining discriminative power.
paper_title: DatBench: Discriminative, Faithful, and Efficient VLM Evaluations

description: PISA-Bench derives from expert-created PISA tests and provides a multilingual benchmark covering six languages (English, Spanish, German, Chinese, French, Italian) with human-extracted instructions and questions. It uniquely offers fully parallel corpus for multilingual multimodal reasoning evaluation.
paper_title: PISA-Bench: The PISA Index as a Multilingual and Multimodal Metric for the Evaluation of Vision-Language Models

description: Object-centric evaluation framework for multi-turn instruction-based image editing with fine-grained assessment. Tests instruction following, content consistency, and visual quality across editing turns using object detection and semantic verification.
paper_title: EdiVal-Agent: An Object-Centric Framework for Automated, Fine-Grained Evaluation of Multi-Turn Editing

description: Reference-free evaluation metric for image captioning with structured explanations based on fluency, relevance, and descriptiveness criteria. Provides both scoring and explanation generation with two-stage evaluation template supervision.
paper_title: EXPERT: An Explainable Image Captioning Evaluation Metric with Structured Explanations

description: Refined and efficient benchmark curated through semi-automated pipeline that filters uninformative samples and eliminates answer leakage. Reduces evaluation samples by 76% while providing more effective model capability distinction.
paper_title: LIME: Less Is More for MLLM Evaluation

description: Introduces a new evaluation paradigm using per-sample criteria with potent MLLMs as judges for open-ended and subjective tasks. Curates evaluation samples across six comprehensive cognitive levels to address limitations in existing automatic evaluation methodologies.
paper_title: MLLM-Bench: Evaluating Multimodal LLMs with Per-sample Criteria

description: Evaluates MLLMs on their ability to strictly adhere to complex layered instructions in generating responses with specific requested patterns. Features 400 diverse image-prompt pairs designed to challenge instruction fidelity across various scenarios.
paper_title: MIA-Bench: Towards Better Instruction Following Evaluation of Multimodal LLMs

description: First comprehensive evaluation framework with modular components (Scenario, Instruction, Inferencer, Metric) that enables standardized and holistic assessment of MLLMs. Introduces 6 new evaluation recipes for key capabilities like calibration, instruction following, and robustness.
paper_title: ChEF: A Comprehensive Evaluation Framework for Standardized Assessment of Multimodal Large Language Models

description: GlobalRG evaluates multicultural understanding in vision-language models through two tasks: retrieval across universals (retrieving culturally diverse images for universal concepts from 50 countries) and cultural visual grounding (grounding culture-specific concepts within images from 15 countries). It addresses limitations in cultural diversity coverage across both universal and culture-specific local concepts.
paper_title: From Local Concepts to Universals: Evaluating the Multicultural Understanding of Vision-Language Models

description: MC² constructs controlled evidence-conflict scenarios to systematically evaluate modality preference in MLLMs' decision-making. It tests whether models exhibit tendencies to favor one modality over another when processing multi-modal contexts, providing insights into how MLLMs handle conflicting information across modalities.
paper_title: Evaluating and Steering Modality Preferences in Multimodal Large Language Model

description: BanglaVerse is a culturally grounded benchmark for evaluating multilingual VLMs on Bengali culture across historically linked languages and regional dialects. Built from 1,152 manually curated images across nine domains, it expands into four languages and five Bangla dialects, yielding ~32.3K artifacts to test cultural understanding under linguistic variation.
paper_title: Many Dialects, Many Languages, One Cultural Lens: Evaluating Multilingual VLMs for Bengali Culture Understanding Across Historically Linked Languages and Regional Dialects

description: FLIP provides a comprehensive evaluation framework for federated prompt learning algorithms, assessing 8 state-of-the-art methods across 4 federated learning protocols and 12 open datasets with 6 distinct evaluation scenarios. It specifically evaluates vision-language models in federated settings considering data scarcity, unseen classes, and cross-domain distributional shifts.
paper_title: FLIP: Towards Comprehensive and Reliable Evaluation of Federated Prompt Learning

description: Presents a unified benchmark to evaluate MLLMs across six key perception abilities (counting, OCR, structural data understanding) focusing on both accuracy and stability. Addresses evaluation variance issues by providing comprehensive assessment across diverse question types, domains, and metrics.
paper_title: Evaluating and Advancing Multimodal Large Language Models in Perception Ability Lens

description: Presents the first comprehensive multi-modal benchmark to evaluate factuality ability of MLLMs in answering natural language questions. Categorizes VQA items into 9 different tasks around objective events and common knowledge with rigorous quality control for high-quality, concise answers.
paper_title: SimpleVQA: Multimodal Factuality Evaluation for Multimodal Large Language Models

description: AI GameStore introduces a scalable platform for evaluating machine general intelligence through human games, representing the "Multiverse of Human Games." It uniquely uses LLMs with humans-in-the-loop to synthesize games from popular gaming platforms, testing world-model learning, memory, and planning capabilities.
paper_title: AI Gamestore: Scalable, Open-Ended Evaluation of Machine General Intelligence with Human Games

description: UPME introduces an unsupervised peer review framework for MLLM evaluation that uses only image data, allowing models to automatically generate questions and conduct peer reviews. It uniquely addresses human workload and bias issues through automated evaluation with a vision-language scoring system.
paper_title: UPME: An Unsupervised Peer Review Framework for Multimodal Large Language Model Evaluation

description: MDK12-Bench is a large-scale multidisciplinary benchmark built from real-world K-12 exams spanning six disciplines with 6,225 knowledge points in a six-layer taxonomy. It uniquely provides dynamic evaluation framework with unfamiliar shifts and comprehensive evaluation across difficulty, temporal, contextual, and knowledge-driven dimensions.
paper_title: MDK12-Bench: A Comprehensive Evaluation of Multimodal Large Language Models on Multidisciplinary Exams

description: MM-BigBench provides comprehensive assessment framework with diverse metrics for evaluating MLLMs on multimodal content comprehension tasks, introducing adaptability metrics between models and instructions.
paper_title: MM-BigBench: Evaluating Multimodal Models on Multimodal Content Comprehension Tasks

description: Introduces a novel binary relevancy dataset covering various tasks for image-text relevancy evaluation. Presents LLaVA-RE framework using MLLMs for binary relevancy evaluation with detailed task instructions and multimodal in-context samples to handle diverse text formats and definition variations.
paper_title: LLaVA-RE: Binary Image-Text Relevancy Evaluation with Multimodal Large Language Model

description: Comprehensive benchmark for evaluating schema-grounded information extraction and reasoning over visual inputs. Built from over 6.5K diverse JSON schemas and 1.8K curated image-schema pairs covering four visual domains: UI screens, natural images, documents, and charts with human-verified quality.
paper_title: SO-Bench: A Structural Output Evaluation of Multimodal LLMs

description: First benchmark for agentic multimodal retrieval-augmented generation with long, step-wise annotated reasoning chains spanning five reasoning structures. Features HAVE verification process and introduces process-level metrics for reasoning quality, stepwise retrieval and planning accuracy.
paper_title: MC-Search: Evaluating and Enhancing Multimodal Agentic Search with Structured Long Reasoning Chains

description: Comprehensive multimodal knowledge editing benchmark comprising eight diverse tasks from multiple datasets. Introduces Knowledge Generalization Index (KGI) and Knowledge Preservation Index (KPI) to evaluate editing effects on in-domain samples without AI-synthetic data.
paper_title: ComprehendEdit: A Comprehensive Dataset and Evaluation Framework for Multimodal Knowledge Editing

description: Comprehensive evaluation framework for multimodal reasoning tasks with vision-text contexts. Introduces innovative metrics including Best Performance, Mean Relative Gain, Stability, and Adaptability across 16 multimodal datasets encompassing 6 distinct tasks using 10 different instructions.
paper_title: MM-InstructEval: Zero-Shot Evaluation of (Multimodal) Large Language Models on Multimodal Reasoning Tasks

description: Evaluates multimodal models' ability to match objects with their colors using a novel Winoground-style schema where captions have identical words but color words are rearranged to modify different objects. Tests fine-grained color-object associations through both image-text matching and visual language understanding.
paper_title: ColorSwap: A Color and Word Order Dataset for Multimodal Evaluation

description: Multi-domain benchmark for zero-shot semantic segmentation covering 22 datasets across diverse domains like medicine, engineering, earth monitoring, biology, and agriculture. Provides holistic analysis of performance across domain-specific datasets using a developed taxonomy.
paper_title: What a MESS: Multi-Domain Evaluation of Zero-Shot Semantic Segmentation

description: A live leaderboard that ranks models based on human feedback from real-world AI applications rather than static datasets. Integrates pairwise model comparisons into natural user interactions with novel mechanisms like Placement Matches and Proximity Sampling for robust ranking.
paper_title: Inclusion Arena: An Open Platform for Evaluating Large Foundation Models with Real-World Apps

description: A large-scale multimodal evaluation dataset combining human and GPT evaluation data across both image-to-text and text-to-image generation tasks. Enables development of multimodal evaluation models that can assess bidirectional generation between image and text.
paper_title: Minos: A Multimodal Evaluation Model for Bidirectional Generation Between Image and Text

description: A comprehensive benchmark spanning six core image generation/editing tasks and six topical domains with 20K fine-grained human annotations. Features an explainable evaluation schema with localized object-level and segment-level error tagging, going beyond opaque automated metrics.
paper_title: ImagenWorld: Stress-Testing Image Generation Models with Explainable Human Evaluation on Open-ended Real-World Tasks

description: A benchmark for assessing referenceless metrics for image description generation with human ratings across quality dimensions and ten robustness checks. Uniquely presents images and descriptions in context, reflecting the importance of context dependence for description quality evaluation.
paper_title: ContextRef: Evaluating Referenceless Metrics For Image Description Generation

description: A dynamic multimodal evaluation protocol that generates new visual question-answering samples by modifying both images and language while maintaining consistency. Addresses data contamination and provides flexible complexity variants of existing benchmarks that co-evolve with model capabilities.
paper_title: Dynamic Multimodal Evaluation with Flexible Complexity by Vision-Language Bootstrapping

description: A fine-grained, multi-domain meta-evaluation benchmark designed to exhibit diverse score distributions and expose visual biases in LVLM judges. Tests whether adversarial visual manipulations can systematically fool LVLM-based evaluation systems into inflating scores unfairly.
paper_title: Fooling the LVLM Judges: Visual Biases in LVLM-Based Evaluation

description: Introduces Markup-QA annotation technique where QAs are enclosed within markups for autonomous driving datasets. Enables simultaneous evaluation of sentence generation and VQA capabilities on driving scenes from the NuScenes dataset.
paper_title: NuScenes-MQA: Integrated Evaluation of Captions and QA for Autonomous Driving Datasets using Markup Annotations

description: Purpose-built for evaluating video foundation models across four core abilities: video understanding, generation, editing, and video reconstruction. Features high-quality diverse multi-shot videos with detailed captions and unified agentic evaluation system.
paper_title: UniVBench: Towards Unified Evaluation for Video Foundation Models

description: Large-scale benchmark for continuous multi-modal 3D human action understanding with 1076 long video sequences in 51 action categories. Provides multi-modality data sources including RGB, depth, infrared, and skeleton with comprehensive action annotations.
paper_title: PKU-MMD: A Large Scale Benchmark for Continuous Multi-Modal Human Action Understanding

description: Introduces PM4Bench, the first Multilingual Multi-Modal Multi-task Benchmark constructed on strictly parallel corpus across 10 languages. The benchmark enables fair comparison of LVLM capabilities across languages and introduces a vision setting where textual queries are visually fused into images.
paper_title: PM4Bench: Benchmarking Large Vision-Language Models with Parallel Multilingual Multi-Modal Multi-task Corpus

description: Multi-vision sensor perception and reasoning benchmark that evaluates LVLMs on images from thermal, depth, and medical X-ray sensors beyond RGB. Tests physical sensor knowledge proficiency and multi-vision sensory reasoning considering physical environment characteristics.
paper_title: SPARK: Multi-Vision Sensor Perception and Reasoning Benchmark for Large-scale Vision-Language Models

description: Large-scale dataset for affective visual dialog with 50K 10-turn visually grounded dialogs, emotion attributions, and textual emotion explanations. Tests emotional reasoning abilities through dialog-based QA, emotion prediction, and affective explanation generation.
paper_title: Affective Visual Dialog: A Large-Scale Benchmark for Emotional Reasoning Based on Visually Grounded Conversations

description: Unified evaluation benchmark for visually-grounded dialogue defining five core multi-modal dialogue tasks across six datasets. Introduces VDscore metric based on Analytic Hierarchy Process for comprehensive assessment across all tasks.
paper_title: VDialogUE: A Unified Evaluation Benchmark for Visually-grounded Dialogue

description: ReForm-Eval reformulates existing benchmarks into unified LVLM-compatible formats to comprehensively evaluate large vision-language models. It addresses the challenge of assessing free-form text outputs by systematically converting task-oriented benchmarks into formats suitable for automatic evaluation.
paper_title: ReForm-Eval: Evaluating Large Vision Language Models via Unified Re-Formulation of Task-Oriented Benchmarks

description: G-VUE is a comprehensive benchmark covering the full spectrum of visual cognitive abilities through four functional domains: Perceive, Ground, Reason, and Act. It encompasses 11 carefully curated tasks from 3D reconstruction to visual reasoning and manipulation, providing a holistic evaluation standard for general-purpose visual systems.
paper_title: Perceive, Ground, Reason, and Act: A Benchmark for General-purpose Visual Representation

description: A framework to study the broad impact of compression (KV cache and weight compression) on Large Vision-Language Models across ten different multi-modal datasets. It evaluates both general performance and ethically critical metrics including toxicity, stereotypes and bias using both real-world and synthetic datasets.
paper_title: LVLM-Compress-Bench: Benchmarking the Broader Impact of Large Vision-Language Model Compression

description: OmniPlay tests fusion and reasoning capabilities of agentic models across the full sensory spectrum through five game environments that create scenarios of modality synergy and conflict. It forces genuine cross-modal reasoning and reveals fragility in fusion mechanisms, uncovering a "less is more" paradox in sensory information processing.
paper_title: OmniPlay: Benchmarking Omni-Modal Models on Omni-Modal Game Playing

description: A benchmark for General Tool Agents featuring real user queries with implicit tool-use requirements, real deployed tools across perception/operation/logic/creativity categories, and real multimodal inputs including images, web screenshots, tables, and code snippets. Designed to evaluate agents' actual task execution performance in realistic scenarios.
paper_title: GTA: A Benchmark for General Tool Agents

description: LM Fight Arena evaluates LMMs by pitting them against each other in Mortal Kombat II, requiring rapid visual understanding and tactical sequential decision-making. It provides fully automated, reproducible assessment of strategic reasoning capabilities in dynamic, adversarial environments.
paper_title: LM Fight Arena: Benchmarking Large Multimodal Models via Game Competition

description: A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating Vision-Language Reward Models across three major categories: process understanding, outcome judgment, and critique generation. Covers 12 tasks spanning mathematical reasoning, hallucination understanding, and multi-image understanding, addressing the gap in all-round evaluation of VLRMs.
paper_title: VLRMBench: A Comprehensive and Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Reward Models

description: A benchmark dataset for multitask learning with multiple languages (three languages) and modalities, designed to test systems on data encountered on the web and digital archives. Includes semantic data and geo-representative subsets weighted for EU countries, targeting digital humanities applications.
paper_title: MLM: A Benchmark Dataset for Multitask Learning with Multiple Languages and Modalities

description: VisText-Mosquito is a multimodal dataset that integrates visual and textual data for automated mosquito breeding site analysis. It includes 1,828 annotated images for object detection, 142 images for water surface segmentation, and natural language reasoning texts linked to each image.
paper_title: VisText-Mosquito: A Unified Multimodal Benchmark Dataset for Visual Detection, Segmentation, and Textual Reasoning on Mosquito Breeding Sites

description: FishDet-M is the largest unified benchmark for fish detection, comprising 13 publicly available datasets spanning diverse aquatic environments with COCO-style annotations. It enables consistent cross-domain evaluation and includes a CLIP-based model selection framework for adaptive deployment.
paper_title: FishDet-M: A Unified Large-Scale Benchmark for Robust Fish Detection and CLIP-Guided Model Selection in Diverse Aquatic Visual Domains

description: OVAD introduces the first open-vocabulary attribute detection benchmark with 117 attribute classes on 80 MS COCO object classes. It provides both positive and negative annotations enabling open-vocabulary evaluation with 1.4 million total annotations.
paper_title: Open-vocabulary Attribute Detection

description: MMBench is a bilingual benchmark for assessing multi-modal capabilities of VLMs with comprehensive evaluation pipeline. It introduces CircularEval strategy and incorporates LLMs to convert free-form predictions into pre-defined choices for accurate evaluation.
paper_title: MMBench: Is Your Multi-modal Model an All-around Player?

description: UniBench provides a unified implementation of 50+ VLM benchmarks spanning comprehensive capabilities from object recognition to spatial awareness and counting. It facilitates systematic evaluation by categorizing capabilities and evaluating nearly 60 vision-language models.
paper_title: UniBench: Visual Reasoning Requires Rethinking Vision-Language Beyond Scaling

description: This benchmark introduces evaluation with both hard negatives and hard positives (112,382 total) to test vision-language model compositionality more rigorously. It reveals that existing improvements have been overstated by not testing invariance to hard positives.
paper_title: The Hard Positive Truth about Vision-Language Compositionality

description: This work creates a culture-centric evaluation benchmark by filtering VizWiz dataset with images taken by people who are blind, focusing on cultural considerations for visual assistants. It addresses the gap in evaluating VLMs for culturally diverse settings and accessibility use cases.
paper_title: Vision-Language Models under Cultural and Inclusive Considerations

description: Massive Multimodal Embedding Benchmark covering 4 meta-tasks (classification, VQA, multimodal retrieval, visual grounding) with 36 datasets including 20 training and 16 evaluation datasets. Designed to evaluate universal multimodal embedding models across both in-distribution and out-of-distribution tasks.
paper_title: VLM2Vec: Training Vision-Language Models for Massive Multimodal Embedding Tasks

description: The first factuality-based visual question-answering benchmark in Chinese, assessing visual factuality of LVLMs across 8 major topics and 56 subtopics. Focuses on decoupling visual factuality into object recognition and knowledge discovery components.
paper_title: "See the World, Discover Knowledge": A Chinese Factuality Evaluation for Large Vision Language Models

description: A large-scale Arabic multimodal dataset and benchmark for cultural understanding, comprising over 309K examples across ten culturally significant domains covering all Arab countries. Includes PEARL, PEARL-LITE, and PEARL-X evaluation benchmarks for assessing cultural variations.
paper_title: Pearl: A Multimodal Culturally-Aware Arabic Instruction Dataset

description: A benchmark designed to evaluate Vision-Language Models on visual understanding across four Arabic-speaking countries (Jordan, Emirates, Egypt, Morocco). Features culturally rich and regionally diverse content for image captioning and visual question answering tasks.
paper_title: JEEM: Vision-Language Understanding in Four Arabic Dialects

description: Introduces a new testing paradigm that consolidates individual evaluation datasets into a unified, ever-expanding sample pool for open-ended capability assessment. Enables custom benchmark generation and addresses heterogeneity and incompleteness challenges through novel aggregation algorithms.
paper_title: ONEBench to Test Them All: Sample-Level Benchmarking Over Open-Ended Capabilities

description: Comprises comprehensive in-domain evaluation benchmarks inspired by infant learning, designed for data-efficient VLM pretraining. Features developmentally aligned evaluation tools and synthetic training dataset created via child-directed transformations of existing datasets.
paper_title: BabyVLM: Data-Efficient Pretraining of VLMs Inspired by Infant Learning

description: LoCoVQA is a dynamic benchmark generator for evaluating long-context extractive reasoning in vision language models. It augments test examples for mathematical reasoning, VQA, and character recognition with increasingly long visual contexts composed of both in-distribution and out-of-distribution distractor images, testing VLMs' ability to ignore irrelevant information.
paper_title: Losing Visual Needles in Image Haystacks: Vision Language Models are Easily Distracted in Short and Long Contexts

description: MEGA-Bench scales multimodal evaluation to over 500 real-world tasks with 8,000+ samples from 16 expert annotators. Unlike existing benchmarks that unify problems into standard multi-choice questions, it embraces diverse output formats (numbers, phrases, code, LaTeX, coordinates, JSON) with over 40 evaluation metrics.
paper_title: MEGA-Bench: Scaling Multimodal Evaluation to over 500 Real-World Tasks

description: LVLM-Playground is a game-based evaluation framework designed to provide comprehensive assessment of LVLMs' cognitive and reasoning skills in structured environments. It evaluates four core tasks: Perceiving, Question Answering, Rule Following, and End-to-End Playing, addressing limitations of existing benchmarks such as inadequate assessment of detailed visual perception, data contamination, and lack of focus on multi-turn reasoning.
paper_title: Are Large Vision Language Models Good Game Players?

description: Introduces five Korean evaluation datasets (four closed-set and one open-set) for bilingual Korean-English vision-language understanding. This addresses the gap in non-English vision-language model evaluation benchmarks.
paper_title: VARCO-VISION: Expanding Frontiers in Korean Vision-Language Models

description: VQA360 is a novel dataset derived from established VQA benchmarks, annotated with task types, application domains, and knowledge types for comprehensive VLM evaluation. It provides a standardized framework for evaluating VLMs across different practical application requirements.
paper_title: Guiding Vision-Language Model Selection for Visual Question-Answering Across Tasks, Domains, and Knowledge Types

description: TTA-VLM is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating test-time adaptation methods on VLMs, implementing 15 TTA methods across 15 datasets. It extends beyond accuracy to include robustness, calibration, out-of-distribution detection, and stability metrics, providing holistic assessment of TTA methods.
paper_title: The Illusion of Progress? A Critical Look at Test-Time Adaptation for Vision-Language Models

description: Designs and introduces a benchmark to evaluate the effectiveness of multilingual and multimodal embedding models, specifically targeting Large Vision-Language Models adapted for multilingual contexts. Tests models' ability to extract meaningful embeddings across different languages and modalities.
paper_title: xVLM2Vec: Adapting LVLM-based embedding models to multilinguality using Self-Knowledge Distillation

description: Presents a diagnostic benchmark featuring dialogues spanning 8 domains and 40 sub-topics for evaluating multimodal multi-turn contextual reasoning in vision-language models. Tests models' ability to maintain context and perform reasoning across multiple dialogue turns with multiple images.
paper_title: MMCR: Advancing Visual Language Model in Multimodal Multi-Turn Contextual Reasoning

description: Introduces ISG-Bench with 1,150 samples across 8 categories and 21 subcategories for evaluating interleaved text-and-image generation using scene graph structure. The benchmark includes complex language-vision dependencies and golden answers, particularly challenging models on vision-centric tasks such as style transfer.
paper_title: Interleaved Scene Graphs for Interleaved Text-and-Image Generation Assessment

description: BharatBench is a comprehensive framework for evaluating VLMs across 10 prominent Indian languages, addressing the gap in multilingual multimodal evaluation. It specifically targets rich linguistic diversity and visual reasoning capabilities across medium and low-resource languages.
paper_title: Chitrarth: Bridging Vision and Language for a Billion People

description: CoBSAT is the first Text-to-Image In-Context Learning benchmark dataset, encompassing ten tasks that evaluate MLLMs' ability to generate images based on textual context and examples. It uniquely focuses on T2I-ICL rather than the commonly studied image-to-text ICL.
paper_title: Can MLLMs Perform Text-to-Image In-Context Learning?

description: A benchmark generation engine that produces tailored benchmarks based on user needs. Contains extensive visual assets (images, videos, 3D objects) and can programmatically generate vast numbers of task instances focusing on MLM perceptual capabilities across multiple modalities.
paper_title: Task Me Anything

description: MMEB-V2 extends the original MMEB benchmark with five new task types specifically for multimodal embeddings: visual document retrieval, video retrieval, temporal grounding, video classification, and video question answering. This comprehensive benchmark spans text, image, video, and visual document inputs to evaluate unified multimodal embedding models across diverse visual forms.
paper_title: VLM2Vec-V2: Advancing Multimodal Embedding for Videos, Images, and Visual Documents

description: OBELICS provides a large-scale web dataset of interleaved image-text documents with 141 million web pages, 353 million images, and 115 billion text tokens. This enables training of multimodal models on naturally occurring web documents rather than paired image-text data.
paper_title: OBELICS: An Open Web-Scale Filtered Dataset of Interleaved Image-Text Documents

description: Introduces the first multilingual multimodal benchmark for evaluating vision-language reasoning capabilities across 10 major languages including underrepresented ones like Hindi, Arabic, Bengali, and Urdu. This benchmark assesses VLM performance on diverse linguistic populations.
paper_title: PALO: A Polyglot Large Multimodal Model for 5B People

description: MMMB is a new benchmark comprising 6 languages, 15 categories, and 12,000 questions to assess multilingual capabilities of VLMs. It addresses the gap in evaluating multimodal models across diverse languages beyond English.
paper_title: Parrot: Multilingual Visual Instruction Tuning

description: MM-MT-Bench is an open-source benchmark for evaluating vision-language models in practical scenarios. It provides standardized evaluation protocols and detailed analysis for multimodal LLMs in real-world applications.
paper_title: Pixtral 12B

description: Introduces a novel multimodal benchmark dataset assembled by integrating WebGPT, ScienceQA, AVSD, and ScanQA datasets to evaluate curiosity-driven reasoning approaches. Tests models' ability to dynamically generate questions and selectively activate relevant modalities.
paper_title: Chain of Questions: Guiding Multimodal Curiosity in Language Models

description: CulturalGround comprises 22 million high-quality, culturally-rich VQA pairs spanning 42 countries and 39 languages. It addresses cultural gaps in MLLMs by grounding models in diverse cultural knowledge using entities from Wikidata.
paper_title: Grounding Multilingual Multimodal LLMs With Cultural Knowledge

description: A large-scale multimodal benchmark covering 19,682 cultural concepts across 188 countries/regions and 15 cultural concepts with 3 question types, designed to characterize and improve VLMs' multicultural understanding capabilities. Addresses cultural biases in predominantly Western-centric training data by evaluating understanding of symbols, gestures, and artifacts from diverse cultures.
paper_title: CultureVLM: Characterizing and Improving Cultural Understanding of Vision-Language Models for over 100 Countries

description: Two large-scale Japanese Vision-and-Language datasets (DEJIMA-Cap for image captioning and DEJIMA-VQA for visual question answering) with 3.88M image-text pairs each. The datasets address scarcity of high-quality Japanese V&L resources with culturally grounded content.
paper_title: DEJIMA: A Novel Large-scale Japanese Dataset for Image Captioning and Visual Question Answering

description: The first deliberately designed benchmark to rigorously evaluate MLLM performance on true high-resolution 4K and 8K images. Addresses the gap where existing MLLM benchmarks only support up to 2K resolution while SOTA models claim 4K capability, revealing significant performance gaps between human and model performance on HR images.
paper_title: Divide, Conquer and Combine: A Training-Free Framework for High-Resolution Image Perception in Multimodal Large Language Models

description: MMCOMPOSITION provides comprehensive evaluation of VLMs' compositionality beyond basic objects, relations, and attributes. It tests deeper reasoning about object interactions, counting, and complex compositions with human annotations for accurate assessment.
paper_title: MMCOMPOSITION: Revisiting the Compositionality of Pre-trained Vision-Language Models

description: A benchmark for analyzing consistency in Vision-Language Models across three tasks: Question Rephrasing, Image Restyling, and Context Reasoning. Evaluates whether VLMs produce semantically similar responses to semantically similar queries, focusing on consistency as a prerequisite for robustness.
paper_title: MM-R$^3$: On (In-)Consistency of Vision-Language Models (VLMs)

description: Novel benchmark to examine LVLMs on instance IDs memory and recognition across four dimensions: matching, location, question-answering, and captioning. Focuses on character identity association across different visual scenarios for movie understanding.
paper_title: IDA-VLM: Towards Movie Understanding via ID-Aware Large Vision-Language Model

description: A new cross-domain few-shot benchmark is established to comprehensively evaluate methods on imaging domains distinct from natural images. It helps assess VLM performance when confronted with cross-domain tasks where imaging domains differ from natural images.
paper_title: Cross-Domain Few-Shot Learning via Multi-View Collaborative Optimization with Vision-Language Models

description: A new benchmark for Incremental Test Time Adaptation (ITTA) of Vision Language Models, integrating single image TTA methods with active labeling techniques. It tackles scenarios where unseen classes and unseen domains continuously appear during testing, allowing models to adapt to both covariate and label shifts.
paper_title: Segmentation Assisted Incremental Test Time Adaptation in an Open World

description: A systematic benchmark to evaluate robustness to different prompt templates for VLMs, including a dataset with hundreds of carefully designed prompt templates divided into six types. Tests model sensitivity to various prompt phrasings and enables evaluation of prompt template robustness.
paper_title: Modeling Variants of Prompts for Vision-Language Models

description: New benchmarks for evaluating In-Context Learning (ICL) capabilities in Vision-Language Models. Tests models' ability to reason about downstream tasks with few-shot demonstrations embedded in the prompt, specifically designed to assess VLM ICL performance.
paper_title: Towards Multimodal In-Context Learning for Vision & Language Models

description: A novel benchmark evaluating VLMs on modeling human creativity using data from 'Guess the Build' Minecraft minigame. Tests sensemaking creativity with 1500 real gameplay images across static/dynamic settings and varying natural language hints.
paper_title: GuessBench: Sensemaking Multimodal Creativity in the Wild

description: Vision-Flan is the most diverse publicly available visual instruction tuning dataset comprising 187 diverse tasks and 1,664,261 instances from academic datasets. Each task includes expert-written instructions, addressing task diversity limitations in visual instruction tuning.
paper_title: Vision-Flan: Scaling Human-Labeled Tasks in Visual Instruction Tuning

description: PerVA is a new dataset for concept identification highlighting challenges in visual ambiguity in personalization tasks. It focuses on evaluating VLMs' ability to understand user-specific concepts when there are visually similar but semantically different objects.
paper_title: Training-Free Personalization via Retrieval and Reasoning on Fingerprints

description: SocialGesture is the first large-scale dataset specifically designed for multi-person gesture analysis in social contexts. It supports video-based recognition and temporal localization tasks, and introduces a novel VQA task to benchmark VLMs' performance on social gesture understanding.
paper_title: SocialGesture: Delving into Multi-person Gesture Understanding

description: UnifyBench is the first benchmark for assessing unified VLM personalization across concept understanding, concept generation, and attribute-reasoning generation. It specifically evaluates personalized attribute-reasoning generation where models must generate complex prompts involving personalized concepts and their attributes.
paper_title: UniCTokens: Boosting Personalized Understanding and Generation via Unified Concept Tokens

description: A cross-cultural Theory of Mind benchmark with visually situated probes across diverse cultural contexts, rituals, and social norms. Tests VLMs' ability to attribute beliefs and intents to others across different cultures with six ToM tasks and four complexity levels.
paper_title: Are Vision Language Models Cross-Cultural Theory of Mind Reasoners?

description: First extensive benchmark for evaluating VLM personalization across 111 personalizable concepts in humans, animals, objects, and characters. Tests models' ability to adapt to individual user preferences and maintain consistency across multi-turn dialogues.
paper_title: MMPB: It's Time for Multi-Modal Personalization

description: The first dataset designed to evaluate Persian VLMs across scientific, reasoning, and human-level understanding tasks. Includes bilingual structure with Persian and English questions covering diverse educational levels and cultural nuances.
paper_title: MEENA (PersianMMMU): Multimodal-Multilingual Educational Exams for N-level Assessment

description: SynthVLM-100K provides 100K curated and synthesized image-caption pairs created by generating images from high-quality captions using diffusion models. This approach ensures precise alignment between images and text, creating higher quality training data than traditional web-scraped datasets.
paper_title: SynthVLM: Towards High-Quality and Efficient Synthesis of Image-Caption Datasets for Vision-Language Models

description: Multilingual datasets covering nine vision-language tasks in English, Japanese, Swahili, and Urdu with rationales for VLM reasoning analysis. Introduces a new 'unrelatedness' task and provides systematic evaluation across multiple languages.
paper_title: Constructing Multilingual Visual-Text Datasets Revealing Visual Multilingual Ability of Vision Language Models

description: A large-scale Bangla VQA dataset with regionally relevant and locally sourced content. Addresses the lack of proper VQA benchmarks for Bangla language with diverse and culturally appropriate visual contexts.
paper_title: ChitroJera: A Regionally Relevant Visual Question Answering Dataset for Bangla

description: The first Person-Centered Therapy-grounded multimodal dataset with multidimensional annotations for verbal-visual incongruence detection. Focuses on identifying emotional misalignment in client-counselor interactions for genuine empathic AI capabilities.
paper_title: E-THER: A Multimodal Dataset for Empathic AI -- Towards Emotional Mismatch Awareness

description: Extends COCO dataset with high-level human-annotated captions along three axes: scenes, actions, and rationales. Focuses on controlled experiments with high-level captions that draw on personal experience and commonsense assumptions, moving beyond object-centric descriptions.
paper_title: HL Dataset: Visually-grounded Description of Scenes, Actions and Rationales

description: A novel VQA dataset derived from The Simpsons TV show designed for inquiry-based learning. Addresses traditional VQA, irrelevant question identification, and answer evaluation tasks specifically on cartoon images, filling a gap in model performance evaluation on animated content.
paper_title: SimpsonsVQA: Enhancing Inquiry-Based Learning with a Tailored Dataset

description: A manually curated, fine-grained image-text dataset capturing intricate features of food cultures across various regions in China. Comprises three multiple-choice QA tasks requiring understanding of regional food diversity and cultural implications through multi-image, single-image, and text-only scenarios.
paper_title: FoodieQA: A Multimodal Dataset for Fine-Grained Understanding of Chinese Food Culture

description: VLFeedback is the first large-scale vision-language feedback dataset comprising multi-modal instructions and comprehensive rationales generated by AI models without human annotations. It enables training VLMs through AI feedback for alignment purposes.
paper_title: VLFeedback: A Large-Scale AI Feedback Dataset for Large Vision-Language Models Alignment

description: CUHK-X is a large-scale multimodal dataset for human action recognition, understanding, and reasoning covering 40 actions across diverse environments. It uses prompt-based scene creation for logically connected activity sequences and includes three benchmarks with six evaluation tasks.
paper_title: A Large-Scale Multimodal Dataset and Benchmarks for Human Activity Scene Understanding and Reasoning

description: Designed for visual intent understanding in multimodal assistants with questions and intents from human annotators viewing images. Tests intent classification combining vision and language features for 14 different intent categories.
paper_title: MMIU: Dataset for Visual Intent Understanding in Multimodal Assistants

description: MMAT-1M is the first million-scale multimodal agent tuning dataset designed to support Chain-of-Thought reasoning, reflection, and dynamic tool usage in multimodal contexts. It introduces a novel four-stage data engine that generates rationales, integrates API calls and RAG information, and provides both multi-turn and compressed single-turn formats.
paper_title: MMAT-1M: A Large Reasoning Dataset for Multimodal Agent Tuning

description: PHMR introduces the novel task of Personality-aware Human-centric Multimodal Reasoning, which uniquely integrates personality traits into behavioral prediction. The dataset spans 225 characters from TV shows and demonstrates how personality factors enhance multimodal reasoning performance for forecasting individual future behaviors.
paper_title: Personality-aware Human-centric Multimodal Reasoning: A New Task, Dataset and Baselines

description: MIntRec provides the first multimodal intent recognition dataset with coarse-grained and fine-grained intent taxonomies, incorporating text, video, and audio modalities. It uniquely includes annotated speaker bounding boxes and demonstrates substantial improvements from non-verbal modalities over text-only approaches.
paper_title: MIntRec: A New Dataset for Multimodal Intent Recognition

description: DataComp is a testbed for dataset experiments centered around 12.8 billion image-text pairs from Common Crawl, enabling participants to design filtering techniques and evaluate on 38 downstream test sets. It spans multiple compute scales across four orders of magnitude and establishes a standardized workflow for multimodal dataset curation and evaluation.
paper_title: DataComp: In search of the next generation of multimodal datasets

description: COCO-Urdu is the largest publicly available Urdu image-caption dataset, derived from MS COCO with quality-validated translations. It addresses the critical under-representation of Urdu in multimodal vision-language research and includes a hybrid multimodal quality estimation framework.
paper_title: COCO-Urdu: A Large-Scale Urdu Image-Caption Dataset with Multimodal Quality Estimation

description: This work introduces a large-scale Multimodal Insect Dataset with Visual Insect Instruction Data and Visual Insect Question Answering benchmarks. It focuses on insect-domain knowledge understanding and includes an Insect Foundation Model with micro-feature learning for capturing subtle differences among insect images.
paper_title: Insect-Foundation: A Foundation Model and Large Multimodal Dataset for Vision-Language Insect Understanding

description: MIntRec2.0 provides a large-scale benchmark for multimodal intent recognition in multi-party conversations with 30 fine-grained intent classes. It includes both in-scope and out-of-scope samples in multi-turn contexts, addressing real-world conversational scenarios with comprehensive speaker information.
paper_title: MIntRec2.0: A Large-scale Benchmark Dataset for Multimodal Intent Recognition and Out-of-scope Detection in Conversations

description: Bangla-Bayanno provides the most comprehensive open-source, high-quality VQA benchmark in Bangla (Bengali), addressing the low-resource language gap in multimodal AI. It includes LLM-assisted translation refinement and classifies questions into nominal, quantitative, and polar answer types.
paper_title: Bangla-Bayanno: A 52K-Pair Bengali Visual Question Answering Dataset with LLM-Assisted Translation Refinement

num_samples: 52K unique cryptic groups spanning 67K species, represented in 166 million images
description: CrypticBio is the largest publicly available multimodal dataset of visually confusing species, specifically addressing the challenge of identifying cryptic species that are nearly indistinguishable visually. It integrates geographical and temporal data as complementary cues and includes rich research-grade annotations.
paper_title: CrypticBio: A Large Multimodal Dataset for Visually Confusing Biodiversity

description: FungiTastic provides a challenging multimodal benchmark for fungal species classification with expert-labeled data spanning 20 years, including DNA-sequenced ground truth for unprecedented label reliability. It supports multiple learning scenarios including few-shot learning and domain shift with meteorological, climatic, and satellite data.
paper_title: FungiTastic: A multi-modal dataset and benchmark for image categorization

description: Large-scale automatically constructed dataset for Vietnamese Visual Question Answering, addressing low-resource multimodal learning. Complements existing Vietnamese VQA datasets (ViVQA, OpenViVQA, ViTextVQA) for Vietnamese language understanding.
paper_title: AutoViVQA: A Large-Scale Automatically Constructed Dataset for Vietnamese Visual Question Answering

description: Dataset for multimodal comprehension of cooking recipes with 20K instructional recipes across multiple modalities. Features automatically generated QA pairs requiring joint understanding of images and text, capturing temporal flow and procedural knowledge.
paper_title: RecipeQA: A Challenge Dataset for Multimodal Comprehension of Cooking Recipes

task_types: Visual Question Answering with Spatial Awareness
Visual Reasoning in Creative Advertisement Videos
description: A comprehensive challenge with two tailored datasets (Lens and AdsQA) for multimodal reasoning evaluation. Lens covers general reasoning in 12 daily scenarios while AdsQA focuses on domain-specific reasoning in advertisement videos, featuring three competition tracks for visual grounding, spatial awareness VQA, and creative advertisement reasoning.
paper_title: MARS2 2025 Challenge on Multimodal Reasoning: Datasets, Methods, Results, Discussion, and Outlook

description: OpenGPT-4o-Image provides a large-scale dataset for advanced image generation and editing with 80k instruction-image pairs covering 11 domains and 51 subtasks. It introduces challenging categories like scientific imagery and complex instruction editing requiring simultaneous execution of multiple operations.
paper_title: OpenGPT-4o-Image: A Comprehensive Dataset for Advanced Image Generation and Editing

description: BLIP3o-60k is a high-quality instruction-tuning dataset for image generation created by prompting GPT-4o with diverse captions covering various scenes, objects, and human gestures. While the paper introduces BLIP3-o models, the dataset serves as a benchmark for unified multimodal models capable of both understanding and generation.
paper_title: BLIP3-o: A Family of Fully Open Unified Multimodal Models-Architecture, Training and Dataset

description: Constructs MMPD dataset, the first large-scale benchmark for multi-modal pedestrian detection incorporating RGB, IR, Depth, LiDAR, and Event data. Includes EventPed, a newly collected dataset covering wide range of sensor modalities.
paper_title: When Pedestrian Detection Meets Multi-Modal Learning: Generalist Model and Benchmark Dataset

description: A unified knowledge protocol with multimodal knowledge graph datasets that categorizes data knowledge into five unit types (in-image, in-text, cross-image, cross-text, image-text). It organizes datasets beyond simple image-text pairs to enable more sophisticated reasoning and vision-language pre-training.
paper_title: UKnow: A Unified Knowledge Protocol with Multimodal Knowledge Graph Datasets for Reasoning and Vision-Language Pre-Training

description: A large-scale dataset for instruction-based image editing from real photographs, featuring comprehensive coverage of edit types, multi-turn editing scenarios, preference data for alignment research, and paired long-short instructions. It addresses the lack of high-quality, openly accessible datasets built from real images.
paper_title: Pico-Banana-400K: A Large-Scale Dataset for Text-Guided Image Editing

description: A refined dataset for vision-language instruction tuning created through a tune-cross-evaluation paradigm that evaluates dataset quality using Meta Quality, Dataset Quality, and Sample Quality metrics. It demonstrates that strategic sample selection can achieve comparable performance with half the data.
paper_title: On the Evaluation and Refinement of Vision-Language Instruction Tuning Datasets

description: UnicBench is a comprehensive benchmark for image editing that extends beyond basic edits to explicitly assess spatial and knowledge-driven reasoning. It introduces novel metrics including Non-edit Consistency and Reasoning Accuracy for fine-grained diagnosis of multimodal model limitations.
paper_title: UnicEdit-10M: A Dataset and Benchmark Breaking the Scale-Quality Barrier via Unified Verification for Reasoning-Enriched Edits

description: FoodLogAthl-218 is a real-world food image dataset constructed from actual dietary management app usage with 6,925 images across 218 categories. It uniquely provides temporal incremental fine-tuning protocols and context-aware classification tasks where models must classify multiple dishes within meal contexts.
paper_title: FoodLogAthl-218: Constructing a Real-World Food Image Dataset Using Dietary Management Applications

description: LMHaze provides a large-scale, high-quality real-world dehazing dataset with over 5K high-resolution paired hazy/haze-free images spanning diverse indoor/outdoor environments and multiple haze intensities. It introduces a novel LMM-based benchmark study to simulate human perception for evaluating dehazed images, surpassing existing datasets by over 25 times in size.
paper_title: LMHaze: Intensity-aware Image Dehazing with a Large-scale Multi-intensity Real Haze Dataset

description: BenchSeg introduces a novel multi-view food video segmentation dataset with 25,284 meticulously annotated frames capturing 55 dish scenes under free 360° camera motion. It provides a dedicated temporal evaluation protocol with continuity, flicker rate, and IoU drift metrics to quantify segmentation stability over time, revealing failure modes invisible in standard per-frame evaluations.
paper_title: BenchSeg: A Large-Scale Dataset and Benchmark for Multi-View Food Video Segmentation

description: IndraEye is a multi-sensor EO-IR dataset for UAV-based aerial perception with 5,612 images and 145,666 instances across multiple viewing angles, altitudes, seven backgrounds, and different times of day. It addresses both illumination changes and correlation shifts in aerial perception, supporting multimodal learning and domain adaptation research for robust aerial object detection and segmentation.
paper_title: IndraEye: Infrared Electro-Optical UAV-based Perception Dataset for Robust Downstream Tasks

description: A comprehensive benchmark for creative image manipulation under complex instructions with fully automated QA-based evaluation. Covers three categories and nine creative dimensions with over 13K evaluation queries to assess complex and creative editing capabilities.
paper_title: CREval: An Automated Interpretable Evaluation for Creative Image Manipulation under Complex Instructions

description: Two new benchmarks for multi-response reward evaluation: MR²Bench-Image with human-annotated rankings over 8 model responses, and MR²Bench-Video derived from 94K crowdsourced pairwise human judgments over video question-answering spanning 19 models.
paper_title: You Only Judge Once: Multi-response Reward Modeling in a Single Forward Pass

description: A benchmark that constructs unanswerable instances from answerable ones via transformations along visual modality dependency and evidence sufficiency axes. Evaluates effective abstention in multimodal reasoning systems.
paper_title: Knowing When Not to Answer: Evaluating Abstention in Multimodal Reasoning Systems

description: A comprehensive benchmark for continual vision-language learning in remote sensing comprising 10 curated subsets. The benchmark evaluates models' ability to adapt to new sensing modalities and tasks without catastrophic forgetting.
paper_title: Continual Vision-Language Learning for Remote Sensing: Benchmarking and Analysis

description: A benchmark evaluation dataset for VLMs in remote sensing, consisting of human-annotated captions and visual question-answer pairs. Enables comprehensive assessment of VLMs in the context of remote sensing applications.
paper_title: RSGPT: A Remote Sensing Vision Language Model and Benchmark

description: GEOBench-VLM is a comprehensive benchmark specifically designed to evaluate VLMs on geospatial tasks including temporal change detection, large-scale object counting, and tiny object detection in remote sensing imagery. It addresses the unique challenges of geospatial applications not covered by generic VLM benchmarks.
paper_title: GEOBench-VLM: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models for Geospatial Tasks

description: OmniEarth is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating remote sensing VLMs across 28 fine-grained tasks covering perception, reasoning, and robustness dimensions. It includes proprietary satellite imagery and adopts a blind test protocol with quintuple semantic consistency requirements.
paper_title: OmniEarth: A Benchmark for Evaluating Vision-Language Models in Geospatial Tasks

description: GeoRC is the first benchmark for geolocation reasoning chains, evaluating VLMs' ability to explain which image evidence led to their location predictions. It collaborates with expert GeoGuessr players to provide ground truth reasoning chains addressing hundreds of discriminative visual attributes.
paper_title: GeoRC: A Benchmark for Geolocation Reasoning Chains

description: This work proposes a comprehensive benchmark to gauge VLMs' capabilities on Earth observation data, assessing scene understanding, localization and counting, and change detection tasks. It addresses the transfer of capabilities from natural images to satellite and aerial images, revealing that VLMs have strong world knowledge but poor spatial reasoning that limits usefulness on object localization and counting tasks.
paper_title: Good at captioning, bad at counting: Benchmarking GPT-4V on Earth observation data

description: CHOICE systematically evaluates hierarchical remote sensing capabilities of VLMs across 2 primary dimensions (perception and reasoning), 6 secondary dimensions, and 23 leaf tasks. It uniquely provides comprehensive assessment of Earth observation capabilities with data collected from 50 globally distributed cities and multiple-choice format for objective evaluation.
paper_title: CHOICE: Benchmarking the Remote Sensing Capabilities of Large Vision-Language Models

description: SARChat-Bench-2M is the first large-scale multimodal dialogue dataset for SAR images with approximately 2 million high-quality image-text pairs across diverse scenarios. It uniquely enables VLM evaluation on synthetic aperture radar image interpretation, providing a paradigmatic framework for remote sensing vertical domains.
paper_title: SARChat-Bench-2M: A Multi-Task Vision-Language Benchmark for SAR Image Interpretation

description: Benchmark for real-world geo-temporal reasoning with 1,455 ground-level images from 80 countries requiring structured prediction of temporal and geographic attributes from visual evidence. Includes spatial-temporal reasoning tasks testing physical plausibility under real-world uncertainty.
paper_title: TimeSpot: Benchmarking Geo-Temporal Understanding in Vision-Language Models in Real-World Settings

description: The first fine-grained, multimodal geolocation benchmark for Korean street views with 1,080 high-resolution images across four urban clusters. It includes privacy-aware evaluation with Korean captions and multi-contextual annotations.
paper_title: Toward Reliable VLM: A Fine-Grained Benchmark and Framework for Exposure, Bias, and Inference in Korean Street Views

description: A large-scale benchmark built on real-world maps with 11,837 human-authored QA pairs across 1,025 maps spanning ten diverse map categories. Focuses on genuine geospatial reasoning with multiple question categories per map type.
paper_title: MapVerse: A Benchmark for Geospatial Question Answering on Diverse Real-World Maps

description: A scalable benchmark for map-to-street-view spatial reasoning asking models to infer camera viewing direction by aligning overhead maps with Street View images. Includes m2sv-20k benchmark and m2sv-sft-11k for supervised fine-tuning.
paper_title: m2sv: A Scalable Benchmark for Map-to-Street-View Spatial Reasoning

description: EarthWhere evaluates VLM image geolocation capabilities across two scales: WhereCountry (500 multiple-choice questions) and WhereStreet (310 fine-grained tasks). It assesses visual recognition, step-by-step reasoning, and evidence use with human-verified key visual clues and Shapley-reweighted thinking scores.
paper_title: Where on Earth? A Vision-Language Benchmark for Probing Model Geolocation Skills Across Scales

description: VRSBench is a versatile vision-language benchmark for remote sensing image understanding with 29,614 human-verified detailed captions, 52,472 object references, and 123,221 question-answer pairs. It addresses limitations of existing single-task datasets by providing comprehensive evaluation across multiple remote sensing tasks.
paper_title: VRSBench: A Versatile Vision-Language Benchmark Dataset for Remote Sensing Image Understanding

description: RSHR-Bench is a super-high-resolution benchmark for remote sensing with 5,329 images having at least 4,000 pixels on the long side. It addresses flawed reasoning-task designs in existing benchmarks and applies adversarial filtering to reduce language priors.
paper_title: A Benchmark for Ultra-High-Resolution Remote Sensing MLLMs

description: MarsRetrieval is the first retrieval benchmark for evaluating vision-language models on Martian geospatial discovery, featuring three tasks across multiple spatial scales: paired image-text retrieval, landform retrieval, and global geo-localization. It addresses domain-specific geomorphic distinctions in planetary settings.
paper_title: MarsRetrieval: Benchmarking Vision-Language Models for Planetary-Scale Geospatial Retrieval on Mars

description: DDFAV is a high-quality remote sensing LVLMs dataset created using data augmentation and mixing strategies, with RSPOPE evaluation method for hallucination assessment. It addresses the lack of specialized datasets for remote sensing applications of LVLMs.
paper_title: DDFAV: Remote Sensing Large Vision Language Models Dataset and Evaluation Benchmark

description: Large-scale benchmark extending existing VPR datasets with over 650,000 natural-language descriptions for place recognition. Enables investigation of multi-modal fusion for enhanced robustness and cross-modal retrieval for language-based localization in challenging environmental conditions.
paper_title: LaVPR: Benchmarking Language and Vision for Place Recognition

description: First comprehensive benchmark for evaluating robustness of Earth observation foundation models across six tasks and twelve corruption types including appearance-based and geometric perturbations. Focuses on high-resolution optical remote sensing images for realistic evaluation in critical applications.
paper_title: REOBench: Benchmarking Robustness of Earth Observation Foundation Models

description: BigEarthNet.txt provides a large-scale, multi-sensor RS image-text dataset with 464,044 co-registered Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2 multispectral images with 9.6M text annotations. It includes geographically anchored captions, VQA pairs, and referring expression detection instructions for comprehensive Earth observation evaluation across multiple tasks.
paper_title: BigEarthNet.txt: A Large-Scale Multi-Sensor Image-Text Dataset and Benchmark for Earth Observation

description: RSMMVP (Remote Sensing Multimodal Visual Patterns) benchmark specifically evaluates MLLMs on remote sensing tasks by identifying CLIP-blind pairs where CLIP-based models incorrectly assign high similarity to visually distinct RS images. It addresses unique challenges in RS imagery including visual grounding and spatial reasoning.
paper_title: A Vision Centric Remote Sensing Benchmark

description: VLRS-Bench is the first benchmark exclusively dedicated to complex remote sensing reasoning, structured across three core dimensions: Cognition, Decision, and Prediction. It comprises 2000 QA pairs with 71-word average length, spanning 14 tasks and up to 8 temporal phases, addressing the bias toward perception tasks in existing RS benchmarks.
paper_title: VLRS-Bench: A Vision-Language Reasoning Benchmark for Remote Sensing

description: Introduces a large-scale neuro-symbolic benchmark for constrained route planning in remote sensing. Features 10,821 route-planning samples with provably optimal solutions generated through automated framework integrating semantic masks with heuristic search, nearly 10 times larger than prior benchmarks.
paper_title: NeSy-Route: A Neuro-Symbolic Benchmark for Constrained Route Planning in Remote Sensing

description: Framework for analyzing long-term urban dynamics through multi-temporal remote sensing imagery spanning 42 US cities from 2005-2023. Features six urban understanding tasks from pixel-level change detection to comprehensive scene-level narratives.
paper_title: DynamicVL: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models for Dynamic City Understanding

description: Proposes a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating spatial reasoning in MLLMs on Earth imagery, supporting quantitative direction and distance reasoning, systematic topological relations, and complex object geometries beyond bounding boxes. It spans qualitative and quantitative spatial reasoning with various object reference formats including textual descriptions, visual overlays, and explicit geometry coordinates.
paper_title: EarthSpatialBench: Benchmarking Spatial Reasoning Capabilities of Multimodal LLMs on Earth Imagery

description: Novel benchmark for geo-temporal prediction combining geographic location inference with time-of-capture estimation. Features globally distributed images spanning over 120 years with meticulously annotated ground-truth reasoning processes for joint year and hierarchical location sequence matching.
paper_title: GTPred: Benchmarking MLLMs for Interpretable Geo-localization and Time-of-capture Prediction

description: HRVQA provides high-resolution aerial images (1024x1024 pixels) with 53,512 images and 1,070,240 semi-automatically generated QA pairs. It addresses the unique challenges of aerial image VQA including huge variation in appearance, scale, and orientation of concepts, targeting applications in disaster monitoring and urban planning.
paper_title: HRVQA: A Visual Question Answering Benchmark for High-Resolution Aerial Images

description: Large-scale multimodal dataset for street-level visual place recognition in pedestrian-only environments comprising 110,529 images and 2,527 video clips across 208 locations. Features comprehensive day-night coverage, multiple viewing angles, and multimodal annotations including GPS coordinates and semantic metadata.
paper_title: MMS-VPR: Multimodal Street-Level Visual Place Recognition Dataset and Benchmark

description: LLMGeo systematically evaluates multimodal language models' geolocation capabilities using a novel image dataset collected from various countries via Google Street View. It provides a comprehensive evaluation framework for both training-free and training-based geolocation assessment.
paper_title: LLMGeo: Benchmarking Large Language Models on Image Geolocation In-the-wild

description: This benchmark evaluates VLMs' ability to infer precise geographic locations from Street View images without specific geolocation training. It represents global coverage distribution and tests both single-image inference and agent-based approaches with supplemental tools.
paper_title: Evaluating Precise Geolocation Inference Capabilities of Vision Language Models

description: A map-based question-answering benchmark using choropleth maps from three geographical regions (US, India, China). Features 43 diverse question templates requiring spatial reasoning about relative relationships and map features.
paper_title: MAPWise: Evaluating Vision-Language Models for Advanced Map Queries

description: Evaluates vision-language models on height-aware 3D geospatial reasoning from RGB imagery alone, without expensive LiDAR sensors. Tests spatial reasoning across 16 task categories including elevation analysis, sky view factors, and land cover patterns in realistic urban planning scenarios.
paper_title: Geo3DVQA: Evaluating Vision-Language Models for 3D Geospatial Reasoning from Aerial Imagery

description: This study provides a comprehensive evaluation framework for geolocation capabilities of MLLMs, particularly focusing on identifying origins of street view imagery. It uniquely analyzes privacy risks associated with MLLM-enabled geolocation and evaluates performance within 1-kilometer radius accuracy.
paper_title: Evaluation of Geolocation Capabilities of Multimodal Large Language Models and Analysis of Associated Privacy Risks

description: VIR-Bench frames itinerary reconstruction from travel videos as a challenging task to evaluate MLLMs' geospatial-temporal intelligence. It uniquely focuses on long-distance travel scenarios spanning extended spatial and temporal scales, addressing gaps in current video benchmarks that focus on indoor or short-range activities.
paper_title: VIR-Bench: Evaluating Geospatial and Temporal Understanding of MLLMs via Travel Video Itinerary Reconstruction

description: Benchmark dataset comprising 14,706 question-answer pairs across three map types (choropleth maps, cartograms, proportional symbol maps) spanning six distinct themes. Evaluates MLLMs using six visual analytical tasks and examines impact of map design changes on model robustness and sensitivity.
paper_title: MapIQ: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models for Map Question Answering

description: Challenges VLMs with geographic temporal reasoning of moving targets in large-scale camera networks. Requires perspective switches between maps and videos, joint reasoning across non-overlapping fields of view, and inference over unobserved spatial-temporal regions.
paper_title: GTR-Bench: Evaluating Geo-Temporal Reasoning in Vision-Language Models

description: ME-RSRG introduces multi-entity reasoning grounding in remote sensing, moving beyond single-entity formulations to require explicit reasoning about relationships between multiple entities. It reformulates remote sensing grounding as a structured reasoning task with subject-object relationships and inter-entity modeling.
paper_title: Think and Answer ME: Benchmarking and Exploring Multi-Entity Reasoning Grounding in Remote Sensing

description: DVGBench focuses on implicit visual grounding in UAV imagery, requiring scenario-specific domain knowledge rather than explicit cues like position or color. It covers six application scenarios (traffic, disaster, security, sport, social, productive) with both explicit and implicit queries to test reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: DVGBench: Implicit-to-Explicit Visual Grounding Benchmark in UAV Imagery with Large Vision-Language Models

description: GovLA-10K is the first management-oriented multi-modal benchmark for low-altitude intelligence focused on urban governance. Unlike exhaustive object annotation, it targets functionally salient objects relevant to practical management needs and provides actionable management suggestions grounded in aerial observations.
paper_title: Towards Governance-Oriented Low-Altitude Intelligence: A Management-Centric Multi-Modal Benchmark With Implicitly Coordinated Vision-Language Reasoning Framework

description: OptSAR-RSVG introduces cross-domain remote sensing visual grounding between optical and SAR sensors, the first large-scale benchmark for this setting. It addresses cross-domain feature modeling challenges and fine-grained semantic discrimination across different sensor modalities in remote sensing applications.
paper_title: OptiSAR-Net++: A Large-Scale Benchmark and Transformer-Free Framework for Cross-Domain Remote Sensing Visual Grounding

description: IMAGEO-Bench systematically evaluates image geolocalization ability in LLMs through accuracy, distance error, geospatial bias, and reasoning process assessment. It includes three diverse datasets covering global street scenes, US points of interest, and unseen images, revealing performance disparities and geospatial biases in current models.
paper_title: From Pixels to Places: A Systematic Benchmark for Evaluating Image Geolocalization Ability in Large Language Models

description: A comprehensive benchmark for cross-view geo-localization and pose estimation with 10,859 panoramic-satellite image pairs across 128 cities in 49 countries, featuring 755,976 QA pairs for evaluating LMMs' geo-spatial understanding.
paper_title: GeoX-Bench: Benchmarking Cross-View Geo-Localization and Pose Estimation Capabilities of Large Multimodal Models

description: A visual benchmark for 3D understanding of remote sensing images with 54,951 pairs of RS images and pixel-level aligned depth maps with textual descriptions. Focuses on 3D spatial perception training and assessment in RS contexts.
paper_title: RS3DBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for 3D Spatial Perception in Remote Sensing

description: A Multi-modal, Multi-GSD, Multi-scene Remote Sensing dataset with approximately 2.1 million text-image pairs for text-to-image generation. Features various modalities, ground sample distances, and environmental conditions across diverse RS scenarios.
paper_title: MMM-RS: A Multi-modal, Multi-GSD, Multi-scene Remote Sensing Dataset and Benchmark for Text-to-Image Generation

description: Three multimodal RS benchmark datasets for land cover classification: Houston2013 (hyperspectral + multispectral), Berlin (hyperspectral + SAR), and Augsburg (hyperspectral + SAR + DSM). Enables assessment of multimodal fusion approaches.
paper_title: Multimodal Remote Sensing Benchmark Datasets for Land Cover Classification with A Shared and Specific Feature Learning Model

description: A multimodal remote sensing benchmark for cross-city semantic segmentation with two cross-city scenes: Berlin-Augsburg (Germany) and Beijing-Wuhan (China). Includes hyperspectral, multispectral, and SAR data for studying generalization across cities.
paper_title: Cross-City Matters: A Multimodal Remote Sensing Benchmark Dataset for Cross-City Semantic Segmentation using High-Resolution Domain Adaptation Networks

description: First benchmark for geolocating astronaut photography from the International Space Station, where ISS position is known but specific Earth locations depicted require automated localization. Provides manually determined geographic locations spanning range of spatial scales and scene types.
paper_title: ISS-Geo142: A Benchmark for Geolocating Astronaut Photography from the International Space Station

description: Evaluates foundation models' understanding of geo-spatial data in grid structure covering 16 climate variables across 150 locations. Features multimodal representations including tabular data, heatmaps, and geographic visualizations with tasks ranging from single location queries to complex spatiotemporal comparisons.
paper_title: GeoGrid-Bench: Can Foundation Models Understand Multimodal Gridded Geo-Spatial Data?

description: Introduces composed image retrieval to remote sensing, allowing queries by image examples modified by textual descriptions. Presents evaluation benchmark focused on color, context, density, existence, quantity, and shape modifications in remote sensing imagery.
paper_title: Composed Image Retrieval for Remote Sensing

description: A comprehensive platform analyzing over 500 publicly published Earth observation datasets across agriculture, land use, disaster monitoring, scene understanding, and climate domains. Provides a new benchmark for fair evaluation of deep learning methods on remote sensing data.
paper_title: EarthNets: Empowering AI in Earth Observation

description: RSCC introduces a large-scale Remote Sensing Change Caption dataset with 62,351 pre-/post-disaster image pairs spanning earthquakes, floods, wildfires, paired with rich human-like change captions. It bridges the temporal and semantic divide in remote sensing data by enabling bi-temporal disaster-aware understanding for vision-language models.
paper_title: RSCC: A Large-Scale Remote Sensing Change Caption Dataset for Disaster Events

description: SQuID (Satellite Quantitative Intelligence Dataset) is a benchmark of 2,000 satellite image Question-Answer pairs designed to evaluate quantitative spatial reasoning. It spans three difficulty tiers with both numerical range and categorical answers, addressing VLMs' fundamental limitation in counting and measurements due to patch embedding compression destroying pixel-level information.
paper_title: Reasoning with Pixel-level Precision: QVLM Architecture and SQuID Dataset for Quantitative Geospatial Analytics

description: CityLens is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating Large Vision-Language Models on predicting urban socioeconomic indicators from satellite and street view imagery across 17 globally distributed cities. It covers 6 key domains (economy, education, crime, transport, health, environment) with 11 prediction tasks using 3 evaluation paradigms, making it the most extensive socioeconomic benchmark to date in terms of geographic coverage and indicator diversity.
paper_title: CityLens: Evaluating Large Vision-Language Models for Urban Socioeconomic Sensing

description: GEOATTRACTION-500 provides a benchmark of 500 globally distributed tourist attractions for evaluating geographic equity and visual knowledge in text-to-video models. The Geo-Attraction Landmark Probing (GAP) framework systematically assesses how faithfully models synthesize attractions from diverse regions and popularity levels.
paper_title: Are Video Generation Models Geographically Fair? An Attraction-Centric Evaluation of Global Visual Knowledge
