description: ELSS (Emergency Landing Site Selection) benchmark evaluates UAV landing site assessment from remote sensing imagery, requiring understanding of semantic risks beyond geometric terrain analysis. It uniquely combines visual features with Point-of-Interest data for context-aware landing decisions.
paper_title: Semantically Aware UAV Landing Site Assessment from Remote Sensing Imagery via Multimodal Large Language Models

description: The benchmark provides about 210,000 remote sensing images with 1.3 million high-quality captions generated using multi-perspective methods. It focuses on enhancing vision-language models for remote sensing applications with comprehensive, multi-perspective image descriptions.
paper_title: Enhancing Remote Sensing Vision-Language Models Through MLLM and LLM-Based High-Quality Image-Text Dataset Generation

description: RSRT is a new benchmark featuring multiple structured captions per remote sensing image, specifically designed to evaluate the practical utility of VLM-generated text in zero-shot retrieval contexts. It addresses the lack of benchmarks for evaluating text-to-text retrieval in remote sensing.
paper_title: Beyond Pixels: A Training-Free, Text-to-Text Framework for Remote Sensing Image Retrieval

description: Introduces Geo-DLC task for object-level fine-grained image captioning in remote sensing and constructs DE-Dataset with 25 categories and 261,806 annotated instances. Also presents DE-Benchmark, an LLM-assisted evaluation suite for this task.
paper_title: DescribeEarth: Describe Anything for Remote Sensing Images

description: A comprehensive benchmark covering multiple remote sensing interpretation tasks and granularity levels for evaluating generalization in complex geospatial scenarios. Tests diverse sub-tasks at different interpretation granularities.
paper_title: SkyMoE: A Vision-Language Foundation Model for Enhancing Geospatial Interpretation with Mixture of Experts

description: The first benchmark for pixel-grounded geospatial reasoning with six sub-tasks that evaluates both answer accuracy and mask quality. It ensures authentic pixel-grounded reasoning for earth observation tasks while handling multi-temporal and multi-modal inputs.
paper_title: TerraScope: Pixel-Grounded Visual Reasoning for Earth Observation

description: A large-scale interactive multi-task instruction dataset for remote sensing change understanding with four complementary tasks. Encompasses change captioning, binary classification, counting, and localization for bi-temporal remote sensing images.
paper_title: Towards Comprehensive Interactive Change Understanding in Remote Sensing: A Large-scale Dataset and Dual-granularity Enhanced VLM

description: A large-scale vision-language dataset from 30-meter Landsat satellite imagery over Australia spanning 36+ years. Addresses the gap in low-resolution, multi-satellite, long-term archives essential for affordable global monitoring, with both captioning and VQA components.
paper_title: Landsat30-AU: A Vision-Language Dataset for Australian Landsat Imagery

description: A large-scale hierarchical road dataset built from GF-2 imagery covering 3,631 km² with dense road masks, vectorized centerlines, and three-level hierarchy labels. Enables joint training and evaluation of segmentation, topology reconstruction, and hierarchy classification for automated road mapping.
paper_title: A Large-Scale Remote Sensing Dataset and VLM-based Algorithm for Fine-Grained Road Hierarchy Classification

description: RoofNet is the largest and most geographically diverse multimodal dataset for global roof material classification, comprising samples from 184 sites across 4 continents. It includes 14 roofing types with rich metadata and supports AI-driven disaster risk assessment.
paper_title: RoofNet: A Global Multimodal Dataset for Roof Material Classification

description: LuojiaHOG is a geospatial-aware, label-extension-friendly image caption dataset for remote sensing image-text retrieval. It employs hierarchical spatial sampling, extensible classification to OGC standards, and detailed caption generation with comprehensive geographic coverage.
paper_title: LuoJiaHOG: A Hierarchy Oriented Geo-aware Image Caption Dataset for Remote Sensing Image-Text Retrival

description: ChatEarthNet is a global-scale, high-quality image-text dataset for remote sensing using Sentinel-2 data with natural language descriptions. It provides global coverage with detailed ChatGPT-generated captions and manual verification for enhanced accuracy.
paper_title: ChatEarthNet: A Global-Scale Image-Text Dataset Empowering Vision-Language Geo-Foundation Models

description: DisasterM3 provides a remote sensing vision-language dataset for global-scale disaster assessment across 5 continents. It features multi-hazard (10 disaster types), multi-sensor (optical + SAR), and multi-task capabilities spanning from disaster recognition to damage assessment and report generation.
paper_title: DisasterM3: A Remote Sensing Vision-Language Dataset for Disaster Damage Assessment and Response

description: EarthScape provides the first AI-ready multimodal dataset for surficial geologic mapping, integrating digital elevation models, aerial imagery, multi-scale terrain features, and hydrologic/infrastructure vector data. It uniquely addresses the labor-intensive nature of current geologic mapping workflows through a unified, reproducible pipeline.
paper_title: EarthScape: A Multimodal Dataset for Surficial Geologic Mapping and Earth Surface Analysis

description: CerraData-4MM provides a multimodal dataset combining Sentinel-1 SAR and Sentinel-2 MSI data for land use and land cover classification in the Cerrado biome. It features hierarchical classification levels and addresses challenges like class imbalance and visually similar categories in environmental monitoring.
paper_title: CerraData-4MM: A multimodal benchmark dataset on Cerrado for land use and land cover classification

description: Multimodal dataset for Glacial Lake Outburst Floods monitoring and prediction, integrating Sentinel-2 multispectral imagery, NASA ITS_LIVE velocity products, and MODIS temperature records. Addresses fragmented data issues for rare but destructive hazard prediction.
paper_title: GLOFNet -- A Multimodal Dataset for GLOF Monitoring and Prediction

description: Largest multi-sensor land cover dataset with very-high-resolution (20 cm) annotations covering France, combining six aligned modalities including aerial imagery, Sentinel-1/2 time series, SPOT imagery, topographic data, and historical aerial images for comprehensive land cover and crop mapping.
paper_title: FLAIR-HUB: Large-scale Multimodal Dataset for Land Cover and Crop Mapping

num_samples: 3.8 million annotated objects across 510k high-resolution images
description: Introduces GroundSet, a large-scale dataset grounded in verifiable cadastral vector data for spatial understanding in remote sensing. Features 135 granular semantic categories and comprehensive instruction-tuning benchmark spanning seven spatial reasoning tasks.
paper_title: GroundSet: A Cadastral-Grounded Dataset for Spatial Understanding with Vector Data

description: Introduces RB-SCD dataset, the first benchmark for semantic change detection of roads and bridges. Offers comprehensive fine-grained annotations for 11 semantic change categories to analyze traffic infrastructure evolution.
paper_title: Semantic Change Detection of Roads and Bridges: A Fine-grained Dataset and Multimodal Frequency-driven Detector

description: A geolocation benchmark combining visual reasoning with knowledge-intensive multi-hop queries across two levels. Tests extracting and composing fragmented visual cues and increases difficulty with long-tail knowledge and obfuscated key entities, requiring expert-annotated stepwise traces.
paper_title: GeoBrowse: A Geolocation Benchmark for Agentic Tool Use with Expert-Annotated Reasoning Traces

description: A dynamic and interactive evaluation benchmark for tool-augmented GIS agents that provides a realistic execution sandbox with 117 atomic GIS tools. It encompasses 53 typical spatial analysis tasks across 6 core GIS domains with dynamic runtime feedback and multimodal spatial outputs.
paper_title: GeoAgentBench: A Dynamic Execution Benchmark for Tool-Augmented Agents in Spatial Analysis

description: Novel benchmark assessing embodied reasoning through precise visual grounding using hierarchical three-stage evaluation protocol. Spans critical domains including indoor, kitchen, driving, and robotic manipulation scenarios requiring object localization, task-driven pointing, and visual trace prediction.
paper_title: Point-It-Out: Benchmarking Embodied Reasoning for Vision Language Models in Multi-Stage Visual Grounding

description: FSIR-BD introduces the Few-Shot Text-to-Image Retrieval task with 38,353 images and 303 queries, focusing on compositional and out-of-distribution scenarios. It's the first benchmark to explicitly target image retrieval by text with reference examples, emphasizing challenging compositional reasoning.
paper_title: Few Shots Text to Image Retrieval: New Benchmarking Dataset and Optimization Methods

description: CrossHOI-Bench is a multiple-choice HOI benchmark with explicit positives and curated negatives that enables unified evaluation of both VLMs and HOI-specific models. It focuses on challenging scenarios like multi-person scenes and fine-grained interaction distinctions to reveal real differences between paradigms.
paper_title: CrossHOI-Bench: A Unified Benchmark for HOI Evaluation across Vision-Language Models and HOI-Specific Methods

description: AMIGO evaluates agentic vision-language models on multi-turn hidden-target identification across galleries of visually similar images. The model must recover a target image by asking Yes/No/Unsure questions under strict protocols, testing question selection under uncertainty, constraint tracking across turns, and fine-grained discrimination.
paper_title: AMIGO: Agentic Multi-Image Grounding Oracle Benchmark

description: RAGNet is a large-scale grasping-oriented affordance segmentation benchmark with 273k images, 180 categories, and 26k reasoning instructions across diverse embodied domains (wild, robot, ego-centric, simulation). It increases instruction difficulty by removing category names and providing only functional descriptions, focusing on reasoning-based affordance prediction for general grasping.
paper_title: RAGNet: Large-scale Reasoning-based Affordance Segmentation Benchmark towards General Grasping

description: VP-Bench systematically evaluates MLLMs' capability in visual prompt perception and utilization using a two-stage framework. It employs 30k visualized prompts spanning eight shapes and 355 attribute combinations to assess how well models interpret and use visual prompts like bounding boxes.
paper_title: VP-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Visual Prompting in Multimodal Large Language Models

description: Proposes multi-context visual grounding task to assess MLLMs' ability to localize instances across multiple images. Constructs dataset with 2K high-quality manually annotated samples featuring instance-level labeled image pairs and open-ended text prompts covering 20 practical skills.
paper_title: MC-Bench: A Benchmark for Multi-Context Visual Grounding in the Era of MLLMs

description: RefBench-PRO decomposes referring expression comprehension into perception and reasoning dimensions across six progressively challenging tasks (attribute, position, interaction, commonsense, relation, reject). It provides interpretable evaluation of MLLM grounding capabilities and includes a fully automated data-generation pipeline for diverse referring expressions across cognitive difficulty levels.
paper_title: RefBench-PRO: Perceptual and Reasoning Oriented Benchmark for Referring Expression Comprehension

description: Knowledge-driven Referring Expression Comprehension benchmark requiring fine-grained multimodal reasoning across text and image based on real-world knowledge. Features elaborately constructed negative samples and three novel evaluation metrics to assess robustness and anti-hallucination capabilities.
paper_title: KnowDR-REC: A Benchmark for Referring Expression Comprehension with Real-World Knowledge

description: OmniGround is a comprehensive spatio-temporal video grounding benchmark with 3,475 videos spanning 81 categories and complex real-world queries. It uniquely addresses limitations in existing benchmarks by including diverse objects and complex queries, using a Forward-Backward-Refinement annotation pipeline for high-quality labels.
paper_title: OmniGround: A Comprehensive Spatio-Temporal Grounding Benchmark for Real-World Complex Scenarios

description: VLT-MI introduces multi-round interaction into Visual Language Tracking for the first time, enabling text updates and object recovery during tracking. It generates diverse, multi-granularity texts for multi-modal interaction using LLMs, expanding VLT to include interactive paradigms.
paper_title: Visual Language Tracking with Multi-modal Interaction: A Robust Benchmark

description: Know-Show evaluates spatio-temporal grounded reasoning by unifying reasoning and localization within a single framework. It tests the ability to reason about actions while simultaneously grounding inferences in visual and temporal evidence across five complementary spatial and temporal scenarios.
paper_title: Know-Show: Benchmarking Video-Language Models on Spatio-Temporal Grounded Reasoning

description: A crowdsourced dataset for grounding visual question answering where models must draw bounding boxes around objects that correctly respond to textual questions. Features 45,199 image-question pairs with ground truth bounding boxes, designed to compare machine performance against human expertise in visual grounding tasks.
paper_title: Toloka Visual Question Answering Benchmark

description: Multimodal Object-Entity Relation Extraction dataset comprising 21 relation types and 20,264 multimodal relational facts annotated on 3,559 pairs of textual news titles and corresponding images. Focuses on extracting cross-modal relational facts where entities appear in different modalities.
paper_title: MORE: A Multimodal Object-Entity Relation Extraction Dataset with a Benchmark Evaluation

description: Twitter2015-Urdu is the first dataset for Urdu Multimodal Named Entity Recognition, adapted from the Twitter2015 dataset with Urdu-specific grammar rules. It addresses the gap in MNER research for low-resource languages by providing annotated multimodal content in Urdu with both textual and visual context.
paper_title: A Benchmark Dataset and a Framework for Urdu Multimodal Named Entity Recognition

description: GroundingME systematically challenges MLLMs across four critical dimensions of visual grounding: discriminative (distinguishing similar objects), spatial (complex relational descriptions), limited (occlusions/tiny objects), and rejection (recognizing ungroundable queries). It creates 1,005 challenging examples that mirror real-world complexity to rigorously assess true grounding capabilities.
paper_title: GroundingME: Exposing the Visual Grounding Gap in MLLMs through Multi-Dimensional Evaluation

description: Proposes a high-quality benchmark dedicated to evaluating the ability to locate objects in images according to natural language instructions. Measures accuracy with multiple prompting approaches and compares performance against human accuracy.
paper_title: LocateBench: Evaluating the Locating Ability of Vision Language Models

description: ViTaB-A evaluates MLLMs on visual table attribution, testing models' ability to point to specific rows and columns that support answers across different table formats (Markdown, JSON, images).
paper_title: ViTaB-A: Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models on Visual Table Attribution

description: A large-scale evaluation dataset covering fine-grained semantic information like color, number, and material to assess semantic grounding ability of LVLMs. Addresses the gap in connecting language to physical-world entities referenced in images with comprehensive misgrounding analysis.
paper_title: Evaluation and Enhancement of Semantic Grounding in Large Vision-Language Models

description: Largest real-world dataset for interactive referential grounding in 3D scenes with imperfect language, containing 11.5K scanned rooms, 7.6M semantic relations, and 4.7M referential statements. Addresses language imperfections and ambiguities in indoor navigation scenarios.
paper_title: IRef-VLA: A Benchmark for Interactive Referential Grounding with Imperfect Language in 3D Scenes

description: First large-scale visual grounding benchmark for complex real-world scenarios using spatially aligned RGB and thermal infrared image pairs. Enables evaluation under diverse challenging conditions like illumination and weather changes with fine-grained scene annotations.
paper_title: RGBT-Ground Benchmark: Visual Grounding Beyond RGB in Complex Real-World Scenarios

description: First visual grounding dataset focused on aerial views with 5K real-world aerial images and 50K annotations. Emphasizes positional relations and spatial reasoning for distinguishing visually similar objects, requiring comprehensive spatial understanding.
paper_title: AerialVG: A Challenging Benchmark for Aerial Visual Grounding by Exploring Positional Relations

description: Benchmark for open-vocabulary visual grounding introducing OV-VG and OV-PL tasks to establish connections between language descriptions and localization of novel objects. Includes 7,272 OV-VG images and 1,000 OV-PL images for comprehensive evaluation.
paper_title: OV-VG: A Benchmark for Open-Vocabulary Visual Grounding

description: Large-scale dataset for interactive visual grounding under language ambiguity with over 520K images and open-ended goal-oriented disambiguation dialogues. First dataset specifically addressing ambiguity-aware human-robot interaction for practical applications.
paper_title: InViG: Benchmarking Interactive Visual Grounding with 500K Human-Robot Interactions

description: Benchmark for diverse-modal entity linking constructed from existing datasets, covering text, image, and table modalities. Unified evaluation framework for entity linking across different modality configurations beyond traditional single-modality approaches.
paper_title: Benchmarking Diverse-Modal Entity Linking with Generative Models

description: SK-VG advances visual grounding by requiring reasoning over long-form scene knowledge where image content and referring expressions alone are insufficient. It forces models to integrate external scene knowledge with visual understanding, going beyond simple description-based grounding to test deeper reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: Advancing Visual Grounding with Scene Knowledge: Benchmark and Method

description: A benchmark for context-aware image retrieval in visual histories that reformulates retrieval as autonomous exploration. Uses a human-model collaborative pipeline to mine latent spatiotemporal associations and requires multi-step reasoning over raw visual histories.
paper_title: DeepImageSearch: Benchmarking Multimodal Agents for Context-Aware Image Retrieval in Visual Histories

description: First fine-grained referring image segmentation benchmark tailored for Low-Altitude Drone scenarios. Addresses unique challenges like diverse viewpoints, high object density, category drift from tiny objects, and object drift in crowded same-class scenes.
paper_title: RIS-LAD: A Benchmark and Model for Referring Low-Altitude Drone Image Segmentation

description: A new Referring Image Segmentation benchmark with complex language queries that include informative descriptions of surrounding objects and backgrounds. Built upon RefCOCO and Visual Genome datasets, it challenges existing RIS methods with enriched, specific queries that resemble real-world applications.
paper_title: Towards Complex-query Referring Image Segmentation: A Novel Benchmark

description: The work constructs two new benchmarks: UAVDE-2M with over 2.4M instances across 1,800+ categories, and UAVCAP-15K providing rich image-text pairs for vision-language pretraining in UAV-based open-vocabulary object detection.
paper_title: Light-Weight Cross-Modal Enhancement Method with Benchmark Construction for UAV-based Open-Vocabulary Object Detection

description: OVDEval is a new benchmark with 9 sub-tasks that introduces evaluations on commonsense knowledge, attribute understanding, position understanding, and object relation comprehension for open-vocabulary detection. It provides hard negatives and a new NMS-AP metric to address limitations of traditional AP metrics.
paper_title: How to Evaluate the Generalization of Detection? A Benchmark for Comprehensive Open-Vocabulary Detection

description: RefEdit-Bench is a rigorous real-world benchmark rooted in RefCOCO for evaluating instruction-based image editing models on referring expressions. It reveals limitations of existing approaches in complex scenes with multiple entities.
paper_title: RefEdit: A Benchmark and Method for Improving Instruction-based Image Editing Model on Referring Expressions

description: RefDrone is a referring expression comprehension benchmark for drone scenes that addresses unique challenges including varying viewpoints, occlusions and scale variations in aerial views. It reveals three key challenges: multi-scale detection, multi-target samples, and complex contextual expressions.
paper_title: RefDrone: A Challenging Benchmark for Referring Expression Comprehension in Drone Scenes

description: OmniLabel provides a challenging benchmark with more than 28K unique object descriptions on over 25K images for language-based object detection. It subsumes standard and open-vocabulary detection as well as referring expressions, with object descriptions that can refer to one, multiple or no objects.
paper_title: OmniLabel: A Challenging Benchmark for Language-Based Object Detection

description: A multi-platform, multi-modal 3D grounding benchmark featuring RGB and LiDAR data from vehicle, drone, and quadruped platforms. Provides over 128,000 objects and 22,000 validated referring expressions across diverse outdoor scenes, 10x larger than existing datasets.
paper_title: 3EED: Ground Everything Everywhere in 3D

description: GroundingSuite provides a comprehensive benchmark for pixel grounding tasks with a large-scale training dataset of 9.56 million referring expressions and a meticulously curated evaluation benchmark of 3,800 images. It addresses limitations in existing datasets including limited object categories, insufficient textual diversity, and scarcity of high-quality annotations.
paper_title: GroundingSuite: Measuring Complex Multi-Granular Pixel Grounding

description: Introduces a real-world benchmark with hand-labeled part segmentation annotations and task-oriented instructions to evaluate models' performance in understanding and executing part-level tasks within everyday contexts. Tests VLMs' ability to understand object components and their affordances for functional task execution.
paper_title: InstructPart: Task-Oriented Part Segmentation with Instruction Reasoning

description: GroundedPlanBench is the first benchmark to evaluate spatially grounded long-horizon action planning for robot manipulation, combining hierarchical sub-action planning with spatial action grounding. It uniquely assesses whether generated sub-actions are spatially executable by requiring models to specify exact spatial locations where robots should interact.
paper_title: Spatially Grounded Long-Horizon Task Planning in the Wild

description: The paper presents the first reasoning grasping benchmark dataset generated from GraspNet-1 billion, incorporating implicit instructions for object-level and part-level grasping. It uniquely requires robots to generate grasp poses based on indirect verbal instructions rather than explicit commands.
paper_title: Reasoning Grasping via Multimodal Large Language Model

description: SCOUT is a high-quality synthetic dataset that mimics human reasoning in visual grounding, providing rich supervision signals with step-by-step multimodal reasoning processes. It enables evaluation of spatial awareness and visual grounding capabilities in multimodal large language models while maintaining existing knowledge.
paper_title: TWIST & SCOUT: Grounding Multimodal LLM-Experts by Forget-Free Tuning

description: A benchmark designed to diagnose multimodal instance-level understanding capabilities in LMMs, focusing on fine-grained comprehension and alignment of specific visual elements. The benchmark evaluates models' ability to understand instances with explicit visual cues.
paper_title: INST-IT: Boosting Instance Understanding via Explicit Visual Prompt Instruction Tuning

description: STGR provides unified spatio-temporal supervision for video reasoning with explicit spatio-temporal evidence including timestamps, objects, and bounding boxes. It enables training models to generate traceable and verifiable reasoning processes with spatial and temporal grounding.
paper_title: Open-o3-Video: Grounded Video Reasoning with Explicit Spatio-Temporal Evidence

description: VUE-STG is a new benchmark for comprehensive evaluation of spatio-temporal grounding (STG), offering critical improvements over existing STG datasets. It enables models to identify not only timestamps but also bounding boxes of target objects within time ranges. The paper also introduces VUE-TR-V2 (upgraded from VUE-TR) and VUE-PLOT for plot understanding with character and reasoning tracks.
paper_title: Vidi2.5: Large Multimodal Models for Video Understanding and Creation

description: MoCentric-Bench is a motion-centric benchmark that ensures video MLLMs leverage the interaction between motion and language rather than being dominated by static appearance cues. It introduces four motion-centric probing techniques for pixel-level visual grounding based on motion patterns.
paper_title: PixFoundation 2.0: Do Video Multi-Modal LLMs Use Motion in Visual Grounding?

description: An extension of RefAVA dataset comprising >2.9 million frames and >75.1k annotated persons for Referring Atomic Video Action Recognition (RAVAR). It focuses on recognizing fine-grained, atomic-level actions of specific persons conditioned on natural language descriptions in complex multi-person scenarios.
paper_title: RefAtomNet++: Advancing Referring Atomic Video Action Recognition using Semantic Retrieval based Multi-Trajectory Mamba

description: A dataset containing 36,630 instances with manually annotated textual descriptions for Referring Atomic Video Action Recognition (RAVAR). It enables identifying atomic actions of specific individuals based on textual descriptions, differing from traditional action recognition by focusing on query-specific person identification.
paper_title: Referring Atomic Video Action Recognition

description: A large-scale real-world visual-language grounding benchmark for robotic perception and manipulation with multi-granularity annotations including bounding boxes, segmentation masks, grasp poses, contact points, and human-verified fine-grained language descriptions. Covers approximately 165,000 images, over 800 object instances, 1.3 million segmentation, detection, and language annotations, and roughly 11 billion grasping examples.
paper_title: RealVLG-R1: A Large-Scale Real-World Visual-Language Grounding Benchmark for Robotic Perception and Manipulation

description: CityLoc is a benchmark built from multi-source point clouds for fine-grained text-to-point-cloud localization. It facilitates systematic evaluation across diverse scenes for the task of inferring precise spatial positions within 3D point cloud maps from natural language descriptions.
paper_title: VLM-Loc: Localization in Point Cloud Maps via Vision-Language Models

description: New benchmark comprising 918 images and 2,533 instance-level masks with diverse reasoning queries to evaluate reasoning segmentation tasks. Focuses on bridging vision-language models and segmentation models through visual-only prompts.
paper_title: NOVO: Bridging LLaVA and SAM with Visual-only Prompts for Reasoning Segmentation

description: A new benchmark protocol for few-shot object detection that evaluates detectors pre-trained on external data and fine-tuned on multi-modal K-shot examples. Reformulates FSOD as aligning foundation models to target concepts using text and visual cues.
paper_title: Revisiting Few-Shot Object Detection with Vision-Language Models

description: An auto-labeled dataset with over 72k object expressions in complex video scenes for referring video object segmentation. Includes 2k manually validated video objects to benchmark grounded understanding in dynamic visual content.
paper_title: Sa2VA: Marrying SAM2 with LLaVA for Dense Grounded Understanding of Images and Videos

description: A four-metric suite (grounding efficiency, content alignment, lexical adaptation, human-likeness) to systematically evaluate VLM performance in interactive grounding contexts. It deploys 150 self-play sessions of referential games to assess how VLMs build common ground through communication.
paper_title: Measuring How (Not Just Whether) VLMs Build Common Ground

description: TRACE dataset pairs robotic manipulation instructions with explicit textual rationales for affordance prediction. It tests VLMs' ability to externalize spatial reasoning before predicting precise coordinate locations for robotic actions, integrating textual chain of reasoning with spatial understanding.
paper_title: TRACE: Textual Reasoning for Affordance Coordinate Extraction

description: A frame-level dataset for autonomous driving with semantic annotations using a 28-key category knowledge base covering environment, road geometry, vehicle behavior, and severity scales. Enables training VLMs for interpretable scene-level understanding in driving contexts.
paper_title: CARScenes: Semantic VLM Dataset for Safe Autonomous Driving

description: A high-quality synthetic dataset for instance-grounded text-to-image generation created by re-captioning 1M curated web images. Uses VLM-generated comprehensive descriptions processed by LLMs to extract categories for open-vocabulary detection, linking global prompts to instance annotations.
paper_title: ROVI: A VLM-LLM Re-Captioned Dataset for Open-Vocabulary Instance-Grounded Text-to-Image Generation

description: Unifies holistic video understanding with pixel-precise segmentation through detailed captions and temporally consistent masks. Tests both high-level understanding and language-guided pixel-accurate segmentation with phrase grounding.
paper_title: ViCaS: A Dataset for Combining Holistic and Pixel-level Video Understanding using Captions with Grounded Segmentation

description: TikTalkCoref is the first Chinese multimodal coreference dataset for real-world social media dialogues, derived from Douyin short videos. It uniquely pairs videos with user comment dialogues and includes manually annotated coreference clusters linking text mentions to person head regions in video frames.
paper_title: Multimodal Coreference Resolution for Chinese Social Media Dialogues: Dataset and Benchmark Approach

description: First benchmark unifying corpus-level retrieval with pixel-level grounding for object search across large-scale image collections. The task requires models to both decide if a described object appears in images and localize it with bounding boxes or segmentation masks.
paper_title: ReSeDis: A Dataset for Referring-based Object Search across Large-Scale Image Collections

description: FineCops-Ref introduces a fine-grained compositional referring expression comprehension dataset with controllable difficulty levels requiring multi-level reasoning across object categories, attributes, and multi-hop relationships. It uniquely incorporates negative text and images through fine-grained editing to test models' ability to reject scenarios where target objects are absent.
paper_title: New Dataset and Methods for Fine-Grained Compositional Referring Expression Comprehension via Specialist-MLLM Collaboration

description: CANVAS benchmarks VLMs on tool-based user interface design through iterative design software operations. It evaluates both design replication and modification capabilities, assessing VLMs' potential to collaborate with designers within conventional software environments.
paper_title: CANVAS: A Benchmark for Vision-Language Models on Tool-Based User Interface Design

description: The first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating both outcome and process reward models on computer-using agent tasks, encompassing trajectories from 10 software categories and 7 agent architectures. It enables systematic assessment across trajectory-level and step-level evaluation with expert annotation protocols.
paper_title: CUARewardBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Reward Models on Computer-using Agent

description: StarBench is a turn-based RPG benchmark derived from Honkai: Star Rail that evaluates multimodal decision-making from pixels to actions and agentic information seeking. It features both direct control and tool-assisted control regimes with an ask-or-act diagnostic to measure when agents seek guidance.
paper_title: StarBench: A Turn-Based RPG Benchmark for Agentic Multimodal Decision-Making and Information Seeking

description: Mobile-Env is a comprehensive toolkit for creating GUI benchmarks in Android mobile environment that offers isolated and controllable settings for reliable evaluations. It accommodates intermediate instructions and rewards with both open-world and fixed world task sets.
paper_title: Mobile-Env: Building Qualified Evaluation Benchmarks for LLM-GUI Interaction

description: PPTArena measures reliable PowerPoint slide modifications under natural-language instructions across 100 decks and 2,125 slides with over 800 targeted edits. It focuses on in-place editing of real slides covering text, charts, tables, animations, and styles with dual VLM-as-judge evaluation.
paper_title: PPTArena: A Benchmark for Agentic PowerPoint Editing

description: FronTalk pioneers conversational code generation with multi-modal feedback for front-end development, featuring dialogues with both textual and visual instructions. It includes a novel agent-based evaluation framework using web agents to measure both functional correctness and user experience.
paper_title: FronTalk: Benchmarking Front-End Development as Conversational Code Generation with Multi-Modal Feedback

description: Benchmark with 32.2K instances across 158 web-based tasks using natural HTML pages originally designed for crowdsourcing workers. Evaluates models' ability to perform complex web-based annotation tasks in realistic environments.
paper_title: Tur[k]ingBench: A Challenge Benchmark for Web Agents

description: Hierarchical benchmark for visual website development spanning static UI-to-code generation, interactive multi-page frontend reproduction, and long-horizon full-stack development. Features real-world websites with workflow-based agent verification using GUI agents and VLM-based judges.
paper_title: Vision2Web: A Hierarchical Benchmark for Visual Website Development with Agent Verification

description: WebMMU evaluates three core web tasks: website visual question answering, code editing (HTML/CSS/JavaScript), and mockup-to-code generation. It uses expert-annotated real-world web data to assess complex multi-step reasoning, element grounding, and functional UI comprehension.
paper_title: WebMMU: A Benchmark for Multimodal Multilingual Website Understanding and Code Generation

description: This benchmark investigates whether MLLMs can mimic human preferences when evaluating individual UIs and comparing them using data from a crowdsourcing platform. It benchmarks GPT-4o, Claude, and Llama across 30 interfaces, examining alignment with human judgments on multiple UI factors, focusing on subjective user evaluations across varied interfaces.
paper_title: MLLM as a UI Judge: Benchmarking Multimodal LLMs for Predicting Human Perception of User Interfaces

description: WebUIBench is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating MLLMs in WebUI-to-Code tasks across four key areas: WebUI Perception, HTML Programming, WebUI-HTML Understanding, and WebUI-to-Code. It comprises high-quality question-answer pairs derived from real-world websites.
paper_title: WebUIBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models in WebUI-to-Code

description: A multi-framework, multi-task evaluation benchmark for automated front-end engineering encompassing React, Vue, Angular and vanilla HTML/CSS across generation, edit, and repair tasks. Unique for incorporating mainstream development frameworks and evaluating complete development workflows beyond just code generation.
paper_title: DesignBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for MLLM-based Front-end Code Generation

description: Interaction2Code is the first benchmark for generating interactive webpages from interactive prototyping. It encompasses 127 unique webpages and 374 distinct interactions across 15 webpage types and 31 interaction categories, focusing on dynamic interaction rather than just static webpage generation.
paper_title: Interaction2Code: Benchmarking MLLM-based Interactive Webpage Code Generation from Interactive Prototyping

description: FullFront evaluates MLLMs across the full front-end development pipeline through three fundamental tasks: Webpage Design (conceptualization), Webpage Perception QA (comprehension), and Webpage Code Generation (implementation). It employs a novel two-stage process to transform real-world webpages into clean, standardized HTML.
paper_title: FullFront: Benchmarking MLLMs Across the Full Front-End Engineering Workflow

description: Design2Code is the first real-world benchmark for converting visual designs into code implementations. It manually curates 484 diverse real-world webpages as test cases and develops automatic evaluation metrics to assess how well MLLMs can generate code implementations that render into given reference webpages from screenshots.
paper_title: Design2Code: Benchmarking Multimodal Code Generation for Automated Front-End Engineering

description: Novel benchmark for multimodal understanding of how UI/UX design affects user behavior, built on real-world UI image pairs from industry A/B tests with empirically validated winners. Evaluates both prediction of effective UI and post-hoc explanation capabilities.
paper_title: Do MLLMs Capture How Interfaces Guide User Behavior? A Benchmark for Multimodal UI/UX Design Understanding

description: First comprehensive benchmark for Chinese mobile GUI agents built on physical device environments. Spans 201 mainstream apps across four device types with two-level structure evaluating atomic abilities and realistic application-level performance along five dimensions.
paper_title: GUI-CEval: A Hierarchical and Comprehensive Chinese Benchmark for Mobile GUI Agents

description: An interactive benchmarking suite for multimodal form-filling agents comprising web-based interface, backend evaluation, and constructed dataset. Covers diverse real-world scenarios with various field formats and high-fidelity form interactions to test GUI automation capabilities.
paper_title: FormFactory: An Interactive Benchmarking Suite for Multimodal Form-Filling Agents

description: A comprehensive smartphone agent benchmark for evaluating MLLM-based agents in interactive Android environments. Features diverse tasks in English and Chinese, plug-and-play framework for real-time agent interaction, and novel evaluation pipeline with seven metrics for task completion and resource consumption.
paper_title: SPA-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for SmartPhone Agent Evaluation

description: A comprehensive benchmark for proactive intelligence on mobile devices where agents autonomously anticipate needs and initiate actions. Formalizes proactive tasks as inferring latent user intent across four contextual dimensions with executable function sequences from 63 APIs across 14 scenarios.
paper_title: ProactiveMobile: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Boosting Proactive Intelligence on Mobile Devices

description: Self-generating, cross-platform, graph-based benchmark for virtual agent capabilities with controllable task complexity through subtask composition. Features multidimensional evaluation framework across 10 capabilities with 91% human acceptance rate.
paper_title: What Limits Virtual Agent Application? OmniBench: A Scalable Multi-Dimensional Benchmark for Essential Virtual Agent Capabilities

description: New video benchmark for multimodal GUI-oriented understanding featuring meticulously crafted Human-MLLM annotations across six GUI scenarios and eight types of GUI-oriented questions. Addresses temporal information perception on GUIs including dynamic web content and multi-step tasks, covering desktop software and multi-window interactions.
paper_title: GUI-World: A Video Benchmark and Dataset for Multimodal GUI-oriented Understanding

description: WebRRSBench jointly evaluates Reasoning, Robustness, and Safety across web understanding tasks with 729 websites. It uniquely focuses on multi-step inference over page structure, robustness to UI perturbations, and safety-critical interaction detection for web-facing MLLM applications.
paper_title: Benchmarking MLLM-based Web Understanding: Reasoning, Robustness and Safety

description: HippoCamp evaluates agents' capabilities on multimodal file management in user-centric environments, instantiating device-scale file systems over real-world profiles spanning 42.4 GB across 2K+ files. It uniquely models individual user profiles and tests context-aware reasoning in massive personal file systems with 46.1K densely annotated structured trajectories.
paper_title: HippoCamp: Benchmarking Contextual Agents on Personal Computers

description: VisBrowse-Bench introduces visual-native search evaluation for multimodal browsing agents with 169 VQA instances. It uniquely evaluates visual reasoning capabilities during search through multimodal evidence cross-validation via text-image retrieval and joint reasoning, focusing on native visual information of web pages.
paper_title: VisBrowse-Bench: Benchmarking Visual-Native Search for Multimodal Browsing Agents

description: EntWorld addresses the gap in enterprise-oriented benchmarks with 1,756 tasks across six enterprise domains including CRM, ITIL, and ERP systems. It uniquely adopts a schema-grounded task generation framework and SQL-based deterministic verification, focusing on high-density interfaces and strict business logic constraints.
paper_title: EntWorld: A Holistic Environment and Benchmark for Verifiable Enterprise GUI Agents

description: BrowseComp-V³ consists of 300 challenging questions emphasizing deep, multi-level, and cross-modal multi-hop reasoning where critical evidence is interleaved across textual and visual modalities. It uniquely incorporates expert-validated, subgoal-driven process evaluation for fine-grained analysis of intermediate reasoning behaviors.
paper_title: BrowseComp-$V^3$: A Visual, Vertical, and Verifiable Benchmark for Multimodal Browsing Agents

description: A benchmark for mobile GUI agents featuring 20K real-world Android device interaction demonstrations with contextual information and user history. Introduces two novel tracks: proactive task suggestions based on environmental observation and user intent analysis, and personalized task execution catering to individual user action preferences.
paper_title: FingerTip 20K: A Benchmark for Proactive and Personalized Mobile LLM Agents

description: Comprehensive environment for evaluating automated GUI Testing across three key subtasks: test intention generation, test task execution, and GUI defect detection. Evaluates performance on real mobile applications, applications with injected defects, and synthetic data.
paper_title: GUI Testing Arena: A Unified Benchmark for Advancing Autonomous GUI Testing Agent

description: First web-based benchmark specifically designed to evaluate visual reasoning and interaction capabilities of MLLM-powered agents through diverse CAPTCHA puzzles. Introduces CAPTCHA Reasoning Depth metric quantifying cognitive and motor steps required to solve each puzzle across 20 modern CAPTCHA types.
paper_title: Open CaptchaWorld: A Comprehensive Web-based Platform for Testing and Benchmarking Multimodal LLM Agents

description: MLA-Trust is the first comprehensive framework to evaluate multimodal LLM-based agents' trustworthiness in GUI environments across four dimensions: truthfulness, controllability, safety and privacy. It uses 34 high-risk interactive tasks with websites and mobile applications as testbeds, revealing unique trustworthiness vulnerabilities in interactive scenarios.
paper_title: MLA-Trust: Benchmarking Trustworthiness of Multimodal LLM Agents in GUI Environments

description: UI-NEXUS is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating mobile agents on compositional operations including Simple Concatenation, Context Transition, and Deep Dive. It supports interactive evaluation in 20 controllable local utility apps and 30 online service apps, comprising 100 interactive task templates with average optimal step count of 14.05.
paper_title: Atomic-to-Compositional Generalization for Mobile Agents with A New Benchmark and Scheduling System

description: ColorBench introduces a novel graph-structured benchmarking framework that bridges offline and online evaluation for mobile agents. It contains 175 complex long-horizon tasks with an average length of over 13 steps, supporting evaluation of multiple valid solutions and enabling quasi-dynamic interaction through modeling finite states observed during real-device interactions.
paper_title: ColorBench: Benchmarking Mobile Agents with Graph-Structured Framework for Complex Long-Horizon Tasks

description: MMSearch-Plus enforces genuine multimodal reasoning by requiring extraction and propagation of fine-grained visual cues through iterative image-text retrieval under noise. It addresses the limitation of existing benchmarks that can be solved with text-only heuristics, requiring spatial and temporal reasoning to extrapolate from visual cues to out-of-image facts.
paper_title: MMSearch-Plus: Benchmarking Provenance-Aware Search for Multimodal Browsing Agents

description: A compact but challenging benchmark of 111 information-seeking questions that evaluate web agents' ability to interact with live web content through virtual keyboard and mouse commands. Unlike prior benchmarks using synthetic pages, BEARCUBS requires accessing real web content and performing multimodal interactions like video understanding and 3D navigation.
paper_title: BEARCUBS: A benchmark for computer-using web agents

description: First benchmark assessing multimodal browsing agents' capabilities with images in prompts and crucial information embedded in webpage images/videos. Includes verified checklists for fine-grained analysis of multimodal dependencies.
paper_title: MM-BrowseComp: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Multimodal Browsing Agents

description: MMInA is a multihop and multimodal benchmark to evaluate embodied agents for compositional Internet tasks on evolving real-world websites. It features 1,050 human-written tasks requiring information extraction from multiple websites and proposes novel evaluation protocols for multihop web browsing.
paper_title: MMInA: Benchmarking Multihop Multimodal Internet Agents

description: First high-fidelity benchmark and interaction environment tailored for vehicle GUIs. Defines 185 parameterized tasks spanning explicit control, implicit intent understanding, and safety-aware tasks with structured multimodal observations and precise programmatic checks.
paper_title: Automotive-ENV: Benchmarking Multimodal Agents in Vehicle Interface Systems

description: Multi-page question-answering dataset requiring reasoning across multiple related web pages. Evaluates information extraction, multimodal retrieval and composition from many web pages with three question categories: single-screen QA, multi-screen QA, and navigation trace-based QA.
paper_title: WebQuest: A Benchmark for Multimodal QA on Web Page Sequences

description: OSWorld is the first scalable, real computer environment for multimodal agents supporting task setup, execution-based evaluation, and interactive learning across Ubuntu, Windows, and macOS. It creates a benchmark of computer tasks involving real web and desktop apps with detailed initial state setup and custom execution-based evaluation scripts.
paper_title: OSWorld: Benchmarking Multimodal Agents for Open-Ended Tasks in Real Computer Environments

description: M^3-Bench is the first benchmark for evaluating multimodal tool use under the Model Context Protocol, targeting realistic multi-hop and multi-threaded workflows requiring visual grounding, textual reasoning, and cross-tool dependencies. It spans 28 servers with 231 tools and provides standardized trajectories with human verification.
paper_title: M^3-Bench: Multi-Modal, Multi-Hop, Multi-Threaded Tool-Using MLLM Agent Benchmark

description: First benchmark for evaluating multimodal agents in professional software environments across 6 disciplines and 13 core applications. Establishes a capability hierarchy for agent use of professional software with executable real-computer environment and human-in-the-loop evaluation paradigm.
paper_title: ProSoftArena: Benchmarking Hierarchical Capabilities of Multimodal Agents in Professional Software Environments

description: First comprehensive benchmark for assessing computer-use agents' tool invocation abilities via Model Context Protocol alongside GUI operation and decision-making. Features 158 high-quality tools across 7 common applications with automated code-generation pipeline.
paper_title: OSWorld-MCP: Benchmarking MCP Tool Invocation In Computer-Use Agents

description: Novel benchmarking platform for cross-platform educational agent evaluation with knowledge base enhancement and dual-graph evaluation framework. Covers Windows, Android, and cross-platform collaborative tasks with fine-grained evaluation metrics.
paper_title: KGCE: Knowledge-Augmented Dual-Graph Evaluator for Cross-Platform Educational Agent Benchmarking with Multimodal Language Models

description: EconWebArena is a benchmark for evaluating autonomous agents on complex, multimodal economic tasks in realistic web environments. It comprises tasks from 82 authoritative websites spanning economic domains, challenging agents to navigate live websites, interpret structured and visual content, and extract precise, time-sensitive economic data.
paper_title: EconWebArena: Benchmarking Autonomous Agents on Economic Tasks in Realistic Web Environments

description: Benchmark evaluating VLMs on automating conversion of rudimentary sketches into webpage prototypes. Supports interactive agent evaluation that mimics real-world design workflows where VLM-based agents iteratively refine generations through communication with simulated users.
paper_title: Sketch2Code: Evaluating Vision-Language Models for Interactive Web Design Prototyping

description: Web2Code provides a large-scale webpage-to-code dataset and evaluation framework for testing MLLMs' webpage understanding and HTML code translation abilities. It includes webpage images as inputs with corresponding HTML code as responses, plus diverse natural language QA pairs about webpage content for comprehensive web content understanding.
paper_title: Web2Code: A Large-scale Webpage-to-Code Dataset and Evaluation Framework for Multimodal LLMs

description: Benchmark for evaluating long-context multimodal agents with video understanding web tasks. Features manually crafted video tutorials totaling almost four hours, focusing on skill retention and factual retention tasks from human demonstrations.
paper_title: VideoWebArena: Evaluating Long Context Multimodal Agents with Video Understanding Web Tasks

description: LiveWeb-IE evaluates web information extraction systems against live websites rather than static HTML snapshots, addressing the temporal evolution of web content. It features natural language queries requiring extraction of text, images, and hyperlinks with four complexity levels based on attribute cardinality.
paper_title: LiveWeb-IE: A Benchmark For Online Web Information Extraction

description: WorldGUI tests GUI automation from non-canonical starting states, evaluating agent robustness when software is partially configured or interfaces differ from defaults. It covers ten desktop and web applications with systematically constructed diverse initial states to assess planning adaptability in realistic mid-workflow scenarios.
paper_title: WorldGUI: An Interactive Benchmark for Desktop GUI Automation from Any Starting Point

description: OmniACT extends beyond traditional web automation to cover diverse desktop applications, evaluating agents' capability to generate executable programs for computer tasks. It includes both fundamental tasks like 'Play the next song' and longer horizon tasks requiring complex multi-step execution across various software applications.
paper_title: OmniACT: A Dataset and Benchmark for Enabling Multimodal Generalist Autonomous Agents for Desktop and Web

description: VideoGUI evaluates GUI assistants on visual-centric tasks involving professional software like Adobe Photoshop, sourced from instructional videos. It features hierarchical evaluation from high-level planning to atomic action execution, focusing on complex activities requiring visual understanding rather than simple language-only instructions.
paper_title: VideoGUI: A Benchmark for GUI Automation from Instructional Videos

description: MPR-GUI-Bench evaluates multilingual Perception and Reasoning capabilities of GUI agents with fine-grained analysis of widget functions and spatial relationships. It addresses the gap in multilingual GUI evaluation by providing detailed assessments across different languages and GUI element interactions.
paper_title: MPR-GUI: Benchmarking and Enhancing Multilingual Perception and Reasoning in GUI Agents

description: VenusBench-GD provides comprehensive, bilingual GUI grounding evaluation across multiple platforms with hierarchical task taxonomy dividing grounding into basic and advanced categories. It features extensive application coverage, diverse UI elements, and six distinct subtasks for complementary model evaluation perspectives.
paper_title: VenusBench-GD: A Comprehensive Multi-Platform GUI Benchmark for Diverse Grounding Tasks

description: GUI-360 provides large-scale, comprehensive evaluation for computer-using agents with LLM-augmented automated pipeline for multi-modal trajectory collection. It features full-resolution screenshots, accessibility metadata, and hybrid GUI+API action space across Windows office applications with both successful and failed trajectories.
paper_title: GUI-360$^\circ$: A Comprehensive Dataset and Benchmark for Computer-Using Agents

description: GUIDE evaluates AI models' ability to understand user behavior, infer intent, and provide assistance in open-ended GUI tasks through screen recordings with think-aloud narrations. It shifts from automation to collaboration by testing behavior state detection, intent prediction, and help prediction across diverse software applications.
paper_title: GUIDE: A Benchmark for Understanding and Assisting Users in Open-Ended GUI Tasks

description: OSUniverse provides complex, multimodal desktop-oriented tasks for GUI-navigation agents with increasing complexity levels from basic clicking to multistep, multiapplication tests. It features automated validation with <2% error rate and is calibrated so SOTA agents achieve <50% while average workers achieve perfect accuracy.
paper_title: OSUniverse: Benchmark for Multimodal GUI-navigation AI Agents

description: IndusGCC is the first dataset tailored to LLM-based General Computer Control in industrial environments, covering seven domains from robotic arm control to production line configuration. It features multimodal human interaction data with mission-critical equipment software and introduces functional/structural metrics for control script evaluation.
paper_title: IndusGCC: A Data Benchmark and Evaluation Framework for GUI-Based General Computer Control in Industrial Automation

description: Mobile-Bench-v2 introduces a realistic benchmark for VLM-based mobile agents with slot-based instruction generation and multi-path evaluation. It includes noisy environments with pop-ups/ads, contaminated environments (AITZ-Noise), and ambiguous instruction splits with preset Q&A interactions to evaluate proactive interaction capabilities, addressing limitations in existing mobile agent benchmarks.
paper_title: Mobile-Bench-v2: A More Realistic and Comprehensive Benchmark for VLM-based Mobile Agents

description: UI-Vision is the first comprehensive, license-permissive benchmark for offline evaluation of computer use agents in desktop environments. It provides dense annotations including bounding boxes, UI labels, and action trajectories across 83 software applications with three fine-to-coarse grained tasks: Element Grounding, Layout Grounding, and Action Prediction.
paper_title: UI-Vision: A Desktop-centric GUI Benchmark for Visual Perception and Interaction

description: CRAB is the first agent benchmark framework designed to support cross-environment tasks with a graph-based fine-grained evaluation method. It enables evaluation across multiple devices (desktop and mobile) and can be extended to any environment with a Python interface, incorporating efficient task and evaluator construction mechanisms.
paper_title: CRAB: Cross-environment Agent Benchmark for Multimodal Language Model Agents

description: A benchmark for LLM-based mobile agents that expands UI operations with 103 APIs and categorizes tasks into SAST, SAMT, and MAMT reflecting varying complexity levels. Introduces CheckPoint metric to assess whether agents reach essential planning and reasoning steps, with 200+ multi-APP collaboration tasks.
paper_title: Mobile-Bench: An Evaluation Benchmark for LLM-based Mobile Agents

description: MobileBench-OL provides 1080 tasks from 80 Chinese mobile apps with an online benchmark that measures task execution, complex reasoning, and noise robustness in real-world mobile environments. It includes 5 subsets with multiple evaluation dimensions and an auto-eval framework with reset mechanism for stable benchmarking.
paper_title: MobileBench-OL: A Comprehensive Chinese Benchmark for Evaluating Mobile GUI Agents in Real-World Environment

description: PSPA-Bench is dedicated to evaluating personalization in smartphone GUI agents. It comprises over 12,855 personalized instructions aligned with real-world user behaviors across 10 daily-use scenarios and 22 mobile apps, introducing structure-aware process evaluation for fine-grained measurement of personalized capabilities.
paper_title: PSPA-Bench: A Personalized Benchmark for Smartphone GUI Agent

description: WebCanvas introduces an innovative online evaluation framework for web agents that addresses the dynamic nature of web interactions. Mind2Web-Live is a refined dataset containing 542 tasks with 2439 intermediate evaluation states, featuring novel evaluation metrics that capture critical intermediate actions while disregarding noise.
paper_title: WebCanvas: Benchmarking Web Agents in Online Environments

description: Persona2Web is the first benchmark for evaluating personalized web agents on the real open web. It requires agents to resolve ambiguous queries by inferring user preferences from historical context rather than relying on explicit instructions, featuring reasoning-aware evaluation framework.
paper_title: Persona2Web: Benchmarking Personalized Web Agents for Contextual Reasoning with User History

description: A verifiable long-chain web benchmark for agentic information-seeking that emphasizes long-chain complexity and subtask-level verifiability. Tasks are decomposed into sequences of interdependent verifiable subtasks across five real-world domains, enabling comprehensive evaluation of web agents in realistic environments.
paper_title: VeriWeb: Verifiable Long-Chain Web Benchmark for Agentic Information-Seeking

description: MVISU-Bench is a bilingual benchmark with 404 tasks across 137 mobile applications, focusing on five key challenges: Multi-App, Vague, Interactive, Single-App, and Unethical Instructions. It addresses real-world mobile agent automation needs identified through user questionnaires.
paper_title: MVISU-Bench: Benchmarking Mobile Agents for Real-World Tasks by Multi-App, Vague, Interactive, Single-App and Unethical Instructions

description: RealWebAssist evaluates sequential instruction-following in realistic long-horizon web interactions with real-world user instructions. It includes ambiguous instructions, different AI assistance levels, and evolving instructions reflecting changes in user mental state over multiple websites.
paper_title: RealWebAssist: A Benchmark for Long-Horizon Web Assistance with Real-World Users

description: A new web UI localization benchmark designed to evaluate VLMs' ability to locate elements on web interfaces. Used to assess web navigation capabilities and complements existing web agent evaluation frameworks.
paper_title: Surfer-H Meets Holo1: Cost-Efficient Web Agent Powered by Open Weights

description: A comprehensive benchmark suite of 50+ interactive challenges designed to evaluate general-purpose web-browsing AI agents. Tests fundamental browser interactions, advanced input processing, cognitive tasks, workflow automation, and interactive entertainment with verifiable ground-truth solutions.
paper_title: WebGames: Challenging General-Purpose Web-Browsing AI Agents

description: Introduces a simulation environment engine for GUI agent navigation research that enables flexible definition and composition of screens, icons, and navigation graphs with full access to environment information. Provides comprehensive agent training and evaluation capabilities for complex screen navigation challenges.
paper_title: GUI Exploration Lab: Enhancing Screen Navigation in Agents via Multi-Turn Reinforcement Learning

description: WorkArena++ consists of 682 tasks corresponding to realistic workflows performed by knowledge workers, evaluating planning, problem-solving, logical/arithmetic reasoning, retrieval, and contextual understanding abilities of web agents. It uniquely focuses on enterprise settings with real workplace scenarios.
paper_title: WorkArena++: Towards Compositional Planning and Reasoning-based Common Knowledge Work Tasks

description: The benchmark evaluates UI-to-code generation from real-world webpage images. It includes 350 real-world websites divided into seen and unseen parts to mitigate data leakage, testing layout preservation and code generation accuracy.
paper_title: MLLM-Based UI2Code Automation Guided by UI Layout Information

description: First benchmark evaluating VLMs' ability to function as world models for mobile GUI agents, using semantic state transitions described in natural language rather than pixel predictions. Includes MobileWorld dataset with 1.4M samples.
paper_title: MobileWorldBench: Towards Semantic World Modeling For Mobile Agents

description: InquireBench is designed to evaluate mobile agents' capabilities in safe interaction and proactive inquiry with users, encompassing 5 categories and 22 sub-categories. It specifically tests when VLM-based mobile agents should seek human confirmation at critical decision points rather than acting autonomously.
paper_title: InquireMobile: Teaching VLM-based Mobile Agent to Request Human Assistance via Reinforcement Fine-Tuning

description: MobiFlow is a comprehensive benchmarking suite designed to evaluate mobile agent systems. It provides systematic evaluation of GUI-based mobile agents' performance in real-world mobile scenarios and task execution.
paper_title: MobiAgent: A Systematic Framework for Customizable Mobile Agents

description: Large-scale dataset for complete screen parsing with dense annotations of all visible UI elements across 771K web screenshots. Provides comprehensive supervision for UI understanding with boxes, 55-class types, and text annotations for 21M elements.
paper_title: Moving Beyond Sparse Grounding with Complete Screen Parsing Supervision

description: ReVision introduces Visual Instruction Rewriting, transforming multimodal instructions into text-only commands for privacy-preserving task-oriented interactions. The dataset spans 14 domains and enables on-device processing while maintaining visual privacy.
paper_title: ReVision: A Dataset and Baseline VLM for Privacy-Preserving Task-Oriented Visual Instruction Rewriting

description: First dataset for modeling UI interactions in precision engineering tasks with 20x longer time horizons than existing datasets. Tests both UI interaction learning and spatial reasoning in professional 3D CAD environments.
paper_title: VideoCAD: A Dataset and Model for Learning Long-Horizon 3D CAD UI Interactions from Video

description: SIMMC 2.0 provides task-oriented dialog grounded in immersive, photo-realistic shopping environments, addressing the limitation of existing datasets that don't situate dialog in multimodal context. It uses a novel two-phase pipeline combining multimodal dialog simulation with manual paraphrasing for diverse referring expressions.
paper_title: SIMMC 2.0: A Task-oriented Dialog Dataset for Immersive Multimodal Conversations

description: A real-world dataset for webpage code generation from designs, comprising 2.56 million instances with design images, webpage code, and layout details. It addresses the gap in high-quality, large-scale datasets for automated webpage development by MLLMs.
paper_title: WebCode2M: A Real-World Dataset for Code Generation from Webpage Designs

description: A benchmark evaluating whether MLLMs can faithfully recreate webpages from demonstration videos. Contains 175 webpages across diverse categories with fine-grained, human-aligned visual rubrics for evaluating generated webpages across multiple dimensions.
paper_title: WebVR: Benchmarking Multimodal LLMs for WebPage Recreation from Videos via Human-Aligned Visual Rubrics

description: A benchmark for standardized and verifiable evaluation of MLLMs as generalist game agents in browser environments. Contains 34 diverse games and 170 tasks with state-verifiable metrics, testing both computer-use agents and generalist multimodal agents in semantic action spaces.
paper_title: GameWorld: Towards Standardized and Verifiable Evaluation of Multimodal Game Agents

description: A meta-evaluation benchmark for assessing Vision-Language Models as auditors of Computer-Use Agents across macOS, Windows, and Linux environments. Evaluates accuracy, calibration of confidence estimates, and inter-model agreement in judging task completion.
paper_title: CUAAudit: Meta-Evaluation of Vision-Language Models as Auditors of Autonomous Computer-Use Agents

description: EvoEnv is a dynamic evaluation environment simulating a trainee agent exploring novel workplace scenarios. It evaluates agents on context-aware scheduling, active exploration under uncertainty, and continuous learning from dynamically generated tasks.
paper_title: The Agent's First Day: Benchmarking Learning, Exploration, and Scheduling in the Workplace Scenarios

description: A hierarchical benchmark for General Tool Agents spanning atomic tool use and open-ended workflows. Built on real-world authenticity with real user queries, deployed tools, and multimodal contexts, featuring both short-horizon closed-ended tasks and long-horizon open-ended workflows.
paper_title: GTA-2: Benchmarking General Tool Agents from Atomic Tool-Use to Open-Ended Workflows

description: A benchmark specifically designed for detailed captioning tasks with DCScore metric. Evaluates hallucinations and fine-grained comprehensiveness by deconstructing responses into primitive information units for individual assessment.
paper_title: Painting with Words: Elevating Detailed Image Captioning with Benchmark and Alignment Learning

description: HalDec-Bench is designed to evaluate hallucination detectors in image captioning in a principled and interpretable manner. It provides captions from diverse VLMs with human annotations for hallucination presence, detailed categories, and segment-level labels.
paper_title: HalDec-Bench: Benchmarking Hallucination Detector in Image Captioning

description: HalDec-Bench evaluates hallucination detectors in image captioning with captions generated by diverse VLMs and human annotations indicating hallucination presence, detailed type categories, and segment-level labels. It uniquely provides principled evaluation of hallucination detection capabilities across different captioning models and hallucination types.
paper_title: HalDec-Bench: Benchmarking Hallucination Detector in Image Captioning

description: A benchmark designed to evaluate commonsense-driven hallucination by creating explicit visual evidence-commonsense conflicts. Covers three dimensions: counting anomalies, relational anomalies, and attribute anomalies to test visual fidelity.
paper_title: CDH-Bench: A Commonsense-Driven Hallucination Benchmark for Evaluating Visual Fidelity in Vision-Language Models

description: DiffSeg30k is a dataset of 30k diffusion-edited images with pixel-level annotations for fine-grained AIGC detection. It shifts detection from binary classification to semantic segmentation, enabling localization of edits and identification of editing models through multi-turn editing scenarios.
paper_title: DiffSeg30k: A Multi-Turn Diffusion Editing Benchmark for Localized AIGC Detection

description: RoCOCO creates altered variants of MS-COCO test set by replacing words in text and generating visually altered images through mixing techniques. It evaluates robustness of vision-language models to subtle semantic changes, revealing that SOTA models often favor altered content over original.
paper_title: RoCOCO: Robustness Benchmark of MS-COCO to Stress-test Image-Text Matching Models

description: OODBench contains 40K instance-level out-of-distribution instance-category pairs with automated construction and minimal human verification. It comprehensively assesses VLMs' performance on OOD data using a Basic-to-Advanced progression of prompted questions across varying difficulty levels.
paper_title: OODBench: Out-of-Distribution Benchmark for Large Vision-Language Models

description: Scalable benchmark for fine-grained hallucination evaluation in LVLMs using automated data construction pipeline. Features hierarchical hallucination categorization assessing both faithfulness and factuality across 12 visual perception aspects and 6 knowledge domains with clean and noisy scenarios.
paper_title: SHALE: A Scalable Benchmark for Fine-grained Hallucination Evaluation in LVLMs

description: HQH is a High-Quality Hallucination benchmark that demonstrates superior reliability and validity under the proposed Hallucination benchmark Quality Measurement framework (HQM). It addresses inconsistent evaluation results in existing benchmarks and provides credible evaluation using specific indicators for reliability and validity assessment.
paper_title: Measuring the Measurers: Quality Evaluation of Hallucination Benchmarks for Large Vision-Language Models

description: MM-SAP (Self-Awareness in Perception for MLLMs) introduces the knowledge quadrant framework to define what MLLMs know and don't know about images. It's the first benchmark specifically designed to assess MLLMs' self-awareness capabilities in perception, addressing their struggle with understanding their own perceptual limitations.
paper_title: MM-SAP: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Assessing Self-Awareness of Multimodal Large Language Models in Perception

description: FREAK is a comprehensive multimodal benchmark for fine-grained hallucination assessment in MLLMs using high-quality photorealistic images with fine-grained counter-commonsense edits. It innovatively evaluates hallucination phenomena in detailed visual perception.
paper_title: FREAK: A Fine-grained Hallucination Evaluation Benchmark for Advanced MLLMs

description: PENDULUM comprises approximately 2,000 human-curated Visual Question Answering pairs specifically designed to elicit sycophantic responses in MLLMs. It spans six distinct image domains and introduces novel metrics to quantify sycophancy in visual reasoning.
paper_title: PENDULUM: A Benchmark for Assessing Sycophancy in Multimodal Large Language Models

description: RealFactBench is a comprehensive benchmark designed to assess fact-checking capabilities of LLMs and MLLMs across diverse real-world tasks, including Knowledge Validation, Rumor Detection, and Event Verification. It consists of 6K high-quality claims from authoritative sources, encompassing multimodal content and introducing the Unknown Rate (UnR) metric for handling uncertainty.
paper_title: RealFactBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Large Language Models in Real-World Fact-Checking

description: Comprehensive benchmark targeting relation hallucinations in MLLMs, integrating perceptive and cognitive perspectives. Built using Visual Genome scene graph dataset with systematic definition of relation hallucinations, addressing more complex reasoning compared to object/attribute-level hallucinations.
paper_title: Reefknot: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Relation Hallucination Evaluation, Analysis and Mitigation in Multimodal Large Language Models

description: First benchmark specifically tailored for evaluating object-related hallucinations across multiple images in MLLMs. Comprises three core tasks: Multi-Image Object Existence Hallucination, Multi-Image Object Count Hallucination, and Object Identity Consistency Hallucination, targeting semantic understanding across object existence, quantity reasoning, and cross-view identity consistency.
paper_title: MIHBench: Benchmarking and Mitigating Multi-Image Hallucinations in Multimodal Large Language Models

description: Diagnostic benchmark comprising 4,026 VQA instances across six semantic domains to quantify text bias in MLLMs. Employs Three-Level Evaluation Framework systematically increasing conflict between visual evidence and textual information, with Visual Robustness Score (VRS) metric designed to penalize linguistic shortcuts and reward true visual fidelity.
paper_title: V-FAT: Benchmarking Visual Fidelity Against Text-bias

description: AMBER is an LLM-free multi-dimensional benchmark for evaluating MLLM hallucinations across generative and discriminative tasks. It evaluates existence, attribute, and relation hallucinations with a low-cost evaluation pipeline that doesn't require expensive human annotation or advanced LLMs.
paper_title: AMBER: An LLM-free Multi-dimensional Benchmark for MLLMs Hallucination Evaluation

description: CertainlyUncertain presents a taxonomy of uncertainty specific to vision-language AI systems, distinguishing epistemic from aleatoric uncertainty. The benchmark features 178K VQA samples as contrastive pairs created by inpainting images and using captions to generate both answerable and unanswerable questions.
paper_title: Certainly Uncertain: A Benchmark and Metric for Multimodal Epistemic and Aleatoric Awareness

description: T2I-FactualBench is the largest benchmark for evaluating factuality of text-to-image models with knowledge-intensive concepts. It features a three-tiered framework from basic memorization to complex composition of knowledge concepts, using multi-round VQA for evaluation.
paper_title: T2I-FactualBench: Benchmarking the Factuality of Text-to-Image Models with Knowledge-Intensive Concepts

description: ORIC-Bench studies object recognition under contextual incongruity, where objects appear unexpectedly or fail to appear in expected contexts. It uses LLM-guided and CLIP-guided sampling strategies to construct incongruous object-context pairs that increase recognition difficulty for LVLMs.
paper_title: ORIC: Benchmarking Object Recognition under Contextual Incongruity in Large Vision-Language Models

description: This benchmark investigates selection bias in LVLMs through fine-grained MCQA tasks spanning easy, medium, and hard difficulty levels defined by semantic similarity. It reveals how selection biases intensify with task difficulty and provides tools for bias mitigation.
paper_title: Benchmarking and Mitigating MCQA Selection Bias of Large Vision-Language Models

description: TUBench evaluates LVLM reliability using unanswerable questions crafted with ten distinct strategies across four diverse domains: code snippets, natural images, geometry diagrams, and statistical tables. It specifically tests trustworthiness in code reasoning, commonsense reasoning, geometric reasoning, and mathematical reasoning when questions cannot be answered due to insufficient visual information.
paper_title: TUBench: Benchmarking Large Vision-Language Models on Trustworthiness with Unanswerable Questions

description: CLASH features COCO images paired with contradictory captions containing controlled object-level or attribute-level contradictions. It evaluates cross-modal contradiction detection capabilities in both multiple-choice and open-ended formats, addressing a fundamental capability for preventing hallucinations.
paper_title: CLASH: A Benchmark for Cross-Modal Contradiction Detection

description: VL-RewardBench is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating vision-language generative reward models, spanning general multimodal queries, visual hallucination detection, and complex reasoning tasks. It uniquely uses an AI-assisted annotation pipeline combined with human verification to create high-quality examples specifically designed to probe VL-GenRMs limitations.
paper_title: VL-RewardBench: A Challenging Benchmark for Vision-Language Generative Reward Models

description: VeriTaS is the first dynamic benchmark for multimodal automated fact-checking, designed to remain robust under ongoing large-scale pretraining of foundation models. It uniquely addresses data leakage issues with quarterly updates via automated pipeline, covering 108 fact-checking organizations across 54 languages with textual and audiovisual content.
paper_title: VeriTaS: The First Dynamic Benchmark for Multimodal Automated Fact-Checking

description: JUS dataset tests VLM capabilities through difficult queries and object counting scenarios to evaluate verbalized uncertainty. It introduces Net Calibration Error (NCE) to measure miscalibration direction and reveals that both LLMs and VLMs have high calibration error and tend to be overconfident.
paper_title: Overconfidence is Key: Verbalized Uncertainty Evaluation in Large Language and Vision-Language Models

description: PROVE introduces programmatic VLM evaluation using scene-graph representations to generate diverse QA pairs with executable verification programs. It constructs 10.5k challenging but visually grounded QA pairs and proposes programmatic evaluation strategy that measures both helpfulness and truthfulness of responses within a unified framework.
