paper_title: BioMol-MQA: A Multi-Modal Question Answering Dataset For LLM Reasoning Over Bio-Molecular Interactions

description: Comprehensive geometric reasoning corpus with two subsets (Geo-Thought-6K and Geo-Thought-Augmented-10K) containing visual descriptions, step-by-step solutions, explicit reasoning chains, and reflection steps for enhancing mathematical geometry reasoning in vision-language models.
paper_title: GeoThought: A Dataset for Enhancing Mathematical Geometry Reasoning in Vision-Language Models

description: The first benchmark centered on Feynman diagram tasks for evaluating multistep diagrammatic reasoning in theoretical physics. Spans electromagnetic, weak, and strong interactions with over 100 distinct types requiring conservation laws, symmetry constraints, and topological reasoning.
paper_title: FeynmanBench: Benchmarking Multimodal LLMs on Diagrammatic Physics Reasoning

description: An open-source toolkit for fair evaluation of medical VLMs across 6 multi-modal tasks, 23 datasets, and 11 medical domains. Provides comprehensive assessment of model generalizability in medical applications.
paper_title: MultiMedEval: A Benchmark and a Toolkit for Evaluating Medical Vision-Language Models

description: XBench is the first systematic benchmark for evaluating cross-modal interpretability in chest X-rays across seven CLIP-style VLM variants. It uniquely quantitatively assesses visual explanation alignment with radiologist-annotated regions using cross-attention and similarity-based localization maps.
paper_title: XBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Visual-Language Explanations in Chest Radiography

description: MedVision is a large-scale dataset and benchmark specifically designed to evaluate VLMs on quantitative medical image analysis tasks that are crucial for clinical decision-making. It focuses on three representative quantitative tasks: detection of anatomical structures and abnormalities, tumor/lesion size estimation, and angle/distance measurement across 22 public datasets covering diverse anatomies and modalities.
paper_title: MedVision: Dataset and Benchmark for Quantitative Medical Image Analysis

description: SurgXBench is an explainable vision-language model benchmark for surgery that evaluates VLMs' zero-shot performance on robotic-assisted laparoscopic datasets for instrument and action classification. It uniquely integrates explainable AI to visualize VLM attention and uncover causal explanations, providing metrics to assess reliability of model predictions in surgical contexts.
paper_title: SurgXBench: Explainable Vision-Language Model Benchmark for Surgery

description: Gut-VLM is a hallucination-aware multimodal benchmark for gastrointestinal image analysis that uniquely features tags identifying hallucinated sentences and their corrections. Unlike traditional datasets with only descriptive texts, it enables evaluation and mitigation of hallucination in medical VLMs through a two-stage pipeline involving ChatGPT generation followed by medical expert review and correction.
paper_title: Hallucination-Aware Multimodal Benchmark for Gastrointestinal Image Analysis with Large Vision-Language Models

description: MedHallTune is a large-scale benchmark designed specifically to evaluate and mitigate hallucinations in medical VLMs, comprising over 100,000 images and 1,000,000 instruction pairs. It includes both hallucination and non-hallucination samples with ground-truth annotations, evaluating models across clinical accuracy, relevance, detail level, and risk level metrics.
paper_title: MedHallTune: An Instruction-Tuning Benchmark for Mitigating Medical Hallucination in Vision-Language Models

description: Gastric-X is a large-scale multimodal benchmark for gastric cancer analysis providing paired resting/dynamic CT scans, endoscopic images, biochemical indicators, diagnostic notes, and bounding box annotations. It uniquely reflects realistic clinical workflows and examines whether VLMs can correlate biochemical signals with spatial tumor features and textual reports.
paper_title: Gastric-X: A Multimodal Multi-Phase Benchmark Dataset for Advancing Vision-Language Models in Gastric Cancer Analysis

description: CT-3DRRG establishes the first comprehensive benchmark for 3D radiograph report generation using the largest publicly available 3D CT-report dataset. It uniquely focuses on 3D medical imaging contexts and provides training recipes for high-performing VLMs in medical report generation.
paper_title: Argus: Benchmarking and Enhancing Vision-Language Models for 3D Radiology Report Generation

description: First and largest visual question answering benchmark for fetal ultrasound imaging evaluation. Covers anatomical plane identification, visual grounding, fetal orientation assessment, clinical view conformity, and diagnosis across over 42,000 images addressing critical gaps in prenatal care evaluation.
paper_title: FETAL-GAUGE: A Benchmark for Assessing Vision-Language Models in Fetal Ultrasound

description: Expert-curated benchmark encompassing 22 biomedical tasks across various scientific disciplines, microscopy modalities, scales, and organisms. Addresses lack of standardized vision-language benchmarks for biological image analysis revealing current model limitations and catastrophic forgetting issues.
paper_title: μ-Bench: A Vision-Language Benchmark for Microscopy Understanding

description: DeepTumorVQA is a diagnostic visual question answering benchmark targeting abdominal tumors in CT scans with 395K expert-level questions spanning Recognition, Measurement, Visual Reasoning, and Medical Reasoning categories. It introduces unique challenges including small tumor detection and clinical reasoning across 3D anatomy.
paper_title: Are Vision Language Models Ready for Clinical Diagnosis? A 3D Medical Benchmark for Tumor-centric Visual Question Answering

description: MedRepBench is a comprehensive benchmark built from de-identified real-world Chinese medical reports spanning diverse departments and demographics. It evaluates structured medical report interpretation through both objective field-level recall metrics and automated subjective evaluation using LLMs as scoring agents.
paper_title: MedRepBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Medical Report Interpretation

description: A comprehensive multimodal medical benchmark with 6,422 expert-validated image-text pairs spanning six imaging modalities and 28 subtasks. It uniquely evaluates both text generation and image generation capabilities through contextually intertwined instructions requiring sophisticated cross-modal reasoning, moving beyond multiple-choice formats to open-ended generative outputs.
paper_title: MedGEN-Bench: Contextually entangled benchmark for open-ended multimodal medical generation

description: A clinician-annotated dermatology VQA benchmark built on the Diverse Dermatology Images dataset with 656 clinical images from 570 unique patients spanning Fitzpatrick skin types I-VI. It uniquely uses a hierarchical annotation schema with 22 main questions covering diagnosis, morphology, and clinical reasoning rather than simple classification.
paper_title: DermaBench: A Clinician-Annotated Benchmark Dataset for Dermatology Visual Question Answering and Reasoning

description: A compact yet reasoning-intensive benchmark specifically designed to probe multimodal clinical reasoning in neurology, integrating multi-sequence MRI scans, structured EHRs, and clinical notes. It uniquely focuses on differential diagnosis, lesion recognition, and rationale generation rather than classification accuracy.
paper_title: Beyond Classification Accuracy: Neural-MedBench and the Need for Deeper Reasoning Benchmarks

description: A Korean medical licensing-exam-style multimodal benchmark with 1,534 questions and 2,043 associated images from Korean Medical Licensing Examinations (2012-2023). It uniquely requires cross-image evidence integration with about 30% containing multiple images and covers diverse clinical modalities including X-ray, CT, ECG, ultrasound, and endoscopy.
paper_title: KorMedMCQA-V: A Multimodal Benchmark for Evaluating Vision-Language Models on the Korean Medical Licensing Examination

description: The first language-conditioned, instance-level surgical grounding benchmark where each instance pairs a surgical image with natural-language description targeting a single instrument. It uniquely spans multiple surgical procedures (ophthalmic, laparoscopic, robotic, open) and evaluates both linguistic reference resolution and pixel-level localization.
paper_title: GroundedSurg: A Multi-Procedure Benchmark for Language-Conditioned Surgical Tool Segmentation

description: TuneVLSeg is an open-source benchmarking framework for prompt tuning techniques in Vision-Language Segmentation Models. It integrates 6 prompt tuning strategies on 2 VLSMs tested across 8 medical datasets including radiology and non-radiology domains.
paper_title: TuneVLSeg: Prompt Tuning Benchmark for Vision-Language Segmentation Models

description: B&J Benchmark comprises 1,245 questions from real-world orthopedics and sports medicine cases across 7 clinical reasoning tasks. It assesses integrated, multimodal reasoning essential for patient care beyond narrow examination success, revealing gaps in current AI clinical competency.
paper_title: The Illusion of Clinical Reasoning: A Benchmark Reveals the Pervasive Gap in Vision-Language Models for Clinical Competency

description: ResGo is a benchmark of laparoscopic frames annotated with Go Zone bounding boxes and clinician-authored rationales covering surgical phase, exposure quality reasoning, next actions and risk reminders. It addresses phase-dependent nature of intraoperative reasoning in minimally invasive surgery.
paper_title: SurGo-R1: Benchmarking and Modeling Contextual Reasoning for Operative Zone in Surgical Video

description: The paper introduces a Medical benchmark that applies multiple templates to VLMs in hierarchical medical visual question answering to evaluate sycophancy. It systematically assesses susceptibility to visual cues and authority-based biases in medical contexts.
paper_title: Benchmarking and Mitigating Sycophancy in Medical Vision Language Models

description: SurgVLM-Bench consists of 6 popular surgical datasets covering crucial downstream tasks for surgical intelligence. It evaluates vision-language models on surgical visual perception, temporal analysis, and reasoning across 16 surgical types and 18 anatomical structures.
paper_title: SurgVLM: A Large Vision-Language Model and Systematic Evaluation Benchmark for Surgical Intelligence

description: HistoVL is a comprehensive histopathology benchmark comprising images from 11 acquisition tools across 26 organs and 31 cancer types with over 5 million patches. It evaluates histopathology VLMs on diverse clinical scenarios with specifically crafted captions incorporating pathology descriptions.
paper_title: How Good is my Histopathology Vision-Language Foundation Model? A Holistic Benchmark

description: NOVA is an evaluation-only benchmark of brain MRI scans spanning 281 rare pathologies with rich clinical narratives and expert bounding-box annotations. It serves as an extreme stress-test for out-of-distribution generalization, enabling joint assessment of anomaly localization, visual captioning, and diagnostic reasoning.
paper_title: NOVA: A Benchmark for Anomaly Localization and Clinical Reasoning in Brain MRI

description: This is the first dataset providing comprehensive PET/CT-report pairs in Vietnamese, addressing gaps in functional imaging data and low-resource language representation in medical VLMs. It includes 2,757 whole-body PET/CT volumes with corresponding Vietnamese clinical reports.
paper_title: Toward a Vision-Language Foundation Model for Medical Data: Multimodal Dataset and Benchmarks for Vietnamese PET/CT Report Generation

description: RadLE (Radiology's Last Exam) is a pilot benchmark of 50 expert-level 'spot diagnosis' cases across multiple imaging modalities to evaluate frontier AI models against board-certified radiologists. It includes a taxonomy of visual reasoning errors and tests models through native web interfaces.
paper_title: Radiology's Last Exam (RadLE): Benchmarking Frontier Multimodal AI Against Human Experts and a Taxonomy of Visual Reasoning Errors in Radiology

description: 3MDBench simulates and evaluates LVLM-driven telemedical consultations with 2996 cases across 34 diagnoses from real-world telemedicine interactions. It uses temperament-based Patient Agents and Assessor Agents to evaluate both diagnostic accuracy and dialogue quality.
paper_title: 3MDBench: Medical Multimodal Multi-agent Dialogue Benchmark

description: ReXInTheWild provides 955 clinician-verified multiple-choice questions spanning seven clinical topics across 484 photographs from biomedical literature. It combines fine-grained natural image understanding with domain-specific medical reasoning for everyday medical photographs.
paper_title: ReXInTheWild: A Unified Benchmark for Medical Photograph Understanding

description: A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating trustworthiness of Medical Large Vision Language Models across five dimensions: trustfulness, fairness, safety, privacy, and robustness. It covers 16 medical image modalities and 27 anatomical regions with both closed and open-ended question formats.
paper_title: CARES: A Comprehensive Benchmark of Trustworthiness in Medical Vision Language Models

description: First bilingual multimodal VQA benchmark for ophthalmology with 30,120 QA pairs across 9 subspecialties and 548 conditions. Features real-world context from WeChat posts with multiple examinations per patient reflecting clinical scenarios.
paper_title: Benchmarking Large Multimodal Models for Ophthalmic Visual Question Answering with OphthalWeChat

description: First comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LVLMs on ultrasound understanding across 15 anatomical regions and 50 application scenarios. Covers classification, detection, regression, and text generation tasks including diagnosis, view recognition, lesion localization, and report generation.
paper_title: U2-BENCH: Benchmarking Large Vision-Language Models on Ultrasound Understanding

description: Comprehensive multimodal oro-dental dataset with 50,000 intraoral images, 8,056 radiographs, and detailed textual records collected over eight years. Tests classification of six oro-dental anomalies and generation of complete diagnostic reports from multimodal inputs.
paper_title: A benchmark multimodal oro-dental dataset for large vision-language models

description: Emergency Room Visual Question Answering dataset with expert-annotated open-ended questions covering diverse emergency room scenarios. Benchmarks healthcare knowledge of LVLMs in hospital settings with detailed error taxonomy and adapted evaluation metrics including Entailment Score and CLIPScore Confidence.
paper_title: ERVQA: A Dataset to Benchmark the Readiness of Large Vision Language Models in Hospital Environments

description: Large-scale surgical video QA dataset harvesting reasoning signals from academic surgical videos with 12 question categories covering safety assessment, decision rationale, and forecasting. Extracts structured supervision from expert narrations across 170 procedure types with expert-validated benchmark.
paper_title: SUREON: A Benchmark and Vision-Language-Model for Surgical Reasoning

description: Large-scale comprehensive medical VQA benchmark collected from 73 datasets spanning 12 modalities and 20+ anatomical regions from authentic medical scenarios. Addresses scarcity of diverse medical images for evaluating LVLMs in real-world medical applications.
paper_title: OmniMedVQA: A New Large-Scale Comprehensive Evaluation Benchmark for Medical LVLM

description: Structured benchmark for evaluating diagnostic reasoning in chest X-rays using automated pipeline that derives intermediate reasoning steps. Supports multi-path, multi-stage evaluation including visual grounding and diagnostic measurements across 12 diagnostic tasks.
paper_title: CXReasonBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Structured Diagnostic Reasoning in Chest X-rays

description: Most comprehensive general medical AI benchmark with well-categorized data structure and multi-perceptual granularity. Constructed from 284 datasets spanning 18 clinical tasks, 18 departments, and 4 perceptual granularities in VQA format with lexical tree structure for customizable evaluation.
paper_title: GMAI-MMBench: A Comprehensive Multimodal Evaluation Benchmark Towards General Medical AI

description: Comprehensive benchmark for evaluating LVLM performance on intelligent ophthalmic diagnosis tasks across multiple dimensions. Part of Eyecare Kit addressing ophthalmology-specific challenges with high-quality ophthalmic visual instruction dataset and specialized model architecture.
paper_title: EyecareGPT: Boosting Comprehensive Ophthalmology Understanding with Tailored Dataset, Benchmark and Model

description: MedMKEB is the first comprehensive benchmark for evaluating knowledge editing in medical multimodal large language models, assessing reliability, generality, locality, portability, and robustness with carefully constructed editing tasks and human expert validation.
paper_title: MedMKEB: A Comprehensive Knowledge Editing Benchmark for Medical Multimodal Large Language Models

description: FunBench is a visual question answering benchmark that comprehensively evaluates MLLMs' fundus reading skills for ophthalmology. It features hierarchical task organization across four levels and offers three targeted evaluation modes including modular analysis of LLM and vision encoder components.
paper_title: FunBench: Benchmarking Fundus Reading Skills of MLLMs

description: ECG-Reasoning-Benchmark is a novel multi-turn evaluation framework comprising over 6,400 samples to systematically assess step-by-step reasoning across 17 core ECG diagnoses. It specifically investigates whether MLLMs perform actual reasoning or rely on superficial visual cues in ECG interpretation, revealing critical failures in multi-step logical deduction.
paper_title: ECG-Reasoning-Benchmark: A Benchmark for Evaluating Clinical Reasoning Capabilities in ECG Interpretation

description: SpineBench is a comprehensive Visual Question Answering (VQA) benchmark designed for fine-grained analysis and evaluation of MLLMs in the spinal domain. It comprises 64,878 QA pairs from 40,263 spine images, covering 11 spinal diseases through two critical clinical tasks: spinal disease diagnosis and spinal lesion localization, with challenging hard negative options based on visual similarity.
paper_title: SpineBench: Benchmarking Multimodal LLMs for Spinal Pathology Analysis

description: OmniBrainBench is the first comprehensive multimodal VQA benchmark for brain imaging analysis across 15 distinct brain imaging modalities. It comprises 9,527 validated VQA pairs and encompasses 15 multi-stage clinical tasks simulating clinical workflows for comprehensive MLLM assessment.
paper_title: OmniBrainBench: A Comprehensive Multimodal Benchmark for Brain Imaging Analysis Across Multi-stage Clinical Tasks

description: MedErr-CT is a novel benchmark for evaluating medical MLLMs' ability to identify and correct errors in CT reports through VQA. It includes six error categories (Omission, Insertion, Direction, Size, Unit, Typo) organized into three task levels: classification, detection, and correction.
paper_title: MedErr-CT: A Visual Question Answering Benchmark for Identifying and Correcting Errors in CT Reports

description: Presents the Clinical Understanding and Retrieval Evaluation benchmark comprising 500 multimodal clinical cases mapped to physician-cited reference literature. It evaluates both clinical reasoning and evidence retrieval capabilities under controlled settings to disentangle their respective contributions.
paper_title: CURE: A Multimodal Benchmark for Clinical Understanding and Retrieval Evaluation

description: A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating medical MLLMs under image quality degradations across 18 degradation types, 30 capability dimensions, and 7 imaging modalities. Unique for introducing Calibration Shift metric to assess metacognitive reliability and systematically testing model performance under various quality degradations.
paper_title: MedQ-Deg: A Multidimensional Benchmark for Evaluating MLLMs Across Medical Image Quality Degradations

description: The first multimodal benchmark explicitly created to evaluate anatomical understanding in the neurosurgical domain, consisting of 9 hours of annotated neurosurgical videos covering 89 procedures. Unique for focusing on anatomical structure identification (68 clinical structures) rather than surgical procedure understanding.
paper_title: NeuroABench: A Multimodal Evaluation Benchmark for Neurosurgical Anatomy Identification

description: The first benchmark designed to evaluate fairness of MLLMs across diverse demographic attributes in medical tasks. What makes it unique is its focus on demographic fairness across race, ethnicity, language, and gender in medical VQA and report generation, introducing Fairness-Aware Performance metric.
paper_title: FMBench: Benchmarking Fairness in Multimodal Large Language Models on Medical Tasks

description: A benchmark specifically designed to evaluate Chain-of-Thought reasoning correctness, efficiency, impact, and consistency in medical image understanding across 24 examination types and 13 varying-difficulty tasks. Unique for focusing on reasoning paths rather than just final answers in medical diagnosis.
paper_title: M3CoTBench: Benchmark Chain-of-Thought of MLLMs in Medical Image Understanding

description: The first benchmark specifically designed to evaluate medical reward models and judges across 13 organ systems and 8 clinical departments. Unique for focusing on reward model evaluation in medical scenarios with expert-annotated cases across six clinically critical dimensions including diagnostic accuracy and clinical relevance.
paper_title: Med-RewardBench: Benchmarking Reward Models and Judges for Medical Multimodal Large Language Models

description: The first expert-driven multimodal in-context learning benchmark for medical tasks, with 111 problems curated by medical experts covering 6 specialties and 13 imaging modalities. Unique for focusing on multimodal ICL abilities in medical domain with task demonstrations and expert validation.
paper_title: SMMILE: An Expert-Driven Benchmark for Multimodal Medical In-Context Learning

description: First benchmark for Medical Image Sequences Grounding, focusing on sequential medical images rather than single images. It evaluates two paradigms: Image Difference Grounding (detecting change regions across images) and Image Consistency Grounding (detecting consistent semantics), covering 76 public datasets and 10 medical imaging modalities.
paper_title: MedSG-Bench: A Benchmark for Medical Image Sequences Grounding

description: Large-scale benchmark for surgical action planning evaluation using cholecystectomy procedures. Provides temporally-grounded surgical action annotations with 1,226 clinically validated action clips, enabling MLLMs to generate next action recommendations from current surgical scenes.
paper_title: SAP-Bench: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models in Surgical Action Planning

description: Multilingual, multimodal benchmark of highly complex clinical cases with up to 7 distinct visual clinical evidence types per case. Mirrors clinical workflow by evaluating differential diagnosis generation and final diagnosis selection, revealing synthesis failure modes in MLLMs.
paper_title: MEDSYN: Benchmarking Multi-EviDence SYNthesis in Complex Clinical Cases for Multimodal Large Language Models

description: Ophthalmic benchmark for evaluating MLLMs with fundus photographs and OCT images. Addresses limitations in existing benchmarks through rigorous quality control and expert annotation, focusing on real-world clinical practice complexities in ophthalmology.
paper_title: A Novel Ophthalmic Benchmark for Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models with Fundus Photographs and OCT Images

description: A comprehensive medical benchmark covering gastrointestinal endoscopy workflow evaluation with 20 fine-grained lesion categories. Uniquely evaluates models across five clinical stages (anatomical localization, lesion identification, diagnosis, description, and management) comparing against human endoscopists.
paper_title: GI-Bench: A Panoramic Benchmark Revealing the Knowledge-Experience Dissociation of Multimodal Large Language Models in Gastrointestinal Endoscopy Against Clinical Standards

description: Comprehensive benchmark for ophthalmic surgery analysis grounded in structured clinical knowledge. Features over 210k VQAs covering fine-grained attributes for perception, medical knowledge graph for comprehension, and clinical reasoning tasks.
paper_title: EyePCR: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Fine-Grained Perception, Knowledge Comprehension and Clinical Reasoning in Ophthalmic Surgery

description: First benchmark leveraging clinician gaze as Cognitive Cursor for egocentric clinical intent understanding. Evaluates spatial, temporal, and standard intent through Three-Dimensional Clinical Intent Framework with Trap QA mechanisms.
paper_title: Benchmarking Egocentric Clinical Intent Understanding Capability for Medical Multimodal Large Language Models

description: Large-scale multimodal ophthalmology benchmark with multi-granular annotations across 12 common conditions and 5 imaging modalities. Supports anatomical recognition, disease screening/staging, and demographic prediction for bias evaluation.
paper_title: LMOD+: A Comprehensive Multimodal Dataset and Benchmark for Developing and Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models in Ophthalmology

description: MicroVQA assesses three research-level reasoning capabilities in microscopy: expert image understanding, hypothesis generation, and experiment proposal. It uniquely targets research-level scientific reasoning rather than college-level difficulty, with biology expert curation across diverse microscopy modalities.
paper_title: MicroVQA: A Multimodal Reasoning Benchmark for Microscopy-Based Scientific Research

description: MedBookVQA systematically derives medical questions from open-access textbooks with automated figure extraction and contextual alignment. It provides hierarchical taxonomies across 42 imaging modalities, 125 anatomical structures, and 31 clinical specialties, enabling nuanced analysis across medical subdomains.
paper_title: MedBookVQA: A Systematic and Comprehensive Medical Benchmark Derived from Open-Access Book

description: MedSPOT is a workflow-aware sequential grounding benchmark for clinical GUI environments with 216 task-driven videos and 597 annotated keyframes. Unlike existing benchmarks treating grounding as standalone tasks, it models procedural interaction as sequences of structured spatial decisions within realistic medical workflows.
paper_title: MedSPOT: A Workflow-Aware Sequential Grounding Benchmark for Clinical GUI

description: A specialized ophthalmology benchmark designed to evaluate hallucinations in medical MLLMs. Categorizes hallucinations into Visual Understanding and Logical Composition classes, addressing the specific challenges of ophthalmic diagnosis including limited knowledge, insufficient visual localization, and reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: EH-Benchmark Ophthalmic Hallucination Benchmark and Agent-Driven Top-Down Traceable Reasoning Workflow

description: A comprehensive medical MLLM benchmark spanning 15 medical specialties with stratification into 3 main categories and 8 sub-categories of clinical tasks. Ensures evaluation across distinct specialties and different diagnostic capacities including perception and disease analysis while avoiding overlap with existing VQA datasets.
paper_title: A Spectrum Evaluation Benchmark for Medical Multi-Modal Large Language Models

description: SurgMLLMBench unifies multimodal evaluation for surgical scene understanding across laparoscopic, robot-assisted, and micro-surgical domains under a unified taxonomy. It uniquely integrates pixel-level instrument segmentation masks with structured VQA annotations, enabling comprehensive evaluation beyond traditional VQA tasks and supporting richer visual-conversational interactions.
paper_title: SurgMLLMBench: A Multimodal Large Language Model Benchmark Dataset for Surgical Scene Understanding

description: MedHallBench is a comprehensive benchmark framework for evaluating and mitigating hallucinations specifically in Medical Large Language Models. It integrates expert-validated medical case scenarios with established medical databases and employs automated ACHMI scoring combined with clinical expert evaluations.
paper_title: MedHallBench: A New Benchmark for Assessing Hallucination in Medical Large Language Models

description: SurgWound-Bench is the first benchmark for surgical wound diagnosis featuring 697 surgical wound images annotated by professional surgeons with eight fine-grained clinical attributes. It includes VQA and report generation tasks to evaluate comprehensive surgical wound assessment capabilities.
paper_title: SurgWound-Bench: A Benchmark for Surgical Wound Diagnosis

description: DermBench is a meticulously curated benchmark pairing 4,000 real-world dermatology images with expert-certified diagnostic narratives. It uses an LLM-based judge to score candidate narratives across clinically grounded dimensions, enabling consistent evaluation of dermatology diagnostic capabilities in multimodal models.
paper_title: Towards Trustworthy Dermatology MLLMs: A Benchmark and Multimodal Evaluator for Diagnostic Narratives

description: Fine-grained Medical Complex Multimodal Reasoning benchmark that systematically decomposes medical reasoning into visual understanding (small-object detection, fine-detail discrimination, spatial understanding) and multi-step reasoning across four clinical scenarios. Covers 11 organ systems and 12 imaging modalities with rigorous clinical validation.
paper_title: Med-CMR: A Fine-Grained Benchmark Integrating Visual Evidence and Clinical Logic for Medical Complex Multimodal Reasoning

description: First benchmark for multi-image medical VQA testing comparative reasoning through educationally-validated diagnostic sequences. Uses narrative transcripts from medical education videos to create high-quality multi-image VQA pairs with explicit, transcript-grounded reasoning chains.
paper_title: MedFrameQA: A Multi-Image Medical VQA Benchmark for Clinical Reasoning

description: Agentic benchmark simulating Molecular Tumor Board decision-making through multimodal, longitudinal oncology questions. Tests sequential clinical decision-making in multi-agent environments requiring integration of heterogeneous data and evolving insights over time with clinician-validated annotations.
paper_title: MTBBench: A Multimodal Sequential Clinical Decision-Making Benchmark in Oncology

description: PMC-MI-Bench is constructed for composite understanding of medical compound figures from biomedical literature, manually validated by medical experts. It uniquely focuses on multi-image analysis capabilities in medical contexts, enabling MLLMs to synthesize information across multiple images from different modalities or time points for medical diagnosis.
paper_title: From Compound Figures to Composite Understanding: Developing a Multi-Modal LLM from Biomedical Literature with Medical Multiple-Image Benchmarking and Validation

description: CrossMed is designed to evaluate compositional generalization in medical multimodal LLMs using a structured Modality-Anatomy-Task (MAT) schema. It reformulates four public medical datasets into a unified VQA format with 20,200 multiple-choice QA instances, uniquely testing generalization across unseen combinations of imaging modality, anatomy, and task type.
paper_title: CrossMed: A Multimodal Cross-Task Benchmark for Compositional Generalization in Medical Imaging

description: SurgBench is a unified surgical video benchmarking framework with SurgBench-P (53 million frames across 22 surgical procedures) for pretraining and SurgBench-E for evaluation across six categories spanning 72 fine-grained tasks. It provides extensive coverage of diverse surgical scenarios for developing surgical video foundation models.
paper_title: SurgBench: A Unified Large-Scale Benchmark for Surgical Video Analysis

description: The first benchmark for Multi-lingual, Multi-modal, and Multi-hop reasoning in medical instructional video understanding. Features a novel multi-hop reasoning task requiring models to locate key entities in text, find corresponding visual evidence in video, and synthesize cross-modal information. Includes two tasks: TAGSV (single video) and TAGVC (video corpus).
paper_title: M$^3$-Med: A Benchmark for Multi-lingual, Multi-modal, and Multi-hop Reasoning in Medical Instructional Video Understanding

description: PediatricsMQA addresses age bias in medical AI with 3,417 text-based and 2,067 vision-based MCQs covering 131 pediatric topics across seven developmental stages. It uses 634 pediatric images from 67 imaging modalities, specifically designed to evaluate pediatric medical understanding where current models show dramatic performance drops.
paper_title: PediatricsMQA: a Multi-modal Pediatrics Question Answering Benchmark

description: CT-Bench is the first comprehensive benchmark for multimodal lesion understanding in computed tomography, comprising 20,335 lesions from 7,795 CT studies with detailed annotations and 2,850 VQA pairs. It covers lesion localization, description, size estimation, and attribute categorization with hard negative examples reflecting real diagnostic challenges.
paper_title: CT-Bench: A Benchmark for Multimodal Lesion Understanding in Computed Tomography

description: PathVG introduces Pathology Visual Grounding as a new benchmark task for detecting regions based on pathological expressions with different attributes. The RefPath dataset contains 27,610 images with 33,500 language-grounded boxes, presenting multi-scale pathological images with expressions requiring specialized pathological knowledge.
paper_title: PathVG: A New Benchmark and Dataset for Pathology Visual Grounding

description: Rad-ReStruct provides fine-grained, hierarchically ordered annotations for structured radiology reporting on X-Ray images. It models structured reporting as hierarchical VQA and is the first public benchmark for evaluating automated structured radiology reporting methods.
paper_title: Rad-ReStruct: A Novel VQA Benchmark and Method for Structured Radiology Reporting

description: FEDMEKI addresses medical knowledge injection into foundation models under privacy constraints using federated learning. It handles multi-site, multi-modal medical data across 7 modalities and 8 medical tasks including both classification and generation tasks while preserving data privacy.
paper_title: FEDMEKI: A Benchmark for Scaling Medical Foundation Models via Federated Knowledge Injection

description: RGC provides high-quality, multi-modality radiographic dataset for medical vision-language pre-training. It enables comprehensive analysis of key factors affecting medical VLP performance and serves as both a pre-training dataset and benchmark for medical report generation and image-text retrieval.
paper_title: Multi-modal Pre-training for Medical Vision-language Understanding and Generation: An Empirical Study with A New Benchmark

description: GEMeX is the largest chest X-ray VQA dataset featuring multi-modal explainability with detailed visual and textual explanations for each QA pair. It includes four question types (open-ended, closed-ended, single-choice, multiple-choice) and groundable explanations to enhance answer comprehensibility.
paper_title: GEMeX: A Large-Scale, Groundable, and Explainable Medical VQA Benchmark for Chest X-ray Diagnosis

description: MedAgentBoard provides systematic evaluation of multi-agent collaboration, single-LLM, and conventional approaches across four diverse medical task categories including medical VQA, lay summary generation, EHR predictive modeling, and clinical workflow automation. It offers evidence-based insights for selecting appropriate AI solutions in medicine across text, medical images, and structured EHR data.
paper_title: MedAgentBoard: Benchmarking Multi-Agent Collaboration with Conventional Methods for Diverse Medical Tasks

description: CXRTrek introduces multi-stage visual question answering for chest X-ray interpretation that explicitly simulates the diagnostic reasoning process of radiologists. It covers 8 sequential diagnostic stages with over 11 million Q&A pairs, modeling dependencies between diagnostic stages and capturing reasoning patterns within radiological context.
paper_title: Interpreting Chest X-rays Like a Radiologist: A Benchmark with Clinical Reasoning

description: MMOral is the first large-scale multimodal instruction dataset and benchmark for panoramic X-ray interpretation in dentistry, consisting of 20,563 annotated images with 1.3 million instruction-following instances. MMOral-Bench provides comprehensive evaluation across five key diagnostic dimensions in dentistry, addressing the specialized challenges of dense anatomical structures and subtle pathological cues in oral radiology.
paper_title: Towards Better Dental AI: A Multimodal Benchmark and Instruction Dataset for Panoramic X-ray Analysis

description: ReXVQA is the largest comprehensive VQA benchmark for chest radiology with approximately 696,000 questions across five core radiological reasoning skills: presence assessment, location analysis, negation detection, differential diagnosis, and geometric reasoning. It introduces clinically authentic tasks that go beyond template-based queries and includes comprehensive human reader studies with radiology residents.
paper_title: ReXVQA: A Large-scale Visual Question Answering Benchmark for Generalist Chest X-ray Understanding

benchmark_name: MC-BEC (Multimodal Clinical Benchmark for Emergency Care)
description: A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating foundation models in emergency medicine using real Emergency Department visits. Features continuous monitoring data including vital signs, ECG waveforms, imaging reports, and multiple prediction tasks at different timescales.
paper_title: Multimodal Clinical Benchmark for Emergency Care (MC-BEC): A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating Foundation Models in Emergency Medicine

description: First standard benchmark for multimodal medical retrieval spanning 5 domains and 16 medical fields. Designed to evaluate retrieval models for healthcare applications with both text and image information.
paper_title: M3Retrieve: Benchmarking Multimodal Retrieval for Medicine

description: RADAR is a multimodal benchmark for 3D image-based radiology report review that pairs 3D medical images with preliminary reports and candidate edits. It defines a structured discrepancy assessment task requiring models to evaluate proposed edits through image-level agreement, clinical severity assessment, and edit type classification.
paper_title: RADAR: A Multimodal Benchmark for 3D Image-Based Radiology Report Review

description: TCM-Ladder is the first comprehensive multimodal QA dataset specifically designed for evaluating Traditional Chinese Medicine language models. It covers multiple core TCM disciplines with over 52,000 questions incorporating various modalities including images and videos.
paper_title: TCM-Ladder: A Benchmark for Multimodal Question Answering on Traditional Chinese Medicine

description: MedMASLab is a unified framework and benchmarking platform for multimodal medical multi-agent systems that introduces standardized multimodal agent communication protocol and automated clinical reasoning evaluator. It provides the most extensive benchmark spanning 11 organ systems and 473 diseases across 24 medical modalities.
paper_title: MedMASLab: A Unified Orchestration Framework for Benchmarking Multimodal Medical Multi-Agent Systems

description: MM-NeuroOnco-Bench is a large-scale multimodal benchmark and instruction dataset for MRI-based brain tumor diagnosis consisting of 24,726 MRI slices with approximately 200,000 semantically enriched multimodal instructions. It includes a manually annotated evaluation benchmark with rejection-aware setting to reduce biases.
paper_title: MM-NeuroOnco: A Multimodal Benchmark and Instruction Dataset for MRI-Based Brain Tumor Diagnosis

description: Comprehensive benchmark evaluating performance, fairness, and interpretability of foundation models using publicly available MIMIC-IV database. Develops standardized data processing pipeline that harmonizes heterogeneous clinical records into analysis-ready format.
paper_title: Benchmarking Foundation Models with Multimodal Public Electronic Health Records

description: LiveClin is a live benchmark built from contemporary, peer-reviewed case reports and updated biannually to ensure clinical currency and resist data contamination. It transforms authentic patient cases into complex, multimodal evaluation scenarios spanning the entire clinical pathway using a verified AI-human workflow.
paper_title: LiveClin: A Live Clinical Benchmark without Leakage

description: CareBench systematically benchmarks multimodal fusion between Electronic Health Records and chest X-rays on standardized cohorts from MIMIC-IV and MIMIC-CXR. It evaluates when multimodal fusion helps, how different strategies compare, robustness to missing modalities, and algorithmic fairness in clinical prediction.
paper_title: When Does Multimodal Learning Help in Healthcare? A Benchmark on EHR and Chest X-Ray Fusion

description: MedXpertQA is a highly challenging benchmark for expert-level medical knowledge and reasoning featuring 4,460 questions spanning 17 specialties and 11 body systems. It includes both text and multimodal subsets with expert-level exam questions containing diverse clinical images and rich patient information, going beyond simple QA pairs.
paper_title: MedXpertQA: Benchmarking Expert-Level Medical Reasoning and Understanding

description: First comprehensive multimodal federated learning benchmark for healthcare with 2-4 modalities including 10 unique medical modalities. Evaluates segmentation, classification, modality alignment, and VQA tasks across federated settings.
paper_title: Med-MMFL: A Multimodal Federated Learning Benchmark in Healthcare

description: First multimodal benchmark constructed from ten Japanese national healthcare licensing exams spanning Medicine, Dentistry, Nursing, Pharmacy, and allied health. Incorporates clinical images and expert-annotated rationales for comprehensive healthcare evaluation.
paper_title: KokushiMD-10: Benchmark for Evaluating Large Language Models on Ten Japanese National Healthcare Licensing Examinations

description: Multimodal agent benchmark for evaluating LLMs in simulated clinical environments with patient interactions, multimodal data collection, and tool usage. Covers nine medical specialties and seven languages with sequential decision-making format.
paper_title: AgentClinic: a multimodal agent benchmark to evaluate AI in simulated clinical environments

description: HESCAPE is a large-scale benchmark for cross-modal contrastive pretraining in spatial transcriptomics, built on a curated pan-organ dataset spanning 6 different gene panels and 54 donors. It uniquely evaluates multimodal learning methods that leverage both histology images and gene expression data for tasks like gene mutation classification and expression prediction.
paper_title: A Large-Scale Benchmark of Cross-Modal Learning for Histology and Gene Expression in Spatial Transcriptomics

description: PathMMU is the largest expert-validated pathology benchmark for Large Multimodal Models, comprising multimodal multi-choice questions with explanations. It uniquely involves seven pathologists to scrutinize each question under strict standards, setting an expert-level performance benchmark specifically for pathology understanding and reasoning.
paper_title: PathMMU: A Massive Multimodal Expert-Level Benchmark for Understanding and Reasoning in Pathology

description: MEDMKG is a Medical Multimodal Knowledge Graph benchmark that unifies visual and textual medical information, fusing MIMIC-CXR data with UMLS knowledge. It uniquely evaluates multimodal knowledge integration in medical AI across three tasks and provides a foundation for developing adaptive strategies for clinical applications.
paper_title: MEDMKG: Benchmarking Medical Knowledge Exploitation with Multimodal Knowledge Graph

description: MITI Dataset provides a benchmark for evaluating stereoscopic visual-inertial computer vision algorithms (SLAM/SfM/3D Reconstruction/VIO) specifically for minimally invasive surgical interventions in the abdomen. It uniquely offers multimodal sensor data including IMU, stereoscopic video, and IR tracking ground truth from actual handheld surgical procedures.
paper_title: MITI: SLAM Benchmark for Laparoscopic Surgery

description: CoRe-BT is a cross-modal radiology-pathology-text benchmark for brain tumor typing designed to study robust multimodal learning under missing modality conditions. It uniquely integrates multi-sequence brain MRI, H&E-stained pathology images, and pathology reports with expert annotations for tumor masks and clinically relevant tumor classifications.
paper_title: CoRe-BT: A Multimodal Radiology-Pathology-Text Benchmark for Robust Brain Tumor Typing

description: This introduces an event-based evaluation framework for multimodal time-series summarization using the TIHM-1.5 dementia monitoring dataset. It uniquely measures clinical event fidelity through abnormality recall, duration recall, and hallucinated event detection rather than traditional semantic similarity metrics for remote therapeutic monitoring.
paper_title: Benchmarking LLM Summaries of Multimodal Clinical Time Series for Remote Monitoring

description: Presents a nationwide, cloud-based benchmarking infrastructure with over 700,000 expert-curated tasks spanning 24 primary and 91 secondary medical specialties. Includes dedicated tracks for LLMs, multimodal models, and agents with multi-stage refinement by clinicians from 500+ institutions.
paper_title: MedBench v4: A Robust and Scalable Benchmark for Evaluating Chinese Medical Language Models, Multimodal Models, and Intelligent Agents

description: Proposes a new evaluation benchmark with OCT/SLO classification and segmentation tasks for retinal image analysis. Designed to validate multimodal foundation models for ophthalmology across different imaging modalities.
paper_title: MIRAGE: Multimodal foundation model and benchmark for comprehensive retinal OCT image analysis

description: Presents a multimodal benchmark dataset of 1794 patients with corresponding EHR data and high-resolution CT scans labeled for pulmonary embolism. Evaluates fairness properties across protected subgroups and integrates imaging with EHR data.
paper_title: RadFusion: Benchmarking Performance and Fairness for Multimodal Pulmonary Embolism Detection from CT and EHR

description: Multilingual, multimodal medical examination dataset with 568 labeled multiple-choice QAs paired with 568 medical images from four countries (Brazil, Israel, Japan, Spain). Covers original languages and validated English translations by native clinicians.
paper_title: WorldMedQA-V: a multilingual, multimodal medical examination dataset for multimodal language models evaluation

description: PathGLS introduces a novel reference-free evaluation framework for pathology Vision-Language Models that assesses models across three dimensions: Grounding, Logic, and Stability without requiring ground truth annotations. It uniquely evaluates both patch-level and whole-slide image analysis while providing a comprehensive trust score for pathology VLMs.
paper_title: PathGLS: Evaluating Pathology Vision-Language Models without Ground Truth through Multi-Dimensional Consistency

description: SURE-VQA introduces a systematic framework for evaluating VLM robustness in medical VQA tasks, focusing on real-world distribution shifts with LLM-based semantic evaluation. It uniquely provides interpretability through sanity baselines and analyzes multimodal impact on VLM performance.
paper_title: SURE-VQA: Systematic Understanding of Robustness Evaluation in Medical VQA Tasks

description: SurgGoal introduces a goal-oriented evaluation of surgical planning via phase-goal satisfiability using expert-defined surgical rules. It provides a multicentric meta-evaluation benchmark with valid procedural variations and invalid plans containing order and content errors.
paper_title: SurgGoal: Rethinking Surgical Planning Evaluation via Goal-Satisfiability

description: Presents a clinically grounded framework for chest X-ray VQA that probes chain-of-thought faithfulness via controlled text and image modifications across three axes: clinical fidelity, causal attribution, and confidence calibration. It uniquely evaluates reasoning faithfulness rather than just answer accuracy.
paper_title: Evaluating Reasoning Faithfulness in Medical Vision-Language Models using Multimodal Perturbations

description: EndoBench is the first comprehensive benchmark for assessing MLLMs across the full spectrum of endoscopic practice with 4 endoscopic scenarios, 12 specialized clinical tasks with 12 secondary subtasks, and 5 levels of visual prompting granularities, resulting in 6,832 validated VQA pairs. It mirrors clinical workflow spanning anatomical recognition, lesion analysis, spatial localization, and surgical operations.
paper_title: EndoBench: A Comprehensive Evaluation of Multi-Modal Large Language Models for Endoscopy Analysis

description: First comprehensive evaluation of MLLMs for Adolescent Idiopathic Scoliosis self-management using a 'Divide and Conquer' framework with visual QA, domain knowledge, and patient education tasks.
paper_title: Adapting and Evaluating Multimodal Large Language Models for Adolescent Idiopathic Scoliosis Self-Management: A Divide and Conquer Framework

description: Establishes a perception-reasoning paradigm for language-based medical image quality assessment using MLLMs. Features MedQ-Perception for low-level visual attributes and MedQ-Reasoning for no-reference and comparison tasks, spanning five imaging modalities with authentic clinical data, physics-based degradations, and AI-generated images.
paper_title: MedQ-Bench: Evaluating and Exploring Medical Image Quality Assessment Abilities in MLLMs

description: First benchmark for analyzing changes in patients' conditions between clinical visits using temporal medical images. Features three tasks (VQA, report generation, image-pair selection) with over 17,000 instances to challenge LVLMs on temporal medical reasoning.
paper_title: TemMed-Bench: Evaluating Temporal Medical Image Reasoning in Vision-Language Models

description: Introduces MIDI-B, a standardized benchmark for evaluating medical image de-identification tools based on HIPAA Safe Harbor regulation and DICOM standards. The challenge employed real de-identified radiology images with synthetic PHI/PII inserted across multiple centers and modalities.
paper_title: Medical Image De-Identification Benchmark Challenge

description: Introduces LMOD, a large-scale multimodal ophthalmology benchmark across five ophthalmic imaging modalities with primary ophthalmology-specific applications. The benchmark reveals significant performance drops for LVLMs in ophthalmology and identifies six major failure modes.
paper_title: LMOD: A Large Multimodal Ophthalmology Dataset and Benchmark for Large Vision-Language Models

description: Introduces MedHEval, a novel benchmark that systematically evaluates hallucinations and mitigation strategies in Medical LVLMs by categorizing them into three underlying causes: visual misinterpretation, knowledge deficiency, and context misalignment. The benchmark establishes a standardized framework for evaluating medical hallucinations.
paper_title: MedHEval: Benchmarking Hallucinations and Mitigation Strategies in Medical Large Vision-Language Models

num_samples: nine datasets across four medical tasks
description: Introduces BenchX, a unified benchmark framework for Medical Vision-Language Pretraining on chest X-rays. The framework enables head-to-head comparison between MedVLP methods with comprehensive datasets, standardized preprocessing, and unified finetuning protocols.
paper_title: BenchX: A Unified Benchmark Framework for Medical Vision-Language Pretraining on Chest X-Rays

description: First benchmark specifically designed for evaluating knowledge editing in clinical multimodal tasks, combining medical Visual Question Answering with knowledge editing. Provides a three-dimensional metric suite (reliability, generality, locality) and supports both understanding and reasoning task types in medical scenarios.
paper_title: MultiMedEdit: A Scenario-Aware Benchmark for Evaluating Knowledge Editing in Medical VQA

description: Comprehensive multimodal dataset for evaluating diagnostic capabilities in electrocardiogram interpretation with 12 essential diagnostic tasks. Features multi-turn dialogue support for conversational medical AI and combines real-world clinical ECG data with synthetic cases.
paper_title: ECG-Expert-QA: A Benchmark for Evaluating Medical Large Language Models in Heart Disease Diagnosis

description: HEAL-MedVQA is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating medical LMMs' localization abilities and hallucination robustness. It features 67K VQA pairs with doctor-annotated anatomical segmentation masks and two innovative evaluation protocols to assess visual and textual shortcut learning in medical contexts.
paper_title: Localizing Before Answering: A Hallucination Evaluation Benchmark for Grounded Medical Multimodal LLMs

description: First large-scale benchmark for cataract surgery planning incorporating structured clinical reasoning. Each case annotated with diagnostic questions, expert reasoning chains, and structured surgical recommendations using knowledge-driven Multi-Agent System.
paper_title: CataractSurg-80K: Knowledge-Driven Benchmarking for Structured Reasoning in Ophthalmic Surgery Planning

description: Open-PMC-18M is a large-scale high-fidelity biomedical dataset with 18 million image-text pairs spanning radiology, microscopy, and visible light photography. It integrates transformer-based subfigure detection and contextual text enrichment for biomedical representation learning.
paper_title: Open-PMC-18M: A High-Fidelity Large Scale Medical Dataset for Multimodal Representation Learning

description: First comprehensive framework for region-specific captioning in medical images with customized assessment protocol, data pre-processing pipeline, and specialized QA template library. Evaluates clinical factuality through attribute-level verification tasks across VinDr-CXR, LIDC-IDRI, and SkinCon datasets.
paper_title: Describe Anything in Medical Images

description: First multimodal benchmark for clinical visual reasoning with three modules: Visual Evidence Comprehension, Reasoning Trajectory Assessment, and Report Generation Evaluation. Covers 20 task types, 17 diagnostic categories, and five imaging modalities reflecting clinical reasoning workflow.
paper_title: DrVD-Bench: Do Vision-Language Models Reason Like Human Doctors in Medical Image Diagnosis?

description: Comprises six public benchmarks and six multi-center external validation datasets from five institutions for surgical scene understanding. Features largest surgical chain-of-thought dataset with 320K reasoning pairs and hierarchical reasoning evaluation across instrument localization, triplet recognition, phase recognition, action recognition, and critical view assessment.
paper_title: Surg-R1: A Hierarchical Reasoning Foundation Model for Scalable and Interpretable Surgical Decision Support with Multi-Center Clinical Validation

description: A large-scale benchmark of 531,850 video-instruction pairs across 8 medical sources spanning video, segment, and frame-level tasks. Curated through rigorous quality assurance with expert-guided prompting for medical video understanding evaluation.
paper_title: MedGRPO: Multi-Task Reinforcement Learning for Heterogeneous Medical Video Understanding

description: Med-HallMark is the first benchmark specifically designed for hallucination detection and evaluation within the medical multimodal domain. It provides multi-tasking hallucination support, multifaceted hallucination data, and hierarchical hallucination categorization, along with the MediHall Score metric that assesses LVLMs' hallucinations through a hierarchical scoring system considering severity and type of hallucination for clinical impact assessment.
paper_title: Detecting and Evaluating Medical Hallucinations in Large Vision Language Models

description: MediMeta-C is a corruption benchmark that systematically applies perturbations across multiple medical imaging datasets to evaluate MVLM robustness. Combined with MedMNIST-C, it establishes a comprehensive framework for assessing medical vision-language model performance under real-world distortions and noise.
paper_title: On the Robustness of Medical Vision-Language Models: Are they Truly Generalizable?

description: Introduces a Vietnamese chest X-ray dataset comprising 5,400 images with expert-written findings and impressions from physicians for evaluating vision-language models in Vietnamese medical contexts. Addresses the gap in Vietnamese medical data exposure for VLMs and establishes a benchmark for Vietnamese clinical domain evaluation.
paper_title: ViX-Ray: A Vietnamese Chest X-Ray Dataset for Vision-Language Models

description: Constructs a long-context benchmark derived from peer-reviewed case reports containing 26,030 multi-modal image-text pairs and 6,354 clinically challenging cases for evaluating diagnostic reasoning for rare skin diseases. Each case includes comprehensive clinical information and step-by-step reasoning chains with DermLIP-based similarity metrics.
paper_title: Mind the Rarities: Can Rare Skin Diseases Be Reliably Diagnosed via Diagnostic Reasoning?

description: Presents a custom clinical dataset for evaluating lightweight agentic multimodal frameworks in video-based scene understanding for healthcare robotics. Tests VLMs' ability to perform temporal reasoning, uncertainty estimation, and generate structured outputs needed for robotic planning in clinical environments.
paper_title: Lightweight Structured Multimodal Reasoning for Clinical Scene Understanding in Robotics

description: MediConfusion is a challenging medical VQA benchmark that probes failure modes of medical MLLMs using visually dissimilar image pairs that are clearly distinct for medical experts but confuse models. It uniquely exposes systematic vulnerabilities in medical AI systems.
paper_title: MediConfusion: Can you trust your AI radiologist? Probing the reliability of multimodal medical foundation models

description: ClinVideoSuite is an evidence-centric, fine-grained clinical video suite designed for clinical reasoning tasks. It provides supervision for iteratively locating, verifying, and justifying predictions with temporally targeted evidence in medical procedures.
paper_title: MedScope: Incentivizing "Think with Videos" for Clinical Reasoning via Coarse-to-Fine Tool Calling

description: A colonoscopic video dataset for video question answering in the surgical domain, focused on motion-related questions and diagnostic attributes, as well as out-of-template questions to assess model robustness. It enables temporally-grounded surgical scene understanding with dynamic procedural contexts.
paper_title: SurgViVQA: Temporally-Grounded Video Question Answering for Surgical Scene Understanding

description: A colonoscopy VideoQA dataset with 6,424 clip-question pairs, including paired rephrased Out-of-Template questions to evaluate sensitivity to linguistic variation and robustness in surgical video understanding. It focuses on temporal grounding while testing resilience to natural variation in clinical question phrasing.
paper_title: TemporalDoRA: Temporal PEFT for Robust Surgical Video Question Answering

description: ColonINST provides a large-scale multimodal instruction tuning dataset for colonoscopy along with ColonGPT model and multimodal benchmark. This enables multimodal medical AI applications specifically for colorectal cancer screening and colonoscopy understanding.
paper_title: Frontiers in Intelligent Colonoscopy

description: A multimodal benchmark that incorporates captioning and VQA tasks to assess capabilities for gigapixel whole-slide pathology images in varied clinical settings such as microscopy and diagnosis. Accompanied by SlideInstruction, the largest instruction-following dataset for WSIs consisting of 4.2K WSI captions and 176K VQA pairs.
paper_title: SlideChat: A Large Vision-Language Assistant for Whole-Slide Pathology Image Understanding

description: A pioneer multimodal benchmark comprising comprehensive topology-centric tasks for medical vessel-like anatomy. The benchmark specifically focuses on topology-aware perception and understanding of vessel structures across different medical imaging modalities.
paper_title: TubeMLLM: A Foundation Model for Topology Knowledge Exploration in Vessel-like Anatomy

description: First large-scale dataset of expert-annotated medical images with structured visual Chain-of-Thought reasoning, featuring bounding boxes and explicit linking between visual regions and reasoning steps. Supports 16 languages with over 700k VQA pairs total.
paper_title: S-Chain: Structured Visual Chain-of-Thought For Medicine

description: PhenoBench is an expert-verified benchmark for phenotype recognition in medical imaging, comprising over 7,800 image-caption pairs covering more than 1,000 medical phenotypes. It evaluates VLMs' ability to recognize and understand structured medical phenotype knowledge.
paper_title: PhenoLIP: Integrating Phenotype Ontology Knowledge into Medical Vision-Language Pretraining

description: Introduces MEDOPENCLAW runtime and MEDFLOWBENCH, a full-study medical imaging benchmark covering multi-sequence brain MRI and lung CT/PET with viewer-only, tool-use, and open-method tracks for evaluating medical agentic capabilities.
paper_title: MedOpenClaw: Auditable Medical Imaging Agents Reasoning over Uncurated Full Studies

description: Med-Zero-17K is a curated dataset for pure RL-based training in medical VQA, encompassing over 30 medical image modalities and 24 clinical tasks. It enables training of medical VLMs through reinforcement learning without requiring ground-truth labels during adaptation.
paper_title: CAPO: Reinforcing Consistent Reasoning in Medical Decision-Making

description: OPG-Bench introduces a structured-report protocol based on (Location, Field, Value) triples derived from real clinical reports for comprehensive dental panoramic X-ray interpretation. It enables evaluation of findings and hallucinations beyond VQA limitations, extending structured reporting capabilities.
paper_title: OPGAgent: An Agent for Auditable Dental Panoramic X-ray Interpretation

description: Evaluates few-shot object detection in optical microscopy images across 11 cell types. Tests in-context learning capabilities of VLMs for biomedical applications where large annotated datasets are unavailable.
paper_title: In-Context Adaptation of VLMs for Few-Shot Cell Detection in Optical Microscopy

description: A benchmark that comprehensively tests VLMs for vulnerabilities against specific modes of Medical Image Spatial Grounding. Tests spatial grounding of anatomical structures in 3D medical images.
paper_title: Medical Image Spatial Grounding with Semantic Sampling

description: A benchmark with realistic synthetic corruptions designed to mimic real-world distribution shifts in digital histopathology, including staining, contamination, blurring, and noise. It dynamically applies corruptions to evaluate Test-Time Adaptation mechanisms for medical VLMs.
paper_title: Histopath-C: Towards Realistic Domain Shifts for Histopathology Vision-Language Adaptation

description: MedVistaGym is a scalable training environment for tool-integrated medical image reasoning that incentivizes VLMs to determine when and which tools to invoke for medical analysis. It provides an interactive framework for training models to localize task-relevant regions and integrate evidence through iterative visual interaction in medical contexts.
paper_title: MEDVISTAGYM: A Scalable Training Environment for Thinking with Medical Images via Tool-Integrated Reinforcement Learning

description: PET2Rep is the first dedicated dataset for PET radiology report generation, uniquely capturing whole-body image-report pairs with metabolic information covering dozens of organs. It addresses the gap in existing benchmarks by focusing on molecular PET imaging rather than structural imaging modalities, with specialized clinical efficacy metrics.
paper_title: PET2Rep: Towards Vision-Language Model-Drived Automated Radiology Report Generation for Positron Emission Tomography

description: MIRP systematically evaluates VLMs' capability to identify relative positions of anatomical structures in medical images, a fundamental prerequisite for clinical applications. It reveals that current VLMs fail at this basic spatial reasoning task in medical contexts, often relying on anatomical knowledge rather than actual image content.
paper_title: Your other Left! Vision-Language Models Fail to Identify Relative Positions in Medical Images

description: End-to-end vulnerability scoring framework for medical VLMs that synthesizes over 30,000 adversarial variants from 5,000 radiology images. Features sophisticated attack templates, imperceptible visual perturbations, and eight-dimensional security evaluation rubric.
paper_title: VSF-Med:A Vulnerability Scoring Framework for Medical Vision-Language Models

description: Dataset of 1M image-caption pairs enriched with context-aware descriptions from full-text biomedical articles. Enables long-context modeling for biomedical VLMs with captions extending up to 512 tokens, reducing token waste significantly.
paper_title: No Tokens Wasted: Leveraging Long Context in Biomedical Vision-Language Models

description: MedObvious is a 1,880-task benchmark that isolates input validation as a set-level consistency capability over small multi-panel medical image sets, requiring models to identify whether any panel violates expected coherence. It spans five progressive tiers from basic orientation/modality mismatches to clinically motivated anatomy/viewpoint verification and triage-style cues.
paper_title: MedObvious: Exposing the Medical Moravec's Paradox in VLMs via Clinical Triage
authors: L D M S S Teja

description: A surgical video dataset created through a deterministic generation pipeline with temporal and spatial continuity filtering. Comprises video clips densely extended with fine-grained spatial-temporal question-answer samples for evaluating VLMs' understanding of complex surgical procedures.
paper_title: An Approach to Enriching Surgical Video Datasets for Fine-Grained Spatial-Temporal Understanding of Vision-Language Models

description: DermaVQA-DAS extends DermaVQA with the Dermatology Assessment Schema (DAS), supporting closed-ended question answering and lesion segmentation in patient-generated dermatology images. DAS provides 36 high-level and 27 fine-grained assessment questions in a structured framework.
paper_title: DermaVQA-DAS: Dermatology Assessment Schema (DAS) & Datasets for Closed-Ended Question Answering & Segmentation in Patient-Generated Dermatology Images

description: 3D-RAD advances 3D Med-VQA using radiology CT scans across six diverse tasks including temporal analysis. It introduces complex reasoning challenges with computational tasks and multi-stage temporal analysis, supporting both open- and closed-ended questions.
paper_title: 3D-RAD: A Comprehensive 3D Radiology Med-VQA Dataset with Multi-Temporal Analysis and Diverse Diagnostic Tasks

description: CorBenchX provides a comprehensive suite for automated error detection and correction in chest X-ray reports. It synthesizes error reports by injecting clinically common errors, with each paired with original text, error type, and human-readable descriptions.
paper_title: CorBenchX: Large-Scale Chest X-Ray Error Dataset and Vision-Language Model Benchmark for Report Error Correction

description: Introduces a 3D vision-language foundation model for abdominal CT interpretation with comprehensive evaluation across 752 individual tasks covering diagnostic, prognostic and quality-related assessments. Tests both zero-shot capabilities and model-adapted tasks across multiple medical institutions.
paper_title: Merlin: A Computed Tomography Vision-Language Foundation Model and Dataset

description: MUDI is the first large-scale multimodal biomedical dataset for understanding pharmacodynamic drug-drug interactions, combining pharmacological text, chemical formulas, molecular structure graphs, and images. It specifically addresses the limitation of existing DDI datasets that focus only on textual information by providing comprehensive multimodal drug representations.
paper_title: MUDI: A Multimodal Biomedical Dataset for Understanding Pharmacodynamic Drug-Drug Interactions

description: MM-OR provides the first realistic large-scale multimodal operating room dataset that enables multimodal scene graph generation. It uniquely captures comprehensive OR scenes with RGB-D data, audio, speech transcripts, robotic logs, and tracking data, establishing a new benchmark for holistic surgical environment understanding.
paper_title: MM-OR: A Large Multimodal Operating Room Dataset for Semantic Understanding of High-Intensity Surgical Environments

description: MECO addresses the underexplored area of multimodal emotion prediction in aging populations, providing 38 hours of synchronized multimodal signals from older adults. It uniquely combines emotional and cognitive annotations including MMSE cognitive scores, facilitating research on affect-cognition relationships and early MCI detection in real-world settings.
paper_title: MECO: A Multimodal Dataset for Emotion and Cognitive Understanding in Older Adults

description: EHWGesture is a multimodal video dataset for clinical gesture understanding featuring five clinically relevant gestures with over 1,100 recordings from 25 healthy subjects using RGB-Depth cameras and event cameras. It embeds action quality assessment within gesture understanding by organizing recordings in execution speed classes that mirror clinical evaluations of hand dexterity.
paper_title: EHWGesture -- A dataset for multimodal understanding of clinical gestures

description: INSPECT contains de-identified longitudinal records from 19,402 patients at risk for pulmonary embolism, including CT images, radiology report impressions, and structured EHR data (demographics, diagnoses, procedures, vitals, medications). It's the largest multimodal dataset integrating 3D medical imaging and EHR for reproducible PE-related task evaluation.
paper_title: INSPECT: A Multimodal Dataset for Pulmonary Embolism Diagnosis and Prognosis

description: This dataset provides curated multimodal data specifically focused on ameloblastoma, integrating annotated radiological, histopathological, and intraoral clinical images with structured case report data. It enables AI-based classification of ameloblastoma variants and assessment of behavioral patterns like recurrence risk.
paper_title: A Unified Multimodal Framework for Dataset Construction and Model-Based Diagnosis of Ameloblastoma

description: CogPic provides a comprehensive multimodal benchmark for early cognitive impairment detection with strictly synchronized audio, visual, and linguistic data from picture description tasks. It includes expert clinical evaluations for reliable diagnostic ground truth and represents the largest, most modality-rich dataset for cognitive assessment.
paper_title: CogPic: A Multimodal Dataset for Early Cognitive Impairment Assessment via Picture Description Tasks

description: EMPD is the first benchmark dataset specifically designed for non-contact physiological sensing via event cameras, using a laser-assisted acquisition system to detect pulse waves. It provides microsecond-level temporal precision for neuromorphic physiological monitoring with synchronized multimodal data.
paper_title: EMPD: An Event-based Multimodal Physiological Dataset for Remote Pulse Wave Detection

description: MMASD provides a privacy-preserving multimodal benchmark for autism intervention analysis with data from play therapy sessions. It includes privacy-preserving modalities like optical flow and 2D/3D skeletons along with clinical ASD evaluation scores, enabling autism studies while protecting privacy.
paper_title: MMASD: A Multimodal Dataset for Autism Intervention Analysis

description: First publicly available volume-level multimodal dataset combining MRI volumes with rich textual annotations for brain tumor segmentation. Derived from BraTS2020 benchmark, enabling text-guided volumetric medical image segmentation.
paper_title: TextBraTS: Text-Guided Volumetric Brain Tumor Segmentation with Innovative Dataset Development and Fusion Module Exploration

description: Publicly available multimodal dataset for head and neck cancer research with 1123 annotated PET/CT studies from 10 international centers. Reflects real-world clinical diversity with comprehensive metadata including survival outcomes and treatment information.
paper_title: A Multimodal and Multi-centric Head and Neck Cancer Dataset for Segmentation, Diagnosis and Outcome Prediction

description: First comprehensive multi-centre, multi-resolution ulcerative colitis endoscopy dataset with expert-validated MES and UCEIS labels plus detailed clinical descriptions. Combines dual scoring metrics for classification with expert-generated captions for image captioning, addressing variability across endoscopic systems and centres.
paper_title: A comprehensive multimodal dataset and benchmark for ulcerative colitis scoring in endoscopy

description: MEETI is the first large-scale ECG dataset synchronizing raw waveform data, high-resolution plotted images, and detailed textual interpretations. It includes beat-level quantitative parameters and supports transformer-based multimodal learning for explainable cardiovascular AI.
paper_title: MEETI: A Multimodal ECG Dataset from MIMIC-IV-ECG with Signals, Images, Features and Interpretations

description: REVEAL-CXR provides a curated benchmark of 200 chest radiographic studies with 12 benchmark labels verified by three radiologists each. It develops an AI-assisted expert labeling procedure to enable efficient radiologist annotation and establishes a holdout dataset for independent model evaluation.
paper_title: RSNA Large Language Model Benchmark Dataset for Chest Radiographs of Cardiothoracic Disease: Radiologist Evaluation and Validation Enhanced by AI Labels (REVEAL-CXR)

description: Introduces HEST-1k, a collection of spatial transcriptomic profiles linked to whole slide images for spatial transcriptomics and histology analysis. Enables identification of 2.1 million expression-morphology pairs and over 76 million nuclei for biomedical research.
paper_title: HEST-1k: A Dataset for Spatial Transcriptomics and Histology Image Analysis

description: Introduces SemiHVision dataset combining human annotations with automated augmentation for medical knowledge representation and diagnostic reasoning. Also presents JAMA Clinical Challenge benchmark for evaluating diagnostic reasoning capabilities.
paper_title: SemiHVision: Enhancing Medical Multimodal Models with a Semi-Human Annotated Dataset and Fine-Tuned Instruction Generation

description: Introduces MicroVQA++, a three-stage high-quality microscopy VQA corpus with expert-validated figure-caption pairs and HiCQA-Graph for filtering inconsistent samples. Features human-checked test split with Bloom's level hard-sample distribution.
paper_title: MicroVQA++: High-Quality Microscopy Reasoning Dataset with Weakly Supervised Graphs for Multimodal Large Language Model

description: Introduces Medical Video Classification and Medical Visual Answer Localization tasks for understanding medical instructional videos and providing visual answers to natural language questions. It focuses on cross-modal understanding between medical language and medical video for first aid and medical education applications.
paper_title: A Dataset for Medical Instructional Video Classification and Question Answering

description: The first large multimodal model for computational pathology integrating single patches, automatically segmented pathology video clips, and manually segmented videos. It provides chain-of-thought instructional pairs for diagnostic reasoning that mirrors pathologists' natural diagnostic process.
paper_title: ViDRiP-LLaVA: A Dataset and Benchmark for Diagnostic Reasoning from Pathology Videos

description: Syn-Mediverse presents the first hyper-realistic multimodal synthetic dataset of diverse healthcare facilities with over 48,000 images from simulated optical tracking cameras. It provides 1.5M annotations spanning five scene understanding tasks specifically designed for healthcare robotics applications, addressing the impracticality of obtaining annotated training data in real healthcare settings.
paper_title: Syn-Mediverse: A Multimodal Synthetic Dataset for Intelligent Scene Understanding of Healthcare Facilities

description: Curated Covid-19 dataset for multimodal depression detection that combines textual, user-specific, and image analysis from social media. The dataset specifically focuses on mental health detection during the Covid-19 pandemic using multimodal social media data.
paper_title: A Multimodal Framework for Depression Detection during Covid-19 via Harvesting Social Media: A Novel Dataset and Method

description: First large-scale multimodal dataset bridging visual tracking with textual descriptions of point status in surgical scenes. Contains 908 in vivo video clips with 754 for tissue tracking and 154 for instrument tracking, enabling context-aware tracking systems for surgical applications.
paper_title: Bridging Vision and Language for Robust Context-Aware Surgical Point Tracking: The VL-SurgPT Dataset and Benchmark

description: Benchmark dataset for multimodal biomedical image registration that correlates neuropathology with neuroimaging findings. Addresses the challenge of identifying precisely where histologic tissue sections were taken from within organs for multiscale pathology analysis.
paper_title: From Tissue Plane to Organ World: A Benchmark Dataset for Multimodal Biomedical Image Registration using Deep Co-Attention Networks

description: Benchmark dataset for 3D computer vision in colonoscopy with 10,015 frames paired with ground truth depth, surface normals, optical flow, occlusion, pose, and 3D models. Features novel multimodal 2D-3D registration technique for accurate ground truth generation in clinical colonoscopy videos.
