description: First dataset to include highly sensitive ASKAP radio telescope images with corresponding infrared images and instance-level annotations for automated detection of multi-component extended radio galaxies and their infrared hosts, featuring bounding boxes, segmentation masks, and keypoint positions.
paper_title: RadioGalaxyNET: Dataset and Novel Computer Vision Algorithms for the Detection of Extended Radio Galaxies and Infrared Hosts

description: First large-scale multimodal olfactory dataset captured 'in the wild' with paired image and chemical sensory signals from 3,500 distinct objects across indoor/outdoor environments, enabling cross-modal smell-to-image retrieval and scene/object recognition from olfactory data alone.
paper_title: New York Smells: A Large Multimodal Dataset for Olfaction

description: First multimodal and multi-view wildlife behavior monitoring dataset from 9 camera-traps in Swiss National Park, containing video with audio, 2D segmentation, and 8.5 hours of individual tracks with species and behavior labels for hierarchical animal behavior recognition and ecology-oriented benchmarks.
paper_title: MammAlps: A multi-view video behavior monitoring dataset of wild mammals in the Swiss Alps

description: Novel multimodal Knowledge Tracing dataset for educational game contexts, incorporating game-playing videos, synthetically generated question text, and detailed game logs across Mathematics, English, Indonesian, and Malaysian subjects with 28 knowledge concepts and multilingual content for adaptive learning systems.
paper_title: ES-KT-24: A Multimodal Knowledge Tracing Benchmark Dataset with Educational Game Playing Video and Synthetic Text Generation

description: Dataset for end-to-end driving focused on long-tail driving events, providing multi-view video data, trajectories, high-level instructions, and detailed multilingual reasoning traces (English, Spanish, Chinese) from domain experts to facilitate few-shot generalization and instruction following evaluation.
paper_title: LongTail Driving Scenarios with Reasoning Traces: The KITScenes LongTail Dataset

description: First longitudinal multimodal empathy dataset collected over a month-long deployment of social robots in participants' homes, capturing 41 participants sharing vulnerable experiences and reading empathic stories with AI agents for modeling empathy toward personal experiences in naturalistic settings.
paper_title: EmpathicStories++: A Multimodal Dataset for Empathy towards Personal Experiences

description: Ambiguity-aware multimodal machine translation dataset with English-Chinese parallel sentences and corresponding images, specifically designed to include more ambiguity and variety than existing MMT datasets using word sense disambiguation to select challenging data for better visual information utilization.
paper_title: 3AM: An Ambiguity-Aware Multi-Modal Machine Translation Dataset

description: 25-year daily-resolution wildfire dataset covering 240 million hectares across British Columbia with 38 covariates including fire detections, weather, fuel conditions, terrain, and anthropogenic factors. Enables long-term temporal modeling and large-scale spatial coverage for wildfire risk prediction research.
paper_title: BCWildfire: A Long-term Multi-factor Dataset and Deep Learning Benchmark for Boreal Wildfire Risk Prediction

description: Step-by-step reasoning dataset for autonomous driving scenarios covering perception, prediction, and planning tasks with detailed reasoning traces. Enables systematic evaluation of visual reasoning capabilities in realistic driving contexts beyond just final answer accuracy.
paper_title: DriveLMM-o1: A Step-by-Step Reasoning Dataset and Large Multimodal Model for Driving Scenario Understanding

description: Large-scale multimodal dataset for tool-augmented VQA featuring real-world visual contexts and challenging implicit multi-step reasoning tasks. Includes 10 multimodal tools across 7 task domains with average 2.78 reasoning steps per instance, using novel ToolEngine pipeline for human-like tool-use reasoning generation.
paper_title: ToolVQA: A Dataset for Multi-step Reasoning VQA with External Tools

description: MUSES is a multi-sensor semantic perception dataset for autonomous driving under adverse conditions, integrating frame camera, lidar, radar, event camera, and IMU/GNSS sensors. It introduces uncertainty-aware panoptic segmentation as a novel task and captures both class-level and instance-level uncertainty in ground truth annotations.
paper_title: MUSES: The Multi-Sensor Semantic Perception Dataset for Driving under Uncertainty

description: Electrolyzers-HSI is a multimodal benchmark for material detection in recycling, featuring co-registered high-resolution RGB images and hyperspectral data spanning 400-2500 nm. It supports automated electrolyzer materials classification with over 4.2 million pixel vectors for sustainable recycling applications.
paper_title: Electrolyzers-HSI: Close-Range Multi-Scene Hyperspectral Imaging Benchmark Dataset

description: OmniHD-Scenes provides comprehensive omnidirectional high-definition data for autonomous driving with 128-beam LiDAR, six cameras, and six 4D imaging radar systems. It includes 450K+ synchronized frames with precise 3D bounding boxes and semantic segmentation annotations, plus a novel automated pipeline for dense occupancy ground truth.
paper_title: OmniHD-Scenes: A Next-Generation Multimodal Dataset for Autonomous Driving

description: CIRThan is a sketch+text Composed Image Retrieval dataset for Thangka imagery, a culturally grounded domain. It contains 2,287 high-quality Thangka images paired with human-drawn sketches and hierarchical textual descriptions at three semantic levels, enabling fine-grained semantic reasoning and structured visual understanding.
paper_title: A Sketch+Text Composed Image Retrieval Dataset for Thangka

description: SOMA-1M provides over 1.3 million pixel-level precisely aligned SAR-Optical image pairs with global multi-scale coverage from 0.5m to 10m resolution. It establishes comprehensive evaluation benchmarks for four hierarchical vision tasks including image matching, fusion, SAR-assisted cloud removal, and cross-modal translation.
paper_title: SOMA-1M: A Large-Scale SAR-Optical Multi-resolution Alignment Dataset for Multi-Task Remote Sensing

description: Extends deception detection to deception reasoning by providing objective evidence to support subjective judgment. Constructs dataset and evaluation metrics for analyzing factual inconsistencies and intent behind potential lies.
paper_title: Can Deception Detection Go Deeper? Dataset, Evaluation, and Benchmark for Deception Reasoning

num_samples: 14 billion user interactions and 250 million items
description: Proposes FORGE, a comprehensive benchmark for semantic identifiers in generative retrieval using industrial datasets from Taobao. Features multimodal item features and explores SID construction optimizations with novel evaluation metrics.
paper_title: FORGE: Forming Semantic Identifiers for Generative Retrieval in Industrial Datasets

description: Introduces ViMRHP, a large-scale Vietnamese benchmark dataset for Multimodal Review Helpfulness Prediction. Covers four domains and leverages AI-assisted annotation to reduce time and costs while maintaining data quality.
paper_title: ViMRHP: A Vietnamese Benchmark Dataset for Multimodal Review Helpfulness Prediction via Human-AI Collaborative Annotation

description: Introduces INTSD, a large-scale dataset of nighttime traffic signboards across 41 classes captured under varying lighting and weather conditions in India. Provides comprehensive benchmark for both detection and classification tasks.
paper_title: Navigating in the Dark: A Multimodal Framework and Dataset for Nighttime Traffic Sign Recognition

description: Constructs EMER dataset with eye behaviors as emotional cues alongside facial expressions using spontaneous emotion induction paradigm. Features separate multi-view emotion labels for multimodal ER and FER to bridge the emotion gap.
paper_title: Smile on the Face, Sadness in the Eyes: Bridging the Emotion Gap with a Multimodal Dataset of Eye and Facial Behaviors

description: Introduces EmoSign, the first sign video dataset with sentiment and emotion labels for American Sign Language. Annotated by 3 Deaf ASL signers and includes open-ended descriptions of emotion cues for understanding multimodal emotion in sign languages.
paper_title: EmoSign: A Multimodal Dataset for Understanding Emotions in American Sign Language

description: Constructs UAV-CB, a new RGB-T UAV detection dataset emphasizing complex low-altitude backgrounds and camouflage characteristics. Specifically curated to capture real-world challenges of UAV detection in cluttered environments.
paper_title: UAV-CB: A Complex-Background RGB-T Dataset and Local Frequency Bridge Network for UAV Detection

description: Presents EMMa, an extensible multimodal dataset of Amazon product listings with rich material annotations, containing over 2.8 million objects with images, text, and physical properties. The dataset introduces a comprehensive taxonomy of 182 physical materials and supports combinatorial multi-task learning configurations across vision and NLP domains.
paper_title: An Extensible Multimodal Multi-task Object Dataset with Materials

description: A large-scale multimodal dataset for robot design understanding covering various robotics domains from scientific literature. It enables automated design generation, text-based design retrieval, and AI-powered design assistant development, addressing the lack of large-scale design datasets in robotics.
paper_title: RoboDesign1M: A Large-scale Dataset for Robot Design Understanding

description: A large-scale multimodal dataset for tree species classification using both Aerial Lidar Scanning point clouds and Very High Resolution aerial images. It spans 339 km² across 449 monospecific forests with 18 tree species, being the largest and most comprehensive Lidar dataset for tree species identification.
paper_title: PureForest: A Large-Scale Aerial Lidar and Aerial Imagery Dataset for Tree Species Classification in Monospecific Forests

description: A multi-task dataset for urban waste management from garbage truck imagery, supporting waste container detection, tracking, and overflow segmentation. It addresses the gap in monitoring overflowing waste containers from real-world logistics contexts rather than static environments.
paper_title: StreetView-Waste: A Multi-Task Dataset for Urban Waste Management

description: The first dataset incorporating touch sensing into multimodal language models with 44K in-the-wild vision-touch pairs and human/GPT-4V annotations. It addresses the gap in tactile data for multimodal alignment and demonstrates improved touch-vision-language understanding capabilities.
paper_title: A Touch, Vision, and Language Dataset for Multimodal Alignment

description: The first dataset for multimodal recognition of ambivalence and hesitancy in videos for digital behavioral change interventions, featuring 10.60 hours of video from 300 participants with expert annotations for temporal and frame-level A/H detection. It addresses the critical need for automatic A/H recognition in cost-effective digital health interventions.
paper_title: BAH Dataset for Ambivalence/Hesitancy Recognition in Videos for Digital Behavioural Change

description: A highway-scale multimodal driving dataset for heavy truck autonomy with long-range sensing up to 1,000 meters for 2D detection and 400 meters for 3D detection. It addresses the gap in existing urban-focused datasets by providing the sensing range necessary for safe heavy truck braking distances.
paper_title: TruckDrive: Long-Range Autonomous Highway Driving Dataset

description: A multimodal eye-tracking dataset for mental workload estimation in industrial human-robot collaboration, time-synchronizing eye-tracking signals with environmental measurements and task context from 26 participants wearing Meta ARIA smart glasses. It enables development of algorithms for workload estimation in realistic industrial HRC scenarios.
paper_title: GAZELOAD A Multimodal Eye-Tracking Dataset for Mental Workload in Industrial Human-Robot Collaboration

description: JIR-Arena presents the first benchmark dataset for Just-in-time Information Recommendation (JIR), evaluating systems' ability to deliver relevant information precisely when users need it. It features diverse, information-request-intensive scenarios with multi-turn, multi-entity validation framework for assessing information need inference, timely recommendations, and content relevance.
paper_title: JIR-Arena: The First Benchmark Dataset for Just-in-time Information Recommendation

description: DrivIng provides a large-scale multimodal autonomous driving dataset with complete geo-referenced digital twin integration over an 18 km route. It enables 1-to-1 transfer of real traffic into simulation while preserving agent interactions, offering continuous recordings from multiple sensors with 3D bounding box annotations at 10 Hz across 12 classes.
paper_title: DrivIng: A Large-Scale Multimodal Driving Dataset with Full Digital Twin Integration

description: EmoArt Dataset presents one of the most comprehensive emotion-annotated art datasets containing 132,664 artworks across 56 painting styles. Each image includes structured annotations covering objective descriptions, visual attributes, arousal-valence labels, emotion categories, and art therapy effects, enabling emotion-driven image synthesis evaluation.
paper_title: EmoArt: A Multidimensional Dataset for Emotion-Aware Artistic Generation

description: CC-Foundation provides a high-quality, diversified dataset containing 200,000 image pairs and 1.2 million captions specifically for Remote Sensing Image Change Captioning (RSICC). The dataset supports the development of multimodal large language models for generating natural language descriptions of surface changes between multi-temporal remote sensing images, detailing categories, locations, and dynamics of changed objects.
paper_title: CCExpert: Advancing MLLM Capability in Remote Sensing Change Captioning with Difference-Aware Integration and a Foundational Dataset

description: This dataset introduces a novel benchmark for segmenting flooded areas in satellite images, addressing the shortage of suitable datasets for flood detection. It consists of satellite imagery from the 2019 Midwestern USA floods with 10 images per location across five states, ensuring uniform resolution and robustness to seasonal changes.
paper_title: A Novel Dataset for Flood Detection Robust to Seasonal Changes in Satellite Imagery

description: ELR-1000 presents a culturally-grounded multimodal dataset of 1,060 traditional recipes from rural communities across 10 endangered Eastern Indian languages. It captures linguistic and cultural nuances in culinary practices with socio-cultural context, providing benchmarks for translation quality assessment and cultural preservation in language technologies for underrepresented languages.
paper_title: ELR-1000: A Community-Generated Dataset for Endangered Indic Indigenous Languages

description: Real-RSCM provides a high-quality manually annotated dataset for Remote Sensing Copy-Move Question Answering (RSCMQA) with realistic tampered images that have spatial logic, unlike randomly generated tampering. The dataset focuses on interpreting complex tampering scenarios and inferring object relationships for defense security and land resource monitoring applications.
paper_title: Challenging Dataset and Multi-modal Gated Mixture of Experts Model for Remote Sensing Copy-Move Forgery Understanding

description: This dataset presents a multimodal benchmark collected using novel Intelligent Knee Sleeves for 3D lower body motion estimation, combining time-series data from wearable sensors with ground truth from motion capture systems. It enables camera-free human pose estimation across various activities and individuals, demonstrating effectiveness for seamless movement assessment without visual occlusion limitations.
paper_title: Intelligent Knee Sleeves: A Real-time Multimodal Dataset for 3D Lower Body Motion Estimation Using Smart Textile

description: First and largest open-source multimodal dataset for controllable generation of road networks and building layouts with 72,400 paired samples covering around 80,000km² globally. The dataset incorporates urban characteristics like road orientation and building density for practical urban design applications.
paper_title: RoBus: A Multimodal Dataset for Controllable Road Networks and Building Layouts Generation

description: Largest multimodal dataset for aerodynamic car design with 8,000 diverse car designs and high-fidelity CFD simulations. Features detailed 3D meshes, parametric models, aerodynamic coefficients, and extensive flow field data for automotive design optimization and ML applications.
paper_title: DrivAerNet++: A Large-Scale Multimodal Car Dataset with Computational Fluid Dynamics Simulations and Deep Learning Benchmarks

description: ConfLab introduces a new concept for multimodal multisensor data collection of in-the-wild free-standing social conversations during a real networking event. It uniquely combines overhead video capture with custom wearable sensors for privacy-preserving social interaction analysis, providing benchmarks for keypoints detection, speaker detection, and F-formation detection.
paper_title: ConfLab: A Data Collection Concept, Dataset, and Benchmark for Machine Analysis of Free-Standing Social Interactions in the Wild

description: WOD-E2E is specifically curated for challenging long-tail driving scenarios with occurrence frequency less than 0.03%, containing 12 hours of driving data from 360-degree camera views. It introduces the novel Rater Feedback Score (RFS) evaluation metric that measures trajectory preference alignment rather than conventional distance-based metrics.
paper_title: WOD-E2E: Waymo Open Dataset for End-to-End Driving in Challenging Long-tail Scenarios

description: A benchmark for Knowledgeable Deep Research covering 9 domains with 41 expert-level questions, incorporating structured knowledge resources (1,252 tables) for multi-step information seeking and reasoning. Evaluates agents' ability to generate comprehensive multimodal reports using both structured and unstructured knowledge.
paper_title: Towards Knowledgeable Deep Research: Framework and Benchmark

description: A multi-category, cross-cultural benchmark for structured cultural metadata inference from images using an LLM-as-Judge framework. Evaluates VLMs' ability to infer creator, origin, period and other cultural attributes across different cultural regions and metadata types.
paper_title: Appear2Meaning: A Cross-Cultural Benchmark for Structured Cultural Metadata Inference from Images

description: A large-scale naturalistic dataset capturing real-world driving automation usage across 127 drivers and 136.6 hours of driving. Synchronizes multimodal data including front-view video, in-cabin video, CAN bus signals, radar, and GPS for predicting control transitions in assisted driving systems.
paper_title: BATON: A Multimodal Benchmark for Bidirectional Automation Transition Observation in Naturalistic Driving

description: The first unified large-scale multi-modal benchmark for nadir-view UAV scenarios with over 273K VQA pairs from high-fidelity simulation. Consolidates single frames, temporal sequences, and cross-modal generation samples across 22 diverse reasoning types for aerial scene understanding.
paper_title: UAVReason: A Unified, Large-Scale Benchmark for Multimodal Aerial Scene Reasoning and Generation

description: A comprehensive benchmark specifically designed to evaluate fine-grained consistency in multi-instance and multi-instruction image editing settings. Evaluates models' ability to perform precise, localized editing across multiple identical instances while maintaining spatial alignment.
paper_title: MIRAGE: Benchmarking and Aligning Multi-Instance Image Editing

description: A benchmark for Software Architecture Diagram Understanding containing 154 carefully curated diagrams spanning behavioral, structural, and ER diagrams with 2,431 question-answer tasks. Evaluates VLMs on architecture diagrams as structured software engineering artifacts rather than generic images.
paper_title: Benchmarking and Evaluating VLMs for Software Architecture Diagram Understanding

description: A benchmark reformulating VLM image tampering from coarse region labels to pixel-grounded, meaning-aware task. Includes per-pixel tamper maps and semantic classification with natural language descriptions.
paper_title: From Masks to Pixels and Meaning: A New Taxonomy, Benchmark, and Metrics for VLM Image Tampering

description: A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating universal multi-reference image editing models across virtual try-on scenarios. Contains five representative VTON tasks with progressively increasing complexity.
paper_title: VTEdit-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Multi-Reference Image Editing Models in Virtual Try-On

description: A design-driven benchmark for poster understanding and generation with 7,765 image-annotation instances across composition structure, typographic hierarchy, and semantic intent. Bridges visual design cognition and generative modeling with tasks for layout parsing and design quality assessment.
paper_title: PosterIQ: A Design Perspective Benchmark for Poster Understanding and Generation

description: An episodic multi-turn multimodal benchmark that instantiates short research workflows requiring agents to navigate across papers, align evidence from figures and tables, and integrate accumulated evidence for cross-paper comparisons and multi-figure integration.
paper_title: EpiBench: Benchmarking Multi-turn Research Workflows for Multimodal Agents

description: A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating scientific data analysis and visualization agents grounded in a structured taxonomy spanning application domain, data type, complexity level, and visualization operation. Features multimodal outcome-centric evaluation pipeline with expert validation.
paper_title: SciVisAgentBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating Scientific Data Analysis and Visualization Agents

description: A three-stage pipeline and dataset for benchmarking automated benchmark generation across computer science, mathematics, medicine, and theory-of-mind reasoning. Tests how well models design benchmarks rather than just answer them, with designer-answerer matrices and psychometric diagnostics.
paper_title: BenchBench: Benchmarking Automated Benchmark Generation

description: A fine-grained multimodal evaluation benchmark for manufacturing scenarios combining real-world 2D images and 3D point clouds with domain-specific annotations. Evaluates workpiece verification, structural surface inspection, and assembly verification tasks with precise model numbers and technical specifications.
paper_title: FORGE:Fine-grained Multimodal Evaluation for Manufacturing Scenarios

description: A benchmark for evaluating culture-conditioned visual value grounding in MLLMs built from World Values Survey questions. Uses minimally contrastive image pairs to represent opposing response options across 13 countries, testing whether models can ground cultural values when options are visualized rather than text-based.
paper_title: ValueGround: Evaluating Culture-Conditioned Visual Value Grounding in MLLMs

description: A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating tool-use proficiency in MLLMs featuring 32 diverse OpenCV-based visual operations. Structured across a nine-category cognitive hierarchy to rigorously assess multi-tool composition and long-horizon, multi-step plan execution.
paper_title: VTC-Bench: Evaluating Agentic Multimodal Models via Compositional Visual Tool Chaining

description: A dataset of over 13,000 image-mask pairs for industrial visual anomaly detection, generated synthetically without requiring real defective data. Provides realistic anomalous images with pixel-level masks for evaluating anomaly detection and segmentation performance.
paper_title: MIRAGE: Model-agnostic Industrial Realistic Anomaly Generation and Evaluation for Visual Anomaly Detection

description: A large-scale benchmark evaluating spatial and nonlinear EEG classification combinations across three open-access datasets. Operates at per-participant level across multiple frequency bands to assess both group-level performance and individual variability.
paper_title: Rethinking Generalized BCIs: Benchmarking 340,000+ Unique Algorithmic Configurations for EEG Mental Command Decoding

description: The first domain-specific evaluation suite for over 100 African English accents across 10+ countries and seven application domains. Benchmarks ASR and multimodal LLM-based speech recognition systems using spontaneous and non-spontaneous speech conversations.
paper_title: AfriSpeech-MultiBench: A Verticalized Multidomain Multicountry Benchmark Suite for African Accented English ASR

description: A Byte-Grounded Traffic Description benchmark combining raw network traffic bytes with structured expert annotations for multimodal reasoning towards explainable encrypted traffic interpretation.
paper_title: Multimodal Reasoning with LLM for Encrypted Traffic Interpretation: A Benchmark

description: NSW-EPNews is the first benchmark jointly evaluating time-series models and LLMs on electricity price prediction. It includes over 175,000 half-hourly spot prices with multimodal inputs including news and weather features, yielding 3.6k prompt-output pairs.
paper_title: NSW-EPNews: A News-Augmented Benchmark for Electricity Price Forecasting with LLMs

description: A comprehensive maritime benchmark built under the Entity-Environment-Event (3E) paradigm with 63 fine-grained vessel categories and 5 dynamic maritime incidents. It covers classification, detection, and VQA tasks in open-water environments to evaluate fine-grained visual understanding and reasoning in maritime scenes.
paper_title: MARINER: A 3E-Driven Benchmark for Fine-Grained Perception and Complex Reasoning in Open-Water Environments

description: The first benchmark designed specifically to evaluate multimodal large language models in hyperspectral image understanding. It features a dual-modality evaluation framework that transforms HSI data into PCA-based composite images and structured textual reports across 13 task categories.
paper_title: HM-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models in Hyperspectral Remote Sensing

description: A museum-grounded benchmark for evaluating vision-language models on Chinese artworks beyond short-form recognition. Comprises four subtasks including evidence-grounded reasoning, expert-style appreciation, reinterpretation, and authenticity discrimination across Palace Museum objects.
paper_title: CArtBench: Evaluating Vision-Language Models on Chinese Art Understanding, Interpretation, and Authenticity

description: A hierarchical, multi-view benchmark for evaluating VLMs in the dietary domain across three cognitive complexity levels: Fine-Grained Classification, Nutrition Estimation, and Visual Question Answering. Comprises 3,021 distinct dishes with an average of 5.27 images per entry.
paper_title: DiningBench: A Hierarchical Multi-view Benchmark for Perception and Reasoning in the Dietary Domain

description: An evidence-grounded multimodal reasoning benchmark for soybean and cotton phenotyping from UAV imagery. Features 385 digital images and over 3,000 benchmark samples spanning disease, pest control, weed management, and yield domains.
paper_title: From UAV Imagery to Agronomic Reasoning: A Multimodal LLM Benchmark for Plant Phenotyping

description: A comprehensive benchmark for training and evaluating MLLM-based general anomaly detection models. Designed to facilitate MLLM-based general AD research with multimodal data specifically for anomaly detection tasks.
paper_title: MMR-AD: A Large-Scale Multimodal Dataset for Benchmarking General Anomaly Detection with Multimodal Large Language Models

description: A human-annotated benchmark for evaluating search-augmented agents in multimodal evidence retrieval and reasoning over noisy web environments. It uses natural language queries without explicit modality cues and incorporates underexplored modalities like video and audio for complex multi-hop reasoning.
paper_title: MERRIN: A Benchmark for Multimodal Evidence Retrieval and Reasoning in Noisy Web Environments

description: A human-annotated dataset for cross-sensory inference containing participant-image pairs across food images. Each pair includes numeric ratings and free-text descriptors for four sensory dimensions: taste, smell, texture, and sound from food images.
paper_title: FoodSense: A Multisensory Food Dataset and Benchmark for Predicting Taste, Smell, Texture, and Sound from Images

description: The first comprehensive benchmark to evaluate VLM ability against compressed images, varying existing widely used image codecs across diverse tasks. Encompasses over one million compressed images to test generalization across different compression levels.
paper_title: Benchmarking and Enhancing VLM for Compressed Image Understanding

description: AesEval-Bench is the first systematic framework for aesthetic quality assessment in graphic design, spanning four dimensions and twelve indicators with three fully quantifiable tasks: aesthetic judgment, region selection, and precise localization. It uniquely focuses on design aesthetics rather than general image quality.
paper_title: Can Vision Language Models Assess Graphic Design Aesthetics? A Benchmark, Evaluation, and Dataset Perspective

description: MagicMirror introduces MagicData340K, a human-annotated dataset of 340K generated images with fine-grained artifact labels, and MagicBench for automated evaluation of T2I model artifacts. It provides systematic assessment of physical artifacts like anatomical and structural flaws.
paper_title: MagicMirror: A Large-Scale Dataset and Benchmark for Fine-Grained Artifacts Assessment in Text-to-Image Generation

description: UniSim-Bench encompasses 7 multi-modal perceptual similarity tasks with 25 datasets total, evaluating how well automated metrics align with human perception across uni- and multimodal inputs. It reveals gaps between general-purpose and specialized models in perceptual similarity assessment.
paper_title: Towards Unified Benchmark and Models for Multi-Modal Perceptual Metrics

description: Bench-C assesses corruption robustness of LVLMs emphasizing discriminative samples, with a selection strategy considering prediction inconsistency under corruption and semantic diversity. It introduces Robustness Alignment Score (RAS) measuring degradation in logit-level prediction structure.
paper_title: Benchmarking Corruption Robustness of LVLMs: A Discriminative Benchmark and Robustness Alignment Metric

description: A comprehensive benchmark for deepfake photorealism assessment that approximates human perception. It includes a diverse quality deepfake video dataset and large-scale annotations with photorealism scores and textual descriptions from 3,500 human annotators.
paper_title: DREAM: A Benchmark Study for Deepfake photoREalism AssessMent

description: User-centric, multi-dimensional benchmark for text-to-image evaluation with 2,845 prompts validated by over 15,000 human comparisons. Introduces role-driven evaluation across seven user roles and nine evaluation angles grounded in expert studies.
paper_title: VISTAR:A User-Centric and Role-Driven Benchmark for Text-to-Image Evaluation

description: AesBench comprehensively evaluates aesthetic perception capacities of MLLMs through an Expert-labeled Aesthetics Perception Database (EAPD) with professional annotations and integrative criteria measuring abilities from four perspectives: Perception (AesP), Empathy (AesE), Assessment (AesA) and Interpretation (AesI).
paper_title: AesBench: An Expert Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models on Image Aesthetics Perception

description: Human Preference-Aligned Concept Customization Benchmark containing single- and multi-concept tasks with stage-wise evaluation across difficulty ranges from individual actions to multi-person interactions. Features decomposed evaluation criteria and human preference alignment.
paper_title: Human Preference-Aligned Concept Customization Benchmark via Decomposed Evaluation

description: The first holistic benchmark specifically for portrait image quality perception with diverse sources (natural, synthetic, AI-generated, artistic, CG). Covers technical distortions, AIGC-specific distortions, and aesthetics with various question formats at global and local levels.
paper_title: Q-Bench-Portrait: Benchmarking Multimodal Large Language Models on Portrait Image Quality Perception

description: First large-scale aesthetic guidance dataset and benchmark with photos annotated with aesthetic scores, analyses, and guidance. Addresses gap between ordinary users and professional photographers through actionable shooting guidance.
paper_title: Venus: Benchmarking and Empowering Multimodal Large Language Models for Aesthetic Guidance and Cropping

description: A unified multi-modal image aesthetic assessment benchmark with three aesthetic levels: Perception, Description, and Assessment. Designed to better align with human aesthetics and evaluate MLLMs' aesthetic capabilities across multiple dimensions beyond traditional single-task constraints.
paper_title: UNIAA: A Unified Multi-modal Image Aesthetic Assessment Baseline and Benchmark

description: A holistic benchmark evaluating MLLMs on low-level visual perception, description, and quality assessment. Includes LLVisionQA dataset with human-asked questions on low-level attributes and LLDescribe dataset with expert-labeled golden descriptions, plus visual quality assessment capabilities.
paper_title: Q-Bench: A Benchmark for General-Purpose Foundation Models on Low-level Vision

description: Q-Bench+ evaluates multimodal large language models on low-level visual perception tasks including quality assessment, comparison, and description on both single images and image pairs. The benchmark uniquely focuses on low-level visual attributes like clarity and lighting, and extends evaluation to pairwise image comparisons which better mirrors human evaluation practices.
paper_title: Q-Bench+: A Benchmark for Multi-modal Foundation Models on Low-level Vision from Single Images to Pairs

description: STORM is a comprehensive benchmark for visual rating capabilities of MLLMs across ordinal regression tasks. It encompasses 14 ordinal regression datasets across five visual rating domains with 655K image-level pairs, evaluating models' ability to understand ordinal relationships in rating labels.
paper_title: STORM: Benchmarking Visual Rating of MLLMs with a Comprehensive Ordinal Regression Dataset

description: This work introduces a meta-evaluation benchmark for text-to-image generation that includes chain-of-thought explanations alongside quality scores for generated images. It provides comprehensive assessment of automatic evaluation methods with manually annotated explanations for reliable evaluation of text-to-image models.
paper_title: Automatic Evaluation for Text-to-image Generation: Task-decomposed Framework, Distilled Training, and Meta-evaluation Benchmark

description: TIEdit is a benchmark for systematic evaluation of text-guided image editing methods, consisting of 512 source images paired with editing prompts across eight representative editing tasks, producing 5,120 edited images. It uniquely provides comprehensive evaluation across perceptual quality, editing alignment, and content preservation with expert-validated ratings.
paper_title: Evaluating Image Editing with LLMs: A Comprehensive Benchmark and Intermediate-Layer Probing Approach

description: A benchmark specifically designed to evaluate large multimodal models' proficiency in discerning video quality, featuring videos from natural scenes, AI-generated content, and computer graphics. Expands evaluation beyond traditional technical, aesthetic, and temporal distortions to include AIGC distortions, with multiple question formats including open-ended and video pair comparisons.
paper_title: Q-Bench-Video: Benchmarking the Video Quality Understanding of LMMs

description: Gen3DHF provides comprehensive quality assessment of AI-generated 3D human faces with videos, Mean Opinion Scores across quality and authenticity dimensions, distortion-aware saliency maps, and descriptions. It enables evaluation of 3D face generation quality and authenticity in a standardized manner.
paper_title: LMME3DHF: Benchmarking and Evaluating Multimodal 3D Human Face Generation with LMMs

description: EvalMi-50K is a comprehensive dataset for evaluating text-to-image generation with 2,100 extensive prompts across 20 fine-grained task dimensions and 100K mean-opinion scores plus 50K QA pairs. It focuses on perceptual quality and text-image alignment assessment using LMM-based evaluation metrics.
paper_title: LMM4LMM: Benchmarking and Evaluating Large-multimodal Image Generation with LMMs

description: DreamBench++ is a human-aligned benchmark for personalized image generation that uses advanced multimodal GPT models for automation. It systematically designs prompts to make GPT both human-aligned and self-aligned with task reinforcement, addressing the misalignment between automated evaluation and human judgment.
paper_title: DreamBench++: A Human-Aligned Benchmark for Personalized Image Generation

description: This benchmark breaks down visual advertisement creativity into atypicality and originality dimensions with fine-grained human annotations. It proposes tasks specifically for subjective creativity assessment and evaluates alignment between state-of-the-art VLMs and humans on automatic creativity assessment.
paper_title: Leveraging Large Models to Evaluate Novel Content: A Case Study on Advertisement Creativity

description: This dataset evaluates how image quality issues (blur, misframing, rotation) affect VLM-generated captions for product identification, specifically focusing on blind and low-vision users' needs. It bridges accessibility research with VLM evaluation using real-world images from BLV participants.
paper_title: "It's trained by non-disabled people": Evaluating How Image Quality Affects Product Captioning with Vision-Language Models

description: First unified and interpretable evaluation framework for diffusion model image watermarking via vision-language models. Provides separate evaluation for residual watermarks (artifact strength, erasure resistance) and semantic watermarks (latent distribution shifts).
paper_title: WMVLM: Evaluating Diffusion Model Image Watermarking via Vision-Language Models

description: Comprehensive evaluation framework testing VLM performance under controlled perturbations including lighting variation, motion blur, and compression artifacts. Uses both lexical-based and neural-based similarity measures across diverse datasets.
paper_title: Evaluating Robustness of Vision-Language Models Under Noisy Conditions

description: Curated set of semantic error graphs containing prompts and sets of increasingly erroneous images. Enables rigorous evaluation of text-to-image prompt faithfulness metrics using meta-metric scores derived from statistical tests.
paper_title: Who Evaluates the Evaluations? Objectively Scoring Text-to-Image Prompt Coherence Metrics with T2IScoreScore (TS2)

description: Scalable framework for assessing realism of synthetic image-editing methods for adding environmental conditions (fog, rain, snow, nighttime) to car-mounted camera images. Uses VLM jury for perceptual realism and embedding-based distributional analysis.
paper_title: Scalable Evaluation of the Realism of Synthetic Environmental Augmentations in Images

description: HanMo-Bench is a specialized dataset for evaluating professional-grade Chinese artistic painting evaluation, featuring authentic auction-grade masterpieces and AI-generated works grounded in real-world market valuations. It uniquely focuses on abstract artistic domain evaluation requiring extensive artistic training.
paper_title: HanMoVLM: Large Vision-Language Models for Professional Artistic Painting Evaluation

description: Q-Real introduces fine-grained evaluation of realism and plausibility in AI-generated images, with entity-level annotations and attribution descriptions. It provides both judgment and grounding with reasoning tasks for evaluating MLLMs on quality assessment.
paper_title: Q-REAL: Towards Realism and Plausibility Evaluation for AI-Generated Content

description: Beyond the Pixels introduces hierarchical evaluation framework for identity preservation in generative models, decomposing subjects into feature-level transformations and including underrepresented categories like anthropomorphic characters.
paper_title: Beyond the Pixels: VLM-based Evaluation of Identity Preservation in Reference-Guided Synthesis

description: Meta-evaluation benchmark for text-to-video evaluation systems focusing on videos up to 10,486 seconds. Uses pairwise comparison framework with synthetically degraded videos across 10 distinct quality aspects to assess evaluation system reliability.
paper_title: SLVMEval: Synthetic Meta Evaluation Benchmark for Text-to-Long Video Generation

description: Large-scale Generated Video Quality assessment dataset with 2,808 AIGC videos from 6 generation models using 468 text prompts. Evaluates perceptual quality from three dimensions: spatial quality, temporal quality, and text-video alignment, accompanied by the UGVQ unified quality assessment model.
paper_title: Benchmarking Multi-dimensional AIGC Video Quality Assessment: A Dataset and Unified Model

description: Pioneering study on human activity AGV quality assessment with 6,000 AGVs from 15 T2V models using 400 human activity text prompts. Evaluates human appearance quality, action continuity quality, and overall video quality while identifying semantic issues of human body parts.
paper_title: Human-Activity AGV Quality Assessment: A Benchmark Dataset and an Objective Evaluation Metric

description: Multi-dimensional benchmark dataset for text-to-video quality evaluation with 148 textual prompts and 1,783 videos from 13 T2V models. Evaluates four dimensions: overall impression, text-video consistency, realness, and technical quality, accompanied by T2VEval multi-branch fusion evaluation scheme.
paper_title: T2VEval: Benchmark Dataset and Objective Evaluation Method for T2V-generated Videos

description: W-Bench is the first comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate the robustness of watermarking methods against a wide range of image editing techniques, including image regeneration, global editing, local editing, and image-to-video generation. It systematically assesses watermark resilience across diverse editing scenarios.
paper_title: Robust Watermarking Using Generative Priors Against Image Editing: From Benchmarking to Advances

description: MCEval is a new image captioning evaluation benchmark covering six distinct domains designed to assess robustness of evaluation metrics. It accompanies the DISCODE method for generating evaluation scores better aligned with human judgments across diverse domains.
paper_title: DISCODE: Distribution-Aware Score Decoder for Robust Automatic Evaluation of Image Captioning

description: A unified evaluation framework for visual generation tasks that leverages pairwise comparison paradigm instead of absolute pointwise scoring to ensure stable and human-aligned evaluation. Achieves 0.86 Spearman correlation with LMArena leaderboard compared to 0.36 for pointwise methods.
paper_title: GenArena: How Can We Achieve Human-Aligned Evaluation for Visual Generation Tasks?

description: A comprehensive benchmark with seven distinct tracks for evaluating text-to-image models, including a formidable Long Text challenge using Generation Chain-of-Thought (GCoT). Uses advanced vision-language models for human-aligned assessment of prompt-image alignment and image aesthetics.
paper_title: FLUX-Reason-6M & PRISM-Bench: A Million-Scale Text-to-Image Reasoning Dataset and Comprehensive Benchmark

description: WeART is a large-scale benchmark of 280K artworks across 152 styles and 1,556 artists for evaluating artistic style understanding. It enables assessment of style retrieval capabilities and supports applications like style relationship mapping and generative model evaluation, addressing the challenge of representing artistic style disentangled from semantic content.
paper_title: StyleDecoupler: Generalizable Artistic Style Disentanglement

description: AIGVE-BENCH 2 is a large-scale benchmark comprising 2,500 AI-generated videos and 22,500 human-annotated detailed comments and numerical scores across nine critical evaluation aspects. It supports AIGVE-MACS, a unified model that provides both numerical scores and multi-aspect language comment feedback for evaluating AI-generated videos, addressing the limitation of existing metrics that only produce numerical scores without explanatory comments.
paper_title: AIGVE-MACS: Unified Multi-Aspect Commenting and Scoring Model for AI-Generated Video Evaluation

description: AICD is the first benchmark dataset for AI art copyright annotated by artists and legal experts, evaluating distinctive artistic style through stylistic consistency, creative uniqueness, and expressive accuracy. It uniquely addresses legal and technological aspects of AI-generated art copyright protection.
paper_title: From Imitation to Innovation: The Emergence of AI Unique Artistic Styles and the Challenge of Copyright Protection

description: First benchmark specifically designed for restored video quality assessment, built to evaluate VLM-based video quality assessment models tailored for restoration tasks. Part of a comprehensive video restoration system evaluation framework.
paper_title: MoA-VR: A Mixture-of-Agents System Towards All-in-One Video Restoration

description: A large-scale dataset of human-annotated preference pairs for graphic layout evaluation using a 4-class annotation scheme. Focuses on spatial arrangement quality assessment where identical elements determine layout quality through their positioning.
paper_title: DesignSense: A Human Preference Dataset and Reward Modeling Framework for Graphic Layout Generation

description: A benchmark dataset featuring systematic quantitative aesthetic annotations for clothing recommendation. Defines multidimensional aesthetic indicators at both item and outfit levels, including silhouette, chromaticity, materiality, craftsmanship, and stylistic synergy for aesthetics-aligned recommendation systems.
paper_title: AesRec: A Dataset for Aesthetics-Aligned Clothing Outfit Recommendation

description: A benchmark for perceptual quality assessment of AI-enhanced user-generated content (AI-UGC), featuring images enhanced through super-resolution, low-light enhancement, and denoising. It addresses the gap in quality assessment for content that blends UGC and AIGC features.
paper_title: AU-IQA: A Benchmark Dataset for Perceptual Quality Assessment of AI-Enhanced User-Generated Content

description: First large-scale benchmark specifically for quality assessment of AI-generated human images, featuring 4,000 images with multidimensional annotations including perceptual quality scores, text-image correspondence, and body part distortion labels. The dataset addresses the unique challenge of evaluating anatomical and textural distortions in AI-generated human images.
paper_title: AGHI-QA: A Subjective-Aligned Dataset and Metric for AI-Generated Human Images

description: Foundational computer vision dataset with approximately 10,610 high-quality human peer-ranked photography images and extensive multi-tier annotations. Represents a data-centric approach for commercial image datasets with scalable foundation for robust AI development.
paper_title: Peer-Ranked Precision: Creating a Foundational Dataset for Fine-Tuning Vision Models from DataSeeds' Annotated Imagery

description: First reference-free visual quality assessment framework for e-commerce in-image machine translation comprising 2K benchmark for evaluation, 30K reasoning-based annotations, and 3.5K expert-labeled preferences. Features 14 interpretable quality dimensions with spatially-aware defect quantification.
paper_title: Vectra: A New Metric, Dataset, and Model for Visual Quality Assessment in E-Commerce In-Image Machine Translation

description: FVAD is the first fine-grained video attractiveness dataset collected from a popular video website, containing 780.6 hours of content with hundreds of millions of user behaviors including view counts, fast-forward, and fast-rewind actions. It uniquely enables prediction of video attractiveness at a fine-grained segment level rather than whole video level.
paper_title: Fine-grained Video Attractiveness Prediction Using Multimodal Deep Learning on a Large Real-world Dataset

description: PortraitCraft is a unified benchmark for portrait composition understanding and generation. It features structured multi-level supervision including global composition scores, 13 composition attributes, attribute-level explanations, and composition-oriented textual descriptions for controllable generation.
paper_title: PortraitCraft: A Benchmark for Portrait Composition Understanding and Generation

description: The first robustness benchmark focused on end-to-end trajectory prediction for VLM-based autonomous driving systems. Evaluates 11 simulated scenarios encompassing sensor corruption and prompt corruption with 250 unique driving scenarios per corruption type.
paper_title: RoboDriveVLM: A Novel Benchmark and Baseline towards Robust Vision-Language Models for Autonomous Driving

description: A comprehensive trustworthiness benchmark for large vision-language models in autonomous driving considering trustfulness, safety, robustness, privacy, and fairness. Comprises over 10k unique scenes and 18k queries for investigating trustworthiness issues.
paper_title: AutoTrust: Benchmarking Trustworthiness in Large Vision Language Models for Autonomous Driving

description: A benchmark for vision-grounded decision-making driven by human values containing 1,240 images depicting diverse real-world situations. Evaluates VLMs' multimodal capabilities in leveraging human values to make decisions under vision-depicted situations.
paper_title: VIVA: A Benchmark for Vision-Grounded Decision-Making with Human Values

description: This benchmark evaluates VLM inferential privacy threats using crowdsourced everyday personal videos from 58 individuals. It uniquely focuses on the risk of inferring sensitive personal attributes from temporal behavioral patterns rather than object recognition.
paper_title: The Pervasive Blind Spot: Benchmarking VLM Inference Risks on Everyday Personal Videos

description: MemeSafetyBench evaluates VLM safety using real meme images paired with harmful and benign instructions, addressing the gap between artificial safety evaluations and real-world deployment. It uniquely demonstrates that VLMs are more vulnerable to meme-based harmful prompts than synthetic images.
paper_title: Are Vision-Language Models Safe in the Wild? A Meme-Based Benchmark Study

description: MultiPriv is the first benchmark designed to systematically evaluate individual-level privacy reasoning in VLMs through hierarchical chain-of-thought reasoning. It uniquely tests VLMs' ability to infer and link distributed information to construct individual profiles using synthetic profiles with linked identifiers and sensitive attributes.
paper_title: MultiPriv: Benchmarking Individual-Level Privacy Reasoning in Vision-Language Models

description: FIUBench is a novel VLM unlearning benchmark designed to evaluate the effectiveness of unlearning algorithms under the Right to be Forgotten setting using a Fictitious Facial Identity VQA dataset. It provides robust evaluation metrics including membership inference and adversarial privacy attacks.
paper_title: Benchmarking Vision Language Model Unlearning via Fictitious Facial Identity Dataset

description: HoliSafe provides a holistic safety dataset and benchmark spanning all five safe/unsafe image-text combinations, offering more robust training and evaluation than existing safety approaches. It uniquely introduces a visual guard module (VGM) for interpretable harmfulness assessment and comprehensive safety evaluation across diverse image-text interaction scenarios.
paper_title: HoliSafe: Holistic Safety Benchmarking and Modeling for Vision-Language Model

description: DSBench is the first comprehensive driving safety benchmark that simultaneously evaluates external environmental risks and in-cabin driving behavior safety across 10 categories and 28 sub-categories. It addresses the critical gap in assessing VLM safety-readiness for autonomous driving applications.
paper_title: Is Your VLM for Autonomous Driving Safety-Ready? A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating External and In-Cabin Risks

description: CrossVLAD evaluates cross-task adversarial attacks on unified VLMs with object-change objectives across multiple task instructions. It introduces a novel success rate metric measuring simultaneous misclassification across all tasks to assess adversarial transferability in unified VLMs.
paper_title: One Object, Multiple Lies: A Benchmark for Cross-task Adversarial Attack on Unified Vision-Language Models

description: AGENTSAFE systematically assesses safety of embodied VLM agents on hazardous instructions across the full perception-planning-execution process. It comprises 45 adversarial scenarios and 1,350 hazardous tasks inspired by Asimov's Three Laws of Robotics with multi-level evaluation protocol.
paper_title: AGENTSAFE: Benchmarking the Safety of Embodied Agents on Hazardous Instructions

description: BackdoorVLM is the first comprehensive benchmark for systematically evaluating backdoor attacks on VLMs across core vision-language tasks. It organizes multimodal backdoor threats into 5 categories and evaluates 12 attack methods across multiple datasets and models.
paper_title: BackdoorVLM: A Benchmark for Backdoor Attacks on Vision-Language Models

description: MORALISE evaluates moral alignment in VLMs using real-world expert-verified data across 13 moral topics grounded in Turiel's Domain Theory. It uniquely addresses limitations of AI-generated images in moral evaluation and provides fine-grained annotations for both topic and modality-specific moral violations.
paper_title: MORALISE: A Structured Benchmark for Moral Alignment in Visual Language Models

description: VLJailbreakBench is a safety benchmark that autonomously generates malicious image-text pairs using VLMs themselves as red team models. It uniquely leverages the target models' own capabilities to create diverse, effective jailbreak prompts across different contexts.
paper_title: IDEATOR: Jailbreaking and Benchmarking Large Vision-Language Models Using Themselves

description: News-image benchmark with 1,343 image-question pairs annotated with demographic attributes to investigate social stereotype risks in VLMs. Features diverse news outlets with systematic analysis of bias across age, gender, race, occupation, and sports using LLM-as-judge assessment.
paper_title: Bias in the Picture: Benchmarking VLMs with Social-Cue News Images and LLM-as-Judge Assessment

description: D-HUMOR introduces a novel dataset of Reddit memes annotated for dark humor detection, target category identification, and three-level intensity rating. It addresses the unique challenges of detecting dark humor in multimodal content through implicit, sensitive, and culturally contextual cues.
paper_title: D-HUMOR: Dark Humor Understanding via Multimodal Open-ended Reasoning -- A Benchmark Dataset and Method

description: LabSafety Bench evaluates LLMs and VLMs on safety issues in scientific laboratories through hazard identification, risk assessment, and consequence prediction. It includes 765 multiple-choice questions and 404 realistic lab scenarios encompassing 3,128 open-ended tasks to assess AI safety in laboratory environments.
paper_title: LabSafety Bench: Benchmarking LLMs on Safety Issues in Scientific Labs

description: SafeEditBench leverages image-editing models to create policy-aligned datasets where safe-unsafe image pairs remain visually similar except for localized regions violating specific safety rules. It enables fine-grained assessment of policy-aware generalization across five distinct safety policies.
paper_title: Towards Policy-Adaptive Image Guardrail: Benchmark and Method

description: This work introduces a more rigorous evaluation dataset for measuring demographic bias in Vision Language Models, addressing problems with current benchmarks. It provides a novel benchmark that generates more meaningful errors than previous datasets for bias detection.
paper_title: debiaSAE: Benchmarking and Mitigating Vision-Language Model Bias

description: A comprehensive safety hazards assessment benchmark with 81,809 training samples from multiple sources and a challenging test set with 1,707 samples including videos and panoramic images. Focuses on trustworthy safety assessment in real-world home environments.
paper_title: TSHA: A Benchmark for Visual Language Models in Trustworthy Safety Hazard Assessment Scenarios

description: Two benchmarking datasets based on contrastive minimal pairs design for safety (MiS) and culture (MiC). Each sample contains minimally differing captions and corresponding images to test fine-grained image-caption alignment in socially critical contexts.
paper_title: MiSCHiEF: A Benchmark in Minimal-Pairs of Safety and Culture for Holistic Evaluation of Fine-Grained Image-Caption Alignment

description: A video-language benchmark for industrial safety with 1,100 video clips from real-world industrial settings. Covers 98 routine and 67 hazardous action categories with multi-label evaluation for safety-critical assessment.
paper_title: iSafetyBench: A video-language benchmark for safety in industrial environment

description: OmniFake integrates human-curated misinformation with AI-generated content in a unified benchmark of 127K samples. It addresses the gap between NLP misinformation detection and CV AI-generated content detection by providing a comprehensive dataset for unified multimodal fake content detection.
paper_title: Towards Unified Multimodal Misinformation Detection in Social Media: A Benchmark Dataset and Baseline

description: SHAWSHANK-BENCH is the first benchmark for indirect environmental jailbreaks (IEJ) in embodied AI, where attacks are conducted through environmental prompts rather than direct prompts. It automatically constructs evaluation scenarios across 3,957 task-scene combinations to test VLM vulnerability to environmental manipulation.
paper_title: The Shawshank Redemption of Embodied AI: Understanding and Benchmarking Indirect Environmental Jailbreaks

description: A novel benchmark for trademark-safe text-to-image generation that addresses fine-grained removal of both trademarks and subtle structural brand features. It uniquely introduces unbranding task and combines VLMs with segmentation-based classifiers trained on human annotations to evaluate brand identifier removal while preserving semantic coherence.
paper_title: From Unlearning to UNBRANDING: A Benchmark for Trademark-Safe Text-to-Image Generation

description: EVADE is the first expert-curated, Chinese, multimodal benchmark for evasive content detection in e-commerce with 2,833 text samples and 13,961 images. It evaluates models' ability to detect content that superficially complies with policies while covertly conveying prohibited claims.
paper_title: EVADE-Bench: Multimodal Benchmark for Evasive Content Detection in E-Commerce Applications

description: VisoGender benchmarks gender bias in vision-language models using occupation-related scenarios with pronoun resolution tasks. It evaluates both resolution bias (accuracy differences between masculine vs feminine gender presentations) and retrieval bias (gender ratio differences in neutral queries).
paper_title: VisoGender: A dataset for benchmarking gender bias in image-text pronoun resolution

description: RRDataset evaluates AI-generated image detection models across three dimensions: scenario generalization across seven major real-world scenarios, internet transmission robustness after social media sharing, and re-digitization robustness. It addresses gaps in evaluating detection performance under complex real-world conditions.
paper_title: Bridging the Gap Between Ideal and Real-world Evaluation: Benchmarking AI-Generated Image Detection in Challenging Scenarios

description: VLM-UnBench is the first benchmark for training-free visual concept unlearning in VLMs, covering four forgetting levels, 7 source datasets, and 11 concept axes. It pairs a three-level probe taxonomy with five evaluation conditions to separate genuine forgetting from instruction compliance.
paper_title: Can VLMs Truly Forget? Benchmarking Training-Free Visual Concept Unlearning

description: The first automated system for LVLM safety benchmarking using four collaborative agents for data preprocessing, generation, augmentation, and selection. It can construct high-quality safety benchmarks within one week at minimal cost with effective discrimination between safe and unsafe models.
paper_title: Automated Safety Benchmarking: A Multi-agent Pipeline for LVLMs

description: First unified multimodal benchmark for evaluating CAPTCHA robustness against VLM-based attacks. Integrates heterogeneous CAPTCHA types from static text to interactive puzzles into a single evaluation protocol.
paper_title: MCA-Bench: A Multimodal Benchmark for Evaluating CAPTCHA Robustness Against VLM-based Attacks

description: First comprehensive benchmark for mixed-source multimodal misinformation detection including textual veracity distortion, visual veracity distortion, and cross-modal consistency distortion. Features 12 sub-categories of misinformation forgery types.
paper_title: MMFakeBench: A Mixed-Source Multimodal Misinformation Detection Benchmark for LVLMs

description: First multimodal moral benchmark for LVLMs extending Moral Foundations Vignettes with corresponding scenario images. Evaluates moral understanding across six moral foundations through moral judgment, classification, and response tasks.
paper_title: M$^3$oralBench: A MultiModal Moral Benchmark for LVLMs

description: Controlled benchmark for evaluating membership inference attacks on large vision-language models with carefully balanced member/non-member distributions. Addresses fundamental challenges in MIA evaluation by providing ground-truth membership labels across three training stages.
paper_title: OpenLVLM-MIA: A Controlled Benchmark Revealing the Limits of Membership Inference Attacks on Large Vision-Language Models

description: Large-scale collection of datasets for evaluating semantic safety of foundation models serving as robot brains. Generates undesirable situations from real-world visual scenes and human injury reports, coupled with framework for automatically generating robot constitutions using Constitutional AI mechanisms.
paper_title: Generating Robot Constitutions & Benchmarks for Semantic Safety

description: First benchmark to systematically evaluate sycophancy (tendency to uncritically echo user-provided information) in medical LVLMs across high-stakes clinical settings. Covers 18 medical departments and 20 modalities with prompts simulating biased inputs from patients, medical students, and physicians.
paper_title: EchoBench: Benchmarking Sycophancy in Medical Large Vision-Language Models

description: Comprehensive benchmark for evaluating privacy preservation capabilities of LVLMs covering privacy awareness and leakage assessment. Encompasses 26 categories of personal privacy, 15 trade secret categories, and 18 state secret categories.
paper_title: Multi-PA: A Multi-perspective Benchmark on Privacy Assessment for Large Vision-Language Models

description: Fine-grained synthetic benchmark for visual PII detection in web screenshots designed for computer-use agents. Features extended PII taxonomy, anticipatory detection for partially-filled forms, and scalable VLM-based UI reproduction with layout-invariant detection capabilities.
paper_title: WebPII: Benchmarking Visual PII Detection for Computer-Use Agents

description: MFC-Bench evaluates factual accuracy of LVLMs across three stages of multimodal fact-checking: Manipulation, Out-of-Context, and Veracity Classification. It addresses the issue of LVLMs' embedded factual information potentially deviating from truth due to bias or incorrect inference, providing comprehensive evaluation of trustworthy AI capabilities.
paper_title: MFC-Bench: Benchmarking Multimodal Fact-Checking with Large Vision-Language Models

description: Forensics-Bench provides comprehensive forgery detection evaluation with 63,292 multi-choice visual questions covering 112 unique forgery detection types across 5 perspectives (semantics, modalities, tasks, types, models). It addresses the challenge of detecting diverse AIGC-generated fake media, requiring comprehensive recognition, localization, and reasoning capabilities on various forgeries.
paper_title: Forensics-Bench: A Comprehensive Forgery Detection Benchmark Suite for Large Vision Language Models

description: MM-SafetyBench evaluates MLLM safety against image-based manipulations where query-relevant images can compromise models even when the text query itself is not malicious. It provides a comprehensive framework with 13 scenarios and 5,040 text-image pairs specifically designed to test safety-critical vulnerabilities in multimodal models.
paper_title: MM-SafetyBench: A Benchmark for Safety Evaluation of Multimodal Large Language Models

description: JailBreakV-28K is a pioneering benchmark assessing transferability of LLM jailbreak techniques to MLLMs, featuring 28,000 test cases including 20,000 text-based and 8,000 image-based jailbreak inputs across diverse adversarial scenarios.
paper_title: JailBreakV: A Benchmark for Assessing the Robustness of MultiModal Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks

description: PRISM is designed to assess MLLMs on biometric safety across two fronts: refusing biometric-related queries and preventing implicit biometric leakage in general responses while maintaining semantic faithfulness. It addresses privacy concerns in multimodal AI systems.
paper_title: Safe-LLaVA: A Privacy-Preserving Vision-Language Dataset and Benchmark for Biometric Safety

description: SaLAD evaluates the safety impact of MLLM responses on human behavior in daily life, containing 2,013 real-world image-text samples across 10 common categories. It balances unsafe scenarios with cases of oversensitivity and emphasizes realistic risk exposure.
paper_title: When Helpers Become Hazards: A Benchmark for Analyzing Multimodal LLM-Powered Safety in Daily Life

description: AdapT-Bench is a unified methodological framework and benchmark suite for systematically evaluating MLLM defense capabilities against dynamic phishing attacks in academic settings. It addresses the gap in existing security benchmarks that don't incorporate academic background information, focusing on dynamic, multilingual, and context-dependent threats targeting academic institutions.
paper_title: Can MLLMs Detect Phishing? A Comprehensive Security Benchmark Suite Focusing on Dynamic Threats and Multimodal Evaluation in Academic Environments

description: SHIELD is an evaluation benchmark for face spoofing and forgery detection with multimodal large language models, featuring true/false and multiple-choice questions across two main tasks. For face anti-spoofing, it evaluates three modalities (RGB, infrared, depth) under six attack types, and for face forgery detection, it evaluates GAN-based and diffusion-based data with visual and acoustic modalities.
paper_title: SHIELD : An Evaluation Benchmark for Face Spoofing and Forgery Detection with Multimodal Large Language Models

description: MultiTrust is the first comprehensive and unified benchmark on the trustworthiness of MLLMs across five primary aspects: truthfulness, safety, robustness, fairness, and privacy. It employs a rigorous evaluation strategy addressing both multimodal risks and cross-modal impacts, encompassing 32 diverse tasks with self-curated datasets to assess trustworthiness challenges specific to multimodal models.
paper_title: MultiTrust: A Comprehensive Benchmark Towards Trustworthy Multimodal Large Language Models

description: Presents the first benchmarking study evaluating demographic fairness of MLLMs in face verification tasks across gender and ethnicity groups. Evaluates nine open-source MLLMs on IJB-C and RFW protocols, measuring verification accuracy and demographic disparity with four FMR-based fairness metrics.
paper_title: Demographic Fairness in Multimodal LLMs: A Benchmark of Gender and Ethnicity Bias in Face Verification

description: Introduces the first benchmark for evaluating misinformation unlearning in MLLMs based on football transfer rumors. Contains 15.68K records for 80 players with four test sets to assess forgetting efficacy, generalization, utility, and robustness, supporting advanced settings like selective unlearning.
paper_title: OFFSIDE: Benchmarking Unlearning Misinformation in Multimodal Large Language Models

description: RiOSWorld is designed to evaluate the potential risks of MLLM-based computer-use agents during real-world computer manipulations. It includes 492 risky tasks spanning various computer applications and categorizes risks into user-originated and environmental risks.
paper_title: RiOSWorld: Benchmarking the Risk of Multimodal Computer-Use Agents

description: USB (Unified Safety Benchmarks) is one of the most comprehensive evaluation benchmarks in MLLM safety, featuring high-quality queries, extensive risk categories, comprehensive modal combinations, and encompassing both vulnerability and oversensitivity evaluations. It provides 4 distinct modality combinations for each of 61 risk sub-categories.
paper_title: USB: A Comprehensive and Unified Safety Evaluation Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models

description: First comprehensive multimodal safety awareness benchmark evaluating MLLMs across 29 safety scenarios. Includes both unsafe and over-safety subsets to assess models' abilities to correctly identify unsafe content while avoiding over-sensitivity that hinders helpfulness.
paper_title: Can't See the Forest for the Trees: Benchmarking Multimodal Safety Awareness for Multimodal LLMs

description: Novel benchmark evaluating gender bias in MLLMs through social relationships in dual-individual interactions. Goes beyond single-entity evaluation to examine relational and contextual gender bias through dual-character profile and narrative generation tasks.
paper_title: From Individuals to Interactions: Benchmarking Gender Bias in Multimodal Large Language Models from the Lens of Social Relationship

description: A multimodal benchmark for assessing safety-critical reasoning and planning in scientific laboratory environments. Grounded in OSHA standards and GHS, it evaluates models' ability to identify hazards and perform safety-aware reasoning across laboratory tasks with diverse manipulation complexities and risk profiles.
paper_title: LABSHIELD: A Multimodal Benchmark for Safety-Critical Reasoning and Planning in Scientific Laboratories

description: The first benchmark specifically designed to evaluate MLLM vulnerability to gaslighting negation attacks across 20 diverse categories. Tests models' ability to maintain logical consistency when users provide contradictory negations to initially correct answers.
paper_title: Benchmarking Gaslighting Negation Attacks Against Multimodal Large Language Models

description: A benchmark for evaluating cross-modal safety and reliability through four stress-testing interaction patterns (Safety, Over-rejection, Bias, Hallucination) covering 61 fine-grained types. Each instance requires integrated image-text interpretation with paired text-only controls to diagnose modality-induced behavior shifts.
paper_title: CSR-Bench: A Benchmark for Evaluating the Cross-modal Safety and Reliability of MLLMs

description: Most comprehensive multimodal content safety evaluation spanning four modalities with systematic annotation across nine critical risk categories. Introduces Multidimensional Cross Risk Score and FairScore for robust evaluation.
paper_title: OutSafe-Bench: A Benchmark for Multimodal Offensive Content Detection in Large Language Models

description: BackdoorDM is the first comprehensive benchmark for backdoor learning on diffusion models, providing nine attack methods, four defense strategies, and visualization tools. It uniquely focuses on backdoor vulnerabilities in generative diffusion models rather than discriminative models.
paper_title: BackdoorDM: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Backdoor Learning on Diffusion Model

description: SAPA-Bench is the first large-scale benchmark (7,138 scenarios) specifically designed to evaluate privacy awareness in MLLM-powered smartphone agents. It uniquely annotates privacy context by type, sensitivity level, and location, revealing that current agents show poor privacy awareness even with explicit hints.
paper_title: Mind the Third Eye! Benchmarking Privacy Awareness in MLLM-powered Smartphone Agents

description: Multi-platform, Multi-lingual, and Multimodal Meme dataset for hate speech detection with fine-grained hierarchical labels and human-verified rationales. Collected from X, 4chan, and Weibo, featuring an agentic annotation framework with seven specialized agents for nuanced hate speech analysis.
paper_title: Is AI Ready for Multimodal Hate Speech Detection? A Comprehensive Dataset and Benchmark Evaluation

description: First benchmark to synchronously evaluate fairness in both understanding and generation tasks in UMLLMs. Integrates 60 granular metrics across three dimensions (Ideal Fairness, Real-world Fidelity, Bias Inertia & Steerability) with a novel fairness space framework for comprehensive bias assessment.
paper_title: Fair in Mind, Fair in Action? A Synchronous Benchmark for Understanding and Generation in UMLLMs

description: Multimodal unlearning benchmark for evaluating targeted deletion of specific sensitive knowledge from MLLMs. Features high-quality image-text pairs with varying-proximity samples and an attack-and-defense framework to test how effectively models can forget sensitive multimodal information.
paper_title: Unlearning Sensitive Information in Multimodal LLMs: Benchmark and Attack-Defense Evaluation

description: RobustSora is designed to assess watermark robustness in AIGC video detection, comprising 6,500 videos across four types including authentic-clean, authentic-spoofed, generated-watermarked, and generated-dewatermarked. It uniquely evaluates how watermark manipulation affects AI-generated video detection systems.
paper_title: RobustSora: De-Watermarked Benchmark for Robust AI-Generated Video Detection

description: OmniSafeBench-MM provides a comprehensive toolbox for multi-modal jailbreak attack-defense evaluation, integrating 13 attack methods and 15 defense strategies. It establishes a three-dimensional evaluation protocol measuring harmfulness, intent alignment, and response detail level across 9 risk domains and 50 fine-grained categories.
paper_title: OmniSafeBench-MM: A Unified Benchmark and Toolbox for Multimodal Jailbreak Attack-Defense Evaluation

description: VideoNorms assesses cultural awareness in VideoLLMs by evaluating understanding of socio-cultural norms from US and Chinese cultures. It uses speech act theory to annotate norm adherence/violations with verbal and non-verbal evidence, testing cross-cultural video understanding capabilities.
paper_title: VideoNorms: Benchmarking Cultural Awareness of Video Language Models

description: Fire360 evaluates perception and reasoning in safety-critical firefighting scenarios using 360-degree videos from professional training sessions under degraded conditions. It introduces Transformed Object Retrieval (TOR) task to test transformation-invariant recognition of fire-damaged objects.
paper_title: Fire360: A Benchmark for Robust Perception and Episodic Memory in Degraded 360-Degree Firefighting Videos

description: RIO-Bench introduces Read-or-Ignore VQA to evaluate selective text use in visual question answering, testing when models should read text versus ignore it based on context. It provides same-scene counterfactuals by varying textual content and question types to assess both typographic robustness and text-reading capabilities.
paper_title: Read or Ignore? A Unified Benchmark for Typographic-Attack Robustness and Text Recognition in Vision-Language Models

description: DVBench evaluates VLLMs' performance in understanding safety-critical driving videos using a hierarchical ability taxonomy aligned with autonomous driving frameworks. It features 10,000 multiple-choice questions focused on safety-critical scenarios that existing multimodal benchmarks fail to adequately assess, revealing significant performance gaps in current models.
paper_title: Are Vision LLMs Road-Ready? A Comprehensive Benchmark for Safety-Critical Driving Video Understanding

description: SALMUBench is a benchmark for Sensitive Association-Level Multimodal Unlearning built upon a synthetic dataset of 60K persona-attribute associations. It introduces a novel evaluation protocol with structured holdout sets to precisely measure unlearning efficacy and collateral damage in contrastively-trained encoders.
paper_title: SALMUBench: A Benchmark for Sensitive Association-Level Multimodal Unlearning

description: Benchmark to evaluate capability of LMMs in recognizing conflicting commands with 20,000 conflicts evenly distributed between language and vision paradigms. Uses novel automatic dataset creation framework to encompass wide range of instruction forms.
paper_title: Dissecting Dissonance: Benchmarking Large Multimodal Models Against Self-Contradictory Instructions

description: First unified benchmark for multimodal deepfake detection with Mega-MMDF dataset containing 0.1 million real and 1.1 million forged samples. Employs 21 forgery pipelines combining 10 audio, 12 visual, and 6 audio-driven face reenactment methods.
paper_title: DeepfakeBench-MM: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Multimodal Deepfake Detection

description: UniSAFE is the first comprehensive benchmark for system-level safety evaluation of Unified Multimodal Models across 7 I/O modality combinations. It uses a shared-target design to enable controlled cross-task comparisons of safety failures across conventional tasks and novel multimodal-context image generation settings.
paper_title: UniSAFE: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Safety Evaluation of Unified Multimodal Models
