Combined with the SpatialQA dataset, it provides multi-level depth-related questions to assess and improve spatial understanding in vision-language models.
paper_title: SpatialBot: Precise Spatial Understanding with Vision Language Models

description: HIS-Bench evaluates Human-In-Scene understanding by requiring agents to comprehend human states and behaviors within 3D scenes, reason about surrounding environments, and answer human-related questions. It systematically evaluates across a spectrum from basic perception to commonsense reasoning and planning.
paper_title: HIS-GPT: Towards 3D Human-In-Scene Multimodal Understanding

description: The benchmark provides 90k image-caption pairs with landmark object annotations using Set-of-Marks prompting. It specifically tests spatial referring expressions and spatial relationships between objects, enabling models to learn spatial understanding without semantic bias.
paper_title: Spatial-LLaVA: Enhancing Large Language Models with Spatial Referring Expressions for Visual Understanding

description: A comprehensive benchmark for omnidirectional image understanding with 2,000 high-quality 360x180 degree images and over 4,000 QA pairs across 10 fine-grained tasks. It evaluates MLLMs' ability to comprehend immersive environments captured by omnidirectional images.
paper_title: ODI-Bench: Can MLLMs Understand Immersive Omnidirectional Environments?

description: DoraVQA is automatically extracted from 8 seasons of Dora the Explorer with precise timestamp alignment, focusing on spatial reasoning, counting, and compositional understanding that preschool children can solve. It provides pedagogically-structured content with context-question-pause-answer format.
paper_title: Structured Over Scale: Learning Spatial Reasoning from Educational Video

description: DSR Suite provides datasets for dynamic spatial reasoning that focus on object geometry evolution and 3D relationships over time. The benchmark emphasizes in-the-wild video sources, viewpoint transformations, multi-object interactions, and fine-grained procedural answers for 4D spatial understanding.
paper_title: Learning to Reason in 4D: Dynamic Spatial Understanding for Vision Language Models

description: SpaceR-151k is a specialized dataset for video spatial reasoning with 91k questions spanning diverse spatial reasoning scenarios with verifiable answers, plus 60k samples for maintaining general multimodal understanding. It specifically targets spatial structure inference from video frames, addressing a gap in existing spatial reasoning capabilities of MLLMs.
paper_title: SpaceR: Reinforcing MLLMs in Video Spatial Reasoning

description: R4D-Bench is a benchmark for depth-aware dynamic scenes with region-level prompting, built via a hybrid automated and human-verified pipeline. It focuses on 4D understanding that combines 3D spatial structures with temporal dynamics for region-level video analysis.
paper_title: 4D-RGPT: Toward Region-level 4D Understanding via Perceptual Distillation

description: SIRI-Bench evaluates VLMs' structural spatial intelligence through spatial-grounded reasoning tasks. It comprises 9,000 video-question-answer triplets where each problem is embedded in a realistic 3D scene and requires both spatial comprehension and structural reasoning.
paper_title: SIRI-Bench: Challenging VLMs' Spatial Intelligence through Complex Reasoning Tasks

description: The 2025 AI City Challenge introduces four tracks for computer vision applications in transportation and safety: Track 1 for multi-class 3D multi-camera tracking (people, humanoids, robots, forklifts), Track 2 for traffic safety video QA with multi-camera incident understanding and 3D gaze labels, Track 3 for spatial reasoning in warehouse environments with RGB-D inputs, and Track 4 for efficient road object detection from fisheye cameras. The benchmark features datasets generated in NVIDIA Omniverse and establishes new benchmarks across multiple computer vision tasks.
paper_title: The 9th AI City Challenge

description: Ego3D-Bench evaluates spatial reasoning abilities of VLMs using ego-centric, multi-view outdoor data with over 8,600 QA pairs. It specifically targets embodied AI scenarios like robotics and self-driving cars, addressing limitations in current spatial understanding benchmarks.
paper_title: Spatial Reasoning with Vision-Language Models in Ego-Centric Multi-View Scenes

description: SpatialRGBT-Bench provides ground-truth 3D annotations encompassing indoor, outdoor, and simulated environments for evaluating 3D spatial cognition in VLMs. It tests spatial perception and reasoning capabilities with region-aware understanding.
paper_title: SpatialRGPT: Grounded Spatial Reasoning in Vision Language Models

description: A challenging benchmark for evaluating multi-view spatial reasoning under realistic scenarios with partial visibility and occlusion. Features 1M QA pairs across 6 tasks, testing spatial understanding from fragmented visual cues in multi-view conditions.
paper_title: SpatialMosaic: A Multiview VLM Dataset for Partial Visibility

description: Benchmark for evaluating spatial-semantic reasoning in robotic manipulation, testing VLMs' ability to bridge geometric primitives with task semantics through automatic primitive extraction and semantic anchoring.
paper_title: PASG: A Closed-Loop Framework for Automated Geometric Primitive Extraction and Semantic Anchoring in Robotic Manipulation

description: First VQA benchmark dataset tailored to evaluate VLM robustness under challenging 3D variations. Uses physically reproducible Adversarial 3D Transformation samples to systematically assess model performance across diverse real-world 3D scenarios.
paper_title: AdvDreamer Unveils: Are Vision-Language Models Truly Ready for Real-World 3D Variations?

description: CoMa-20K is a comprehensive dataset for architectural building massing generation that includes detailed massing geometries, associated economical and programmatic data, and visual representations of development sites within existing urban contexts. It enables evaluation of VLMs on conditional massing generation tasks based on functional requirements and site context.
paper_title: CoMa: Contextual Massing Generation with Vision-Language Models

description: An automated pipeline that processes approximately 5M images with over 45M objects to generate 3D spatial VQA pairs across diverse tasks and scenes. Tests hierarchical 3D spatial understanding from geometric perception to abstract spatial reasoning in four progressively complex levels.
paper_title: HiSpatial: Taming Hierarchical 3D Spatial Understanding in Vision-Language Models

description: New benchmark for evaluating open-world reasoning-based 3D segmentation capabilities. Tests MLLMs' ability to interpret both explicit and implicit instructions for 3D object grounding and manipulation on Gaussian Splatting representations.
paper_title: REALM: An MLLM-Agent Framework for Open World 3D Reasoning Segmentation and Editing on Gaussian Splatting

description: A fully synthetic benchmark that jointly measures spatial reasoning accuracy and reasoning efficiency in VLMs. Tests mental rotation, navigation, spatial relationships, and 3D geometry transformations to probe spatial intelligence and reasoning mechanisms.
paper_title: Imagine in Space: Exploring the Frontier of Spatial Intelligence and Reasoning Efficiency in Vision Language Models

description: MM-CoT dataset provides multimodal chain-of-thought reasoning data that injects aligned visual and textual reasoning signals for spatial reasoning tasks. It enables perception-aware spatial understanding from monocular images with object-centric grounding using Visual Reference Tokens.
paper_title: Perception-Aware Multimodal Spatial Reasoning from Monocular Images

description: A new space-grounding benchmark that evaluates VLMs' ability to localize spatial references described in natural language instructions. It tests complex spatial reasoning including distance, geometry, and inter-object relationships in real environments.
paper_title: C2F-Space: Coarse-to-Fine Space Grounding for Spatial Instructions using Vision-Language Models

description: Large-scale VQA benchmark for 3D spatial reasoning in 360-degree panoramic environments using ERP images. Features five categories of 3D spatial understanding questions with accurate 3D annotations including depth, segmentation, and bounding boxes.
paper_title: PanoEnv: Exploring 3D Spatial Intelligence in Panoramic Environments with Reinforcement Learning

description: XR-Scene is a cross-room understanding benchmark for evaluating large 3D scene understanding abilities of 3D-VLMs. It contains tasks including XR-QA, XR-EmbodiedPlanning, and XR-SceneCaption that specifically test understanding across multiple rooms and large-scale 3D environments.
paper_title: LSceneLLM: Enhancing Large 3D Scene Understanding Using Adaptive Visual Preferences

description: Benchmarks for relative camera pose estimation testing VLMs' 3D spatial understanding. VRRPI-Bench uses egocentric videos with realistic scenarios, while VRRPI-Diag isolates individual motion degrees of freedom.
paper_title: Lost in Space? Vision-Language Models Struggle with Relative Camera Pose Estimation

description: A manually annotated benchmark with 271 questions across five categories designed for quantitative spatial reasoning. Tests VLMs' capability to reason about object sizes and distances quantitatively.
paper_title: Reasoning Paths with Reference Objects Elicit Quantitative Spatial Reasoning in Large Vision-Language Models

description: A benchmark of 1,100 real-world images with high spatial complexity designed to evaluate VLMs' spatial perception, structural understanding, and reasoning capabilities. It includes five tasks that minimize reliance on domain-specific knowledge to isolate general spatial reasoning ability.
paper_title: Jigsaw-Puzzles: From Seeing to Understanding to Reasoning in Vision-Language Models

description: A benchmark featuring over 138.6K QA pairs across five distinct tasks focused on evolving spatial relationships and temporal reasoning. It evaluates understanding of temporal 3D context changes and spatial-visual reasoning from monocular video inputs.
paper_title: VLM-3R: Vision-Language Models Augmented with Instruction-Aligned 3D Reconstruction

description: FlipSet evaluates Level-2 visual perspective taking in VLMs by requiring models to simulate 180-degree rotations of 2D character strings from another agent's perspective. This isolates spatial transformation abilities from 3D scene complexity and specifically tests the integration of social awareness with spatial operations.
paper_title: Egocentric Bias in Vision-Language Models

description: CyclingVQA evaluates perception, spatio-temporal understanding, and traffic-rule-to-lane reasoning from a cyclist's perspective rather than the typical vehicle-centric viewpoint. It specifically tests cyclist-centric traffic understanding and the ability to interpret cyclist-specific traffic cues and lane associations.
paper_title: From Steering to Pedalling: Do Autonomous Driving VLMs Generalize to Cyclist-Assistive Spatial Perception and Planning?

description: HandVQA evaluates fine-grained spatial reasoning about human hand anatomy through VQA tasks. Built on 3D hand datasets, it includes over 1.6M controlled multiple-choice questions probing spatial relationships between hand joints like angles, distances, and relative positions.
paper_title: HandVQA: Diagnosing and Improving Fine-Grained Spatial Reasoning about Hands in Vision-Language Models

description: XVR is a large-scale dataset designed to teach VLMs spatial reasoning across multiple views, comprising 100K vision-question-answer samples from 18K diverse 3D scenes and 70K robotic manipulation trajectories. It spans three fundamental spatial reasoning tasks: Correspondence (matching objects across views), Verification (validating spatial relationships), and Localization (identifying object positions).
paper_title: Learning Multi-View Spatial Reasoning from Cross-View Relations

description: LinkS²Bench is the first comprehensive benchmark designed to evaluate VLMs' wide-area, dynamic cross-view spatial intelligence, linking 1,022 minutes of dynamic UAV footage with high-resolution satellite imagery covering over 200 km². It comprises 17.9k high-quality QA pairs across 12 fine-grained tasks spanning perception, localization, relation, and reasoning dimensions.
paper_title: Are VLMs Lost Between Sky and Space? LinkS$^2$Bench for UAV-Satellite Dynamic Cross-View Spatial Intelligence

description: SpatiaLab is a comprehensive benchmark for evaluating VLMs' spatial reasoning in realistic, unconstrained contexts, comprising 1,400 visual question-answer pairs across six major categories: Relative Positioning, Depth & Occlusion, Orientation, Size & Scale, Spatial Navigation, and 3D Geometry. It captures real-world complexity and visual noise that existing synthetic benchmarks miss.
paper_title: SpatiaLab: Can Vision-Language Models Perform Spatial Reasoning in the Wild?

description: WildDepth is a multimodal dataset and benchmark suite for depth estimation, behavior detection, and 3D reconstruction from diverse categories of animals ranging from domestic to wild environments with synchronized RGB and LiDAR. It addresses the limitation of existing animal datasets that lack metric scale validation.
paper_title: WildDepth: A Multimodal Dataset for 3D Wildlife Perception and Depth Estimation

description: NovelCraft provides a multimodal episodic dataset for visual novelty detection in complex 3D scenes using a modified Minecraft environment. It focuses on detecting novel objects of varying sizes within complex scenes during a pogo stick assembly task, addressing limitations of existing datasets that only evaluate on simple, well-centered objects.
paper_title: NovelCraft: A Dataset for Novelty Detection and Discovery in Open Worlds

num_samples: 32 data subsets over roughly 100,000 m², 767 GB of data
description: TUM2TWIN provides the first comprehensive multimodal Urban Digital Twin benchmark dataset with georeferenced, semantically aligned 3D models and various terrestrial, mobile, aerial, and satellite observations. It supports robust analysis of sensors and development of advanced 3D reconstruction methods for urban environments.
paper_title: TUM2TWIN: Introducing the Large-Scale Multimodal Urban Digital Twin Benchmark Dataset

description: FlyAwareV2 provides a multimodal dataset encompassing both real and synthetic UAV imagery for urban scene understanding, including RGB, depth, and semantic labels across diverse environmental conditions. It includes benchmarks for synthetic-to-real domain adaptation and multimodal semantic segmentation.
paper_title: FlyAwareV2: A Multimodal Cross-Domain UAV Dataset for Urban Scene Understanding

description: Large-scale 3D visual instruction dataset focusing on precise camera-object relations including orientation, viewpoint, and camera shots. Generated using 3D assets with rendering and diffusion models to create photorealistic images with accurate spatial annotations for improving MLLMs' 3D understanding capabilities.
paper_title: Advancing Multimodal LLMs by Large-Scale 3D Visual Instruction Dataset Generation

description: Large-scale indoor layout dataset for LLM-driven 3D scene generation, covering 18 room types with 29,215 3D object assets and instance-level natural language annotations. Supports flexible multimodal export including point clouds, bounding boxes, multiview images, and semantic masks for 3D scene generation tasks.
paper_title: IL3D: A Large-Scale Indoor Layout Dataset for LLM-Driven 3D Scene Generation

description: PhoCaL provides a multimodal dataset for category-level object pose estimation focusing on photometrically challenging objects including highly reflective, transparent and symmetric objects. It uses robot-supported data acquisition with sub-millimeter pose accuracy and perfect camera synchronization.
paper_title: PhoCaL: A Multi-Modal Dataset for Category-Level Object Pose Estimation with Photometrically Challenging Objects

description: Presents the P3 dataset for building vectorization using aerial LiDAR point clouds, high-resolution aerial imagery, and vectorized 2D building outlines. Demonstrates LiDAR as robust modality for predicting building polygons with decimeter-level accuracy.
paper_title: The P$^3$ dataset: Pixels, Points and Polygons for Multimodal Building Vectorization

description: A multimodal large-scale scene reconstruction benchmark using 3D Gaussian Splatting and NeRF methods, featuring over 6.5 square kilometers with comprehensive RGB and LiDAR data. It reveals the unreliability of current image-based metrics and geometric reconstruction limitations in Gaussian Splatting methods.
paper_title: GauU-Scene V2: Assessing the Reliability of Image-Based Metrics with Expansive Lidar Image Dataset Using 3DGS and NeRF

description: S3E presents an expansive multimodal dataset captured by unmanned ground vehicles across four collaborative trajectory paradigms, featuring 13 outdoor and 5 indoor sequences. It provides meticulously synchronized data streams including 360-degree LiDAR, stereo imagery, IMU, and UWB observations specifically designed for Collaborative SLAM (C-SLAM) evaluation and benchmarking.
paper_title: S3E: A Multi-Robot Multimodal Dataset for Collaborative SLAM

description: STCrowd is a large-scale multimodal dataset for pedestrian perception in crowded scenes with 219K pedestrian instances and 20 persons per frame on average. It provides synchronized LiDAR and camera data with 3D labels for various tasks including LiDAR-only, image-only, and sensor-fusion based pedestrian detection and tracking in dense, occluded scenarios.
paper_title: STCrowd: A Multimodal Dataset for Pedestrian Perception in Crowded Scenes

description: MV-ScanQA is a novel 3D question answering dataset where 68% of questions explicitly require integrating information from multiple views (compared to less than 7% in existing datasets). It rigorously tests multi-view compositional reasoning over distant objects in 3D scenes, addressing limitations of single-viewpoint reasoning in existing datasets.
paper_title: Advancing 3D Scene Understanding with MV-ScanQA Multi-View Reasoning Evaluation and TripAlign Pre-training Dataset

description: A pure-text cognitive benchmark for spatial understanding that isolates text-based spatial reasoning through dual-source methodology. Integrates human-annotated descriptions of real 3D indoor environments with code-generated logically precise scenes.
paper_title: SpatialText: A Pure-Text Cognitive Benchmark for Spatial Understanding in Large Language Models

description: The first large-scale SAR semantic benchmark dataset featuring 65 diverse physical scenes and over 180,000 projections with dense, decoupled annotations for spatial augmented reality. Designed to distinguish between projected content and physical scenes in SAR environments.
paper_title: ProCap: Projection-Aware Captioning for Spatial Augmented Reality

description: A large-scale dataset and benchmark for assembly-oriented spatial reasoning with precise 6D pose annotations. It extends spatial reasoning evaluation beyond 2D into full 3D geometric inference for robotic assembly tasks.
paper_title: AssemLM: Spatial Reasoning Multimodal Large Language Models for Robotic Assembly

description: A controlled benchmark for exact visual path traversal where models must recover the ordered sequence encountered when following continuous polylines with start markers and vertices. Explicitly balances path-structural factors while minimizing reliance on OCR and world knowledge.
paper_title: TraversalBench: Challenging Paths to Follow for Vision Language Models

description: A multimodal spatial question-answering benchmark spanning factory, warehouse, and retail environments. Uses compute-grounded reasoning with structured spatial scene graphs and deterministic computation.
paper_title: Spatial Atlas: Compute-Grounded Reasoning for Spatial-Aware Research Agent Benchmarks

description: A symbolic evaluator for 3D indoor scene synthesis using SceneOnto ontology. Provides object-level and relationship-level assessments for semantic, orientation, and geometric coherence across object relationships.
paper_title: SceneCritic: A Symbolic Evaluator for 3D Indoor Scene Synthesis

description: A benchmark for real-time vision-language models that evaluates perceptual updating and contingency awareness in streaming video. Requires models to generate utterances with precise timing aligned to dynamic visual input.
paper_title: Temporally-Grounded Language Generation: A Benchmark for Real-Time Vision-Language Models

description: Benchmark for streaming spatial question answering with active exploration, where queries are temporally grounded and must be answered using only observations up to specific timestamps. Combines scalable simulator with real-world streaming videos to test long-horizon inference and active perception.
paper_title: See, Remember, Explore: A Benchmark and Baselines for Streaming Spatial Reasoning

description: A fine-grained benchmark for real-time video analysis with MLLMs featuring multi-timestamp QA, hierarchical question structures, and multi-dimensional evaluation. Built on three key principles to evaluate continuous perception, understanding, and reasoning in dynamic video streams.
paper_title: RTV-Bench: Benchmarking MLLM Continuous Perception, Understanding and Reasoning through Real-Time Video

description: PhoStream is the first mobile-centric streaming benchmark unifying on-screen and off-screen scenarios for real-time audio-visual understanding. It contains 5,572 open-ended QA pairs from 578 videos across 4 scenarios, uniquely evaluating models' ability to track streaming inputs and respond at the right time in mobile contexts.
paper_title: PhoStream: Benchmarking Real-World Streaming for Omnimodal Assistants in Mobile Scenarios

description: RIVER Bench is designed for evaluating real-time online video comprehension with a novel framework comprising Retrospective Memory, Live-Perception, and Proactive Anticipation tasks. It mimics interactive dialogues rather than responding to entire videos at once, focusing on real-time processing capabilities.
paper_title: RIVER: A Real-Time Interaction Benchmark for Video LLMs

num_samples: 1,000 streaming QA pairs with 4,576 query points
description: VCBench repositions counting as a minimal probe for diagnosing world state maintenance capability in long videos. It decomposes this into object counting and event counting across 8 subcategories, with frame-by-frame annotations and streaming multi-point queries to evaluate spatial-temporal state maintenance.
paper_title: VCBench: A Streaming Counting Benchmark for Spatial-Temporal State Maintenance in Long Videos

description: SVBench introduces temporal multi-turn question-answering chains for streaming video understanding, featuring consecutive multi-turn dialogues over video segments with temporal linkages between QA chains. It's the first benchmark specifically designed for long-context streaming video evaluation.
paper_title: SVBench: A Benchmark with Temporal Multi-Turn Dialogues for Streaming Video Understanding

description: OmniMMI is a comprehensive multi-modal interaction benchmark for OmniLLMs in streaming video contexts with over 1,121 videos and 2,290 questions. It addresses streaming video understanding and proactive reasoning across six distinct subtasks, focusing on continuous multi-modal data processing capabilities.
paper_title: OmniMMI: A Comprehensive Multi-modal Interaction Benchmark in Streaming Video Contexts

description: Unified evaluation framework for assessing streaming video understanding capabilities under realistic constraints. Uses fixed-capacity memory bank to normalize accessible historical visual context and evaluates trade-offs between efficiency, storage and accuracy.
paper_title: StreamingEval: A Unified Evaluation Protocol towards Realistic Streaming Video Understanding

description: The paper proposes VStream-QA, a novel question answering benchmark specifically designed for online video streaming understanding. This benchmark tests real-time processing of extremely long video streams with asynchronous user queries, addressing the gap in existing offline video benchmarks.
paper_title: Flash-VStream: Memory-Based Real-Time Understanding for Long Video Streams

description: Large-scale dataset specifically designed for streaming video understanding with interleaved video-text sequences and diverse instruction formats. Enables training and evaluation of models for real-time, multi-turn video interactions and proactive response generation.
paper_title: StreamBridge: Turning Your Offline Video Large Language Model into a Proactive Streaming Assistant

description: A benchmark for evaluating Visual Instruction Feedback in streaming videos, where models must recognize visual cues (like gestures) and extract instructions from them. It includes seven key subtasks relevant to visual modality instruction understanding.
paper_title: ViSpeak: Visual Instruction Feedback in Streaming Videos

description: StreamEQA is the first benchmark designed for streaming video question answering in embodied scenarios. It evaluates models along two dimensions: Embodied (perception, interaction, planning) and Streaming (backward, real-time, forward reasoning). Built upon 156 long videos with 42 tasks, it generates approximately 21K question-answer pairs with precise timestamps for streaming video understanding in embodied applications.
paper_title: StreamEQA: Towards Streaming Video Understanding for Embodied Scenarios

description: StreamGaze is the first benchmark to evaluate how effectively MLLMs utilize human gaze signals for temporal and proactive reasoning in streaming videos. It introduces gaze-guided past, present, and proactive tasks with spatio-temporally grounded QA pairs reflecting human perceptual dynamics.
paper_title: StreamGaze: Gaze-Guided Temporal Reasoning and Proactive Understanding in Streaming Videos

description: A new framework and benchmark for Visual Streaming Assistants that evaluates streaming vision-language models in real-time scenarios. Features temporally dense annotations across diverse domains with standardized synchronous and asynchronous evaluation protocols measuring proactiveness, consistency, and accuracy.
paper_title: VSAS-BENCH: Real-Time Evaluation of Visual Streaming Assistant Models

description: Compact evaluation subsets that isolate temporally sensitive samples from existing video QA benchmarks using Frame Selection Sensitivity diagnostic. Identifies samples that genuinely require temporal understanding versus frame-agnostic questions.
paper_title: TempCore: Are Video QA Benchmarks Temporally Grounded? A Frame Selection Sensitivity Analysis and Benchmark

description: SPLICE evaluates event-based reasoning across multiple dimensions (temporal, causal, spatial, contextual, and general knowledge) by having models rearrange video clips from instructional videos into coherent event sequences. It uniquely tests visual reasoning capabilities through event sequencing rather than traditional QA formats.
paper_title: Can you SPLICE it together? A Human Curated Benchmark for Probing Visual Reasoning in VLMs

description: First benchmark evaluating spatio-temporal reasoning in autonomous driving through comprehensive 3D perception. Tests complex interactions among traffic participants using multi-view cameras and LiDAR data with scenario-based framework across 43 diverse traffic scenarios.
paper_title: STSBench: A Spatio-temporal Scenario Benchmark for Multi-modal Large Language Models in Autonomous Driving

description: Extends autonomous driving evaluation to closed-loop VLM assessment, automatically generating diverse behavior-grounded QA pairs for all driving situations including severe deviations. Provides unified protocol for comparing VLMs with traditional agents in dynamic environments.
paper_title: Bench2Drive-VL: Benchmarks for Closed-Loop Autonomous Driving with Vision-Language Models

description: ReaMOT Challenge introduces Reasoning-based Multi-Object Tracking that requires models to identify and track targets satisfying implicit constraints via logical reasoning. It features 1,156 instructions across High-Level Reasoning and Low-Level Perception with 423,359 image-language pairs.
paper_title: ReaMOT: A Benchmark and Framework for Reasoning-based Multi-Object Tracking

description: CycliST evaluates Video Language Models on textual reasoning over cyclical state transitions using synthetic video sequences with periodic patterns. It employs tiered evaluation with increasing difficulty through variations in cyclic objects, scene clutter, and lighting conditions.
paper_title: CycliST: A Video Language Model Benchmark for Reasoning on Cyclical State Transitions

description: A benchmark for evaluating whether models can infer causal dependencies between visual observations and procedural text. Each example requires deciding whether a visual step occurs before or after a referenced text step in procedural plans.
paper_title: ISO-Bench: Benchmarking Multimodal Causal Reasoning in Visual-Language Models through Procedural Plans

description: Task-agnostic benchmark for assessing fine-grained linguistic and temporal grounding capabilities of Video-Language Models. Uses carefully curated counterfactuals and proficiency tests to evaluate temporal aspects of moving images.
paper_title: ViLMA: A Zero-Shot Benchmark for Linguistic and Temporal Grounding in Video-Language Models

description: Multimodal benchmark for temporal reasoning and planning using professional recipe corpus with Temporal Execution Order annotations as graphs. Evaluates LVLMs' understanding of temporal execution required for real-world planning tasks.
paper_title: MATEO: A Multimodal Benchmark for Temporal Reasoning and Planning in LVLMs

description: SVAG-Bench introduces the novel task of Spatio-temporal Video Action Grounding (SVAG) requiring simultaneous detection, tracking, and temporal localization of multiple objects based on natural language action descriptions. It comprises 688 videos with 19,590 annotated records covering 903 unique verbs for fine-grained object-action interaction understanding.
paper_title: SVAG-Bench: A Large-Scale Benchmark for Multi-Instance Spatio-temporal Video Action Grounding

description: B4DL is specifically designed for training and evaluating MLLMs on 4D LiDAR understanding in dynamic outdoor environments. It addresses spatio-temporal reasoning using 4D point clouds with precise spatial geometry and rich temporal cues.
paper_title: B4DL: A Benchmark for 4D LiDAR LLM in Spatio-Temporal Understanding

description: This benchmark assesses the predictive reasoning capabilities of MLLMs across three important domains: abstract pattern reasoning, human activity prediction, and physical interaction prediction. It develops three evaluation methods powered by LLM to robustly quantify a model's performance in predicting and reasoning the future based on multi-visual context, providing a standardized framework for predictive reasoning evaluation.
paper_title: Benchmarking Sequential Visual Input Reasoning and Prediction in Multimodal Large Language Models

description: Presents a diagnostic benchmark for compositional spatio-temporal understanding using minimal-pairs paradigm. Features 600 curated instances (2400 video-question pairs) where video pairs share identical static content but differ in temporal structure, categorizing temporal understanding into three cognitive levels.
paper_title: TimeBlind: A Spatio-Temporal Compositionality Benchmark for Video LLMs

description: Video-MSR is the first benchmark specifically for Multi-hop Spatial Reasoning in dynamic video scenarios. It systematically evaluates complex visual-spatial logical chains through four distinct tasks: constrained localization, chain-based reference retrieval, route planning, and counterfactual physical deduction.
paper_title: Video-MSR: Benchmarking Multi-hop Spatial Reasoning Capabilities of MLLMs

description: ReXTime focuses on reasoning-across-time in videos, testing AI models' ability to perform temporal reasoning when questions and answers occur in different video segments. It includes 921 validation samples and 2,143 test samples, each manually curated for evaluating cause-and-effect relationships across video segments.
paper_title: ReXTime: A Benchmark Suite for Reasoning-Across-Time in Videos

description: DanmakuTPPBench is a comprehensive multi-modal Temporal Point Process benchmark comprising DanmakuTPP-Events (derived from Bilibili video platform with user-generated bullet comments) and DanmakuTPP-QA (challenging QA dataset). It uniquely addresses the gap in multi-modal TPP modeling by providing events with precise timestamps, rich textual content, and corresponding video frames.
paper_title: DanmakuTPPBench: A Multi-modal Benchmark for Temporal Point Process Modeling and Understanding

description: V-STaR introduces Video Spatio-Temporal Reasoning benchmark that decomposes video understanding into Reverse Spatio-Temporal Reasoning (RSTR) tasks. It evaluates what objects are present, when events occur, and where they are located while capturing Chain-of-thought logic, addressing the limitation of existing benchmarks that focus on object presence rather than relational reasoning.
paper_title: V-STaR: Benchmarking Video-LLMs on Video Spatio-Temporal Reasoning

description: VBenchComp provides an automated pipeline to categorize existing video understanding benchmark questions into LLM-Answerable, Semantic, and Temporal domains. This enables fine-grained evaluation by identifying which questions can be answered without video, which remain answerable with shuffled frames, and which require true temporal understanding.
paper_title: Breaking Down Video LLM Benchmarks: Knowledge, Spatial Perception, or True Temporal Understanding?

description: TVBench addresses critical issues in existing video-language benchmarks where temporal reasoning is not required, proposing a novel multiple-choice QA benchmark that genuinely requires high-level temporal understanding. It filters out questions solvable by static information, text alone, or world knowledge.
paper_title: Lost in Time: A New Temporal Benchmark for VideoLLMs

description: TIME develops a novel benchmark for temporal-sensitive video understanding that covers five key temporal dimensions while rigorously filtering out potential shortcuts. It ensures more accurate evaluation of genuine temporal comprehension capabilities in Video-LLMs.
paper_title: TIME: Temporal-Sensitive Multi-Dimensional Instruction Tuning and Robust Benchmarking for Video-LLMs

description: A benchmark dedicated to evaluating fine-grained temporal understanding in videos with ~10K video question-answer pairs from ~2K high-quality human annotations. Tests various temporal abilities like action frequency, motion magnitude, event order, and introduces Multiple Binary Accuracy (MBA) to correct bias in multi-choice QA where models exploit centralized descriptions.
paper_title: TemporalBench: Benchmarking Fine-grained Temporal Understanding for Multimodal Video Models

description: Causal event progression benchmark for chained text-to-multi-image generation grounded in world knowledge and structured by spatiotemporal causality. Includes 1,000 four-stage prompts spanning six scientific and humanities domains with Envision-Score metric.
paper_title: Envision: Benchmarking Unified Understanding & Generation for Causal World Process Insights

description: OpenEvents V1 emphasizes contextual and temporal grounding through event-centric vision-language understanding tasks including event-aware image captioning, retrieving event-relevant news articles, and retrieving event-relevant images from narrative queries. It uniquely focuses on deep reasoning over complex real-world events rather than surface-level descriptions.
paper_title: OpenEvents V1: Large-Scale Benchmark Dataset for Multimodal Event Grounding

description: AoT-PsyPhyBENCH evaluates VLMs' temporal reasoning through arrow of time judgment using psychophysically validated stimuli. It tests whether models can infer temporal direction in natural videos, focusing on physically irreversible processes and causal manual actions with human behavioral baselines.
paper_title: Which Way Does Time Flow? A Psychophysics-Grounded Evaluation for Vision-Language Models

description: TimeCausality evaluates VLMs' causal reasoning ability in the temporal dimension, focusing on irreversible transformations like fruit decay and human aging. It requires deeper comprehension of object state changes over time beyond simple event sequence understanding.
paper_title: TimeCausality: Evaluating the Causal Ability in Time Dimension for Vision Language Models

description: Video-based benchmark with three progressive difficulty levels for evaluating counterfactual reasoning in vision-language models. Tests ability to infer alternative outcomes under hypothetical conditions and understand causal structures in video scenarios.
paper_title: CounterVQA: Evaluating and Improving Counterfactual Reasoning in Vision-Language Models for Video Understanding

description: Multimodal entity tracking benchmark evaluating vision-language models' ability to track entity states across textual and visual modalities. Tests world modeling capabilities through coherent entity representation maintenance over time in structured domains.
paper_title: MET-Bench: Multimodal Entity Tracking for Evaluating the Limitations of Vision-Language and Reasoning Models

description: Temporal-oriented benchmark for fine-grained understanding on dense dynamic videos with two complementary tasks: captioning and QA. Features diverse video scenarios and dynamics with interpretable and robust evaluation criteria.
paper_title: TUNA: Comprehensive Fine-grained Temporal Understanding Evaluation on Dense Dynamic Videos

description: Quadrilingual multiple-choice QA benchmark for assessing video-language models on temporal reasoning of action duration and completion. Pairs everyday activity videos with event completion labels and perfectivity-tailored distractors across four languages.
paper_title: Deep Temporal Reasoning in Video Language Models: A Cross-Linguistic Evaluation of Action Duration and Completion through Perfect Times

description: Automatically generates QA pairs to evaluate temporal logical understanding in VideoQA using temporal operators from logic theory. Tests understanding of event sequences and temporal relationships across 16 categories of temporal logic with varying complexity.
paper_title: TimeLogic: A Temporal Logic Benchmark for Video QA

description: Novel benchmark for cognitive intrusion perception in intelligent railway transportation systems. Integrates curated datasets with cognitively driven question-answer annotations to support spatio-temporal reasoning and prediction for safety-critical rail scenarios.
paper_title: CogRail: Benchmarking VLMs in Cognitive Intrusion Perception for Intelligent Railway Transportation Systems

description: Benchmarks temporal compositionality in video generation by focusing on emergence of new concepts and relation transitions over time. Features meticulously crafted text prompts with initial and final states, ground truth videos, and new metrics measuring completeness of component transitions that correlate better with human judgments.
paper_title: TC-Bench: Benchmarking Temporal Compositionality in Text-to-Video and Image-to-Video Generation

description: Automated framework for modeling, evaluating, and measuring causal understanding of Video Generation Models in real-world scenarios. Combines causal analysis techniques with LLM assistance to assess causal behavior without human annotation, providing multi-level causal evaluation metrics.
paper_title: VACT: A Video Automatic Causal Testing System and a Benchmark

description: Benchmark specifically designed to assess Physical Coherence of generated videos with 120 prompts covering 7 categories of physical principles. Introduces PhyCoPredictor, an automated evaluation model using optical flow-guided frame prediction that aligns closely with human evaluation.
paper_title: A Physical Coherence Benchmark for Evaluating Video Generation Models via Optical Flow-guided Frame Prediction

description: Novel benchmark for evaluating temporal and metamorphic capabilities in text-to-time-lapse video generation. Features 1,649 prompts across biological, human-created, meteorological, and physical phenomena categories, with new metrics MTScore and CHScore for measuring metamorphic amplitude and temporal coherence.
paper_title: ChronoMagic-Bench: A Benchmark for Metamorphic Evaluation of Text-to-Time-lapse Video Generation

description: Comprehensive benchmark focusing on evaluating complex temporal causality and world knowledge in T2V models with event-level descriptions. Features 300 prompts, 815 events, and 793 evaluation questions across ten evaluation dimensions covering dynamic and static properties.
paper_title: VideoVerse: Does Your T2V Generator Have World Model Capability to Synthesize Videos?

description: A large-scale open-world resource built from 11,328 real-world videos including an 8,000-example benchmark for evaluating Teleo-Spatial Intelligence (TSI). Tests ability to link spatiotemporal change to goal-directed structure and physical dynamics reasoning.
paper_title: Vision-language models lag human performance on physical dynamics and intent reasoning

description: Video-CoT contains 192,000 fine-grained spatiotemporal question-answer pairs and 23,000 high-quality CoT-annotated samples designed to enhance spatiotemporal understanding using Chain-of-Thought methodologies. It provides a comprehensive benchmark with 750 images per task and tailored evaluation metrics for assessing video comprehension capabilities.
paper_title: Video-CoT: A Comprehensive Dataset for Spatiotemporal Understanding of Videos Based on Chain-of-Thought

description: Do-Undo introduces a novel benchmark task requiring Vision-Language Models to simulate the outcome of a physical action and then accurately reverse it, reflecting true cause-and-effect in the visual world. Unlike prior work focused on object-level edits, it evaluates understanding of physically plausible scene transformations driven by real-world actions with a large-scale dataset of reversible actions from real-world videos.
paper_title: Do-Undo: Generating and Reversing Physical Actions in Vision-Language Models

description: CaTS-Bench is a comprehensive benchmark for Context-aware Time Series reasoning across 11 diverse domains, centered on 1746 human-rewritten captions that measure how effectively models translate numeric trends into interpretable narratives. It addresses the scarcity of human-annotated data with a scalable pipeline for generating high-fidelity synthetic captions and includes a diagnostic suite of 910 multiple-choice questions with tailored numeric metrics.
paper_title: CaTS-Bench: Can Language Models Describe Time Series?

description: The paper introduces Event-Bench, an event-oriented long video understanding benchmark with six event-related tasks and 2,190 test instances. This benchmark specifically tests video event understanding ability and addresses short-cut bias by ensuring rich events require watching entire videos rather than just a few frames.
paper_title: Towards Event-oriented Long Video Understanding

description: The paper proposes Causal2Needles, a long-context video understanding benchmark that assesses two crucial abilities: extracting information from two separate locations in a long video and modeling cause-and-effect relationships in human behaviors. It evaluates through noncausal one-needle, causal one-needle, and complex causal two-needle questions.
paper_title: Two Causally Related Needles in a Video Haystack

description: The paper introduces MotionBench, a benchmark for evaluating motion understanding across four motion types: linear, curved, rotational, and contact-based. This benchmark specifically tests video models' ability to understand and reason about different types of motion in video content.
paper_title: Efficient Motion-Aware Video MLLM

description: A dataset with 40K videos and 500K temporal grounding annotations for instructed temporal grounding in videos. It enables adaptive frame sampling based on user instructions and focuses on instruction-conditioned temporal reasoning.
paper_title: VideoITG: Multimodal Video Understanding with Instructed Temporal Grounding

description: Two high-quality multi-task video reasoning datasets for supervised fine-tuning and reinforcement learning, combining temporal grounding and question answering tasks. They enable training agentic video reasoning systems with multimodal chain-of-thought capabilities.
paper_title: Thinking With Videos: Multimodal Tool-Augmented Reinforcement Learning for Long Video Reasoning

description: FineAction-CGR is specifically devised to assess Vid-LLMs' ability to process fine-grained temporal understanding tasks. It focuses on evaluating temporal grounding and detailed temporal reasoning capabilities with high-frequency temporal details.
paper_title: SlowFocus: Enhancing Fine-grained Temporal Understanding in Video LLM

description: The first benchmark for Temporal Visual Screening (TVS), designed to evaluate models' ability to retain focus-critical video segments and reconstruct queries to their most direct form while preserving answer consistency. It tests temporal information screening for improved video-language understanding.
paper_title: Video-LLMs with Temporal Visual Screening

description: AoTBench is a new multi-faceted benchmark that probes temporally challenging questions focused on Arrow of Time (AoT) awareness in video understanding. It evaluates models' ability to perceive and utilize temporal directionality and time's irreversible flow in video when responding to language queries.
paper_title: Seeing the Arrow of Time in Large Multimodal Models

description: Rigorously designed VideoQA benchmark targeting causal action reasoning, temporal action reasoning, and common scene comprehension. Goes beyond superficial scene description towards deeper understanding of videos with multi-choice and open-ended QA tasks.
paper_title: NExT-QA:Next Phase of Question-Answering to Explaining Temporal Actions

description: A spatio-temporal reasoning benchmark involving kinematic instruction tuning with real-world videos and 3D annotations. Tests understanding of object motion dynamics including traveled distance, speed, movement direction, and inter-object relationships.
paper_title: ST-VLM: Kinematic Instruction Tuning for Spatio-Temporal Reasoning in Vision-Language Models

description: First benchmark dataset for evaluating temporal awareness in VLMs with over 10,000 images and temporal ground truth. Tests VLMs' ability to position visual content in time and introduces methodology to reveal temporal structure in embedding spaces.
paper_title: A Matter of Time: Revealing the Structure of Time in Vision-Language Models

description: Comprehensive Visual Question Answering dataset with 2,900 scenarios and 1.1 million agent-level samples for risk assessment in autonomous driving. Provides BEV-based sequential images with quantitative risk annotations enabling spatio-temporal reasoning about evolving risks.
paper_title: NuRisk: A Visual Question Answering Dataset for Agent-Level Risk Assessment in Autonomous Driving

description: The first benchmark specifically designed to evaluate spatiotemporal reasoning capabilities of VLMs. Tests translational and rotational motions, perspective awareness, and motion continuity in real-world and synthetic videos.
paper_title: VLM4D: Towards Spatiotemporal Awareness in Vision Language Models

description: A benchmark dataset with over 600K video-text pairs for interpretable reasoning in autonomous driving. It characterizes driving as sequential perception, prediction, and reasoning steps, with automatically collected QA pairs from diverse outdoor driving datasets to study chain-based reasoning.
paper_title: Reason2Drive: Towards Interpretable and Chain-based Reasoning for Autonomous Driving

description: A Visual Question-Answering dataset specifically designed to elicit and evaluate Foresight Intelligence - the capability to anticipate and interpret future events. It enables the first comprehensive study of VLMs under foresight-oriented tasks and can assess world models through semantic coherence.
paper_title: Thinking Ahead: Foresight Intelligence in MLLMs and World Models

description: Progress-Bench systematically evaluates VLMs' ability to estimate task progress from partial visual observations. It tests whether models can reason about long-horizon dynamics and infer how far a task has progressed, rather than just recognizing static visual content, requiring temporal understanding of task completion states.
paper_title: PROGRESSLM: Towards Progress Reasoning in Vision-Language Models

description: TwiFF introduces TwiFF-2.7M, the first large-scale temporally grounded Visual Chain-of-Thought dataset from 2.7 million video clips, and TwiFF-Bench with 1,078 samples for dynamic visual reasoning. It assesses both reasoning trajectory plausibility and answer correctness in open-ended dynamic settings, enabling temporal visual question answering with future frame generation.
paper_title: TwiFF (Think With Future Frames): A Large-Scale Dataset for Dynamic Visual Reasoning

description: AEGIS is a large-scale benchmark targeting detection of hyper-realistic and semantically nuanced AI-generated videos from diverse state-of-the-art models including Sora. It features specially constructed challenging subsets with multimodal annotations for authenticity detection and forgery localization.
paper_title: AEGIS: Authenticity Evaluation Benchmark for AI-Generated Video Sequences

description: Video Language Bias Evaluation Benchmark designed to assess language bias in video-involved LVLMs through ambiguous video contrast and interrogative question probing. Tests models' tendency to prioritize language over video content, leading to incorrect responses.
paper_title: VidLBEval: Benchmarking and Mitigating Language Bias in Video-Involved LVLMs

description: EgoIllusion is the first benchmark specifically designed to evaluate MLLM hallucinations in egocentric videos. It comprises 1,400 videos with 8,000 human-annotated questions designed to trigger hallucinations in both visual and auditory cues from first-person perspective videos.
paper_title: EGOILLUSION: Benchmarking Hallucinations in Egocentric Video Understanding

description: Elevates video anomaly analysis from descriptive understanding to structured multi-stage reasoning through Perception-Cognition-Action Chain-of-Thought. Features 8,641 videos with diverse question types corresponding to different reasoning depths.
paper_title: Advancing Adaptive Multi-Stage Video Anomaly Reasoning: A Benchmark Dataset and Method

description: The first benchmark dedicated to long-video hallucination focusing on Semantic Aggregation Hallucination (SAH) that arises when aggregating frame-level semantics into event-level groups. Systematically investigates hallucinations in long videos with increased semantic complexity across multiple events.
paper_title: ELV-Halluc: Benchmarking Semantic Aggregation Hallucinations in Long Video Understanding

description: VISE is the first benchmark designed to evaluate sycophantic behavior in Video-LLMs, where models align with user input even when it contradicts visual evidence. It pioneeringly brings linguistic perspectives on sycophancy into the video domain, enabling fine-grained analysis across multiple sycophancy types and interaction patterns.
paper_title: Flattery in Motion: Benchmarking and Analyzing Sycophancy in Video-LLMs

description: NOAH systematically evaluates narrative prior-induced errors by constructing composite videos through inserting clips from other sources into target videos. It enables controlled analysis of hallucinations and omissions across varying semantic similarity and insertion positions, providing the first standardized evaluation of narrative prior-induced errors in Video LLMs.
paper_title: NOAH: Benchmarking Narrative Prior driven Hallucination and Omission in Video Large Language Models

description: INFACT provides comprehensive coverage of both faithfulness and factuality hallucinations with fine-grained taxonomies across real and synthetic videos. It evaluates models in four modes including Visual Degradation, Evidence Corruption, and Temporal Intervention, introducing Resist Rate and Temporal Sensitivity Score metrics to quantify reliability under induced challenging conditions.
paper_title: INFACT: A Diagnostic Benchmark for Induced Faithfulness and Factuality Hallucinations in Video-LLMs

description: ViBe is a large-scale dataset of hallucinated videos from Text-to-Video models with 3,782 manually annotated videos identifying five major hallucination types: Vanishing Subject, Omission Error, Numeric Variability, Subject Dysmorphia, and Visual Incongruity. It serves as a benchmark for evaluating T2V reliability and advancing hallucination detection.
paper_title: ViBe: A Text-to-Video Benchmark for Evaluating Hallucination in Large Multimodal Models

description: A comprehensive benchmark for evaluating hallucinations of large multimodal models in video understanding tasks, built upon three dimensions: hallucination causes, hallucination aspects, and question formats. The first benchmark specifically designed to study hallucination problems in video modality, which is more dynamic and challenging than static modalities.
paper_title: Exploring Hallucination of Large Multimodal Models in Video Understanding: Benchmark, Analysis and Mitigation

description: VANE-Bench assesses Video-LMMs' ability to detect and localize anomalies and inconsistencies in videos. It features synthetically generated videos with five categories of anomalies (unnatural transformations, appearance, pass-through, disappearance, sudden appearance) plus real-world crime and unusual event samples.
paper_title: VANE-Bench: Video Anomaly Evaluation Benchmark for Conversational LMMs

description: First benchmark for evaluating VLM robustness under dynamic visual degradation in temporal sequences. Tests hallucination persistence, error recovery, and temporal consistency through multi-turn question-answering tasks with physics-based corruptions including motion blur, sensor noise, and compression artifacts.
paper_title: DIQ-H: Evaluating Hallucination Persistence in VLMs Under Temporal Visual Degradation

description: Synthetic dataset of physics- and commonsense-violating scenes generated with Veo2, Sora, and Kling. Includes expert-annotated question-answer pairs across four categories of violations to test VLMs' ability to detect impossible or inconsistent events.
paper_title: VideoHallu: Evaluating and Mitigating Multi-modal Hallucinations on Synthetic Video Understanding

description: VidHalluc is the largest benchmark specifically designed to examine hallucinations in MLLMs for video understanding. It uniquely tests hallucination vulnerabilities across three critical dimensions: action, temporal sequence, and scene transition using paired videos that highlight cases prone to hallucinations.
paper_title: VidHalluc: Evaluating Temporal Hallucinations in Multimodal Large Language Models for Video Understanding

description: First comprehensive benchmark for hallucination detection in large video-language models. Categories hallucinations into intrinsic and extrinsic types with detailed subcategories using adversarial binary VideoQA method.
paper_title: VideoHallucer: Evaluating Intrinsic and Extrinsic Hallucinations in Large Video-Language Models

description: FineVAU introduces fine-grained Video Anomaly Understanding focused on rich, domain-specific understanding of anomalous events through three key elements: What (events), Who (entities), and Where (location). It features human-aligned evaluation metrics and comprehensive visual information for detailed anomaly analysis.
paper_title: FineVAU: A Novel Human-Aligned Benchmark for Fine-Grained Video Anomaly Understanding

description: First comprehensive benchmark for safety evaluation of LVLMs under video-text attacks. Features synthesized videos paired with harmful or benign queries across 48 fine-grained unsafe categories, addressing temporal dynamics that static image evaluations miss.
paper_title: Video-SafetyBench: A Benchmark for Safety Evaluation of Video LVLMs

description: A fine-grained benchmark with 400 highly deceptive AI-generated videos and 100 real videos, annotated across 15 authenticity dimensions covering perceptual, optical, physical, and temporal cues. Enables dimension-wise evaluation of LVLM capabilities in AI-generated video detection.
paper_title: GenVideoLens: Where LVLMs Fall Short in AI-Generated Video Detection?

description: UNSCENE evaluates action-scene hallucination in Video-LLMs, testing whether models incorrectly predict actions based on scene context or scenes based on observed actions. This benchmark specifically targets cross-modal hallucination between temporal actions and spatial scenes.
paper_title: MASH-VLM: Mitigating Action-Scene Hallucination in Video-LLMs through Disentangled Spatial-Temporal Representations

description: LogicGaze is a novel benchmark framework that tests whether VLMs can validate sequential causal chains against visual inputs, specifically targeting hallucination issues. It integrates causal sequences with visually contradictory yet linguistically plausible perturbations from video segments and images, compelling models to verify the authenticity of each reasoning step through tripartite evaluation.
paper_title: LogicGaze: Benchmarking Causal Consistency in Visual Narratives via Counterfactual Verification

description: This work proposes the first frame-accurate benchmark for evaluating small VLMs on video question-answering under controlled frame-sampling strategies. It addresses the suspected frame-sampling bias in current video benchmarks by providing a reproducible and unbiased protocol that reveals both data-specific and task-specific behaviors of models under different frame-sampling techniques.
paper_title: Frame Sampling Strategies Matter: A Benchmark for small vision language models

description: FAQ introduces temporal deepfake analysis as a multiple-choice task with a three-level hierarchy: Facial Perception, Temporal Deepfake Grounding, and Forensic Reasoning. It uniquely focuses on temporal inconsistencies in video forgeries rather than just static artifacts, challenging VLMs to reason about dynamic forensic cues.
paper_title: Beyond Static Artifacts: A Forensic Benchmark for Video Deepfake Reasoning in Vision Language Models

description: UAL-Bench is the first comprehensive benchmark for unusual activity localization in videos, featuring three datasets and evaluating models' ability to localize rare events like human errors or surveillance incidents. It addresses the challenge of insufficient representation of unusual events in pretraining datasets.
paper_title: UAL-Bench: The First Comprehensive Unusual Activity Localization Benchmark

description: MotionBench comprehensively evaluates fine-grained motion comprehension in video understanding models through six primary categories of motion-oriented question types. It addresses the under-explored capability of fine-grained motion understanding with data from diverse real-world video content.
paper_title: MotionBench: Benchmarking and Improving Fine-grained Video Motion Understanding for Vision Language Models

description: Native-Italian benchmark for fine-grained investigation of VLM reasoning on videos across twelve categories. Evaluates consistency between visual statement verification and open-ended QA tasks using aggregated metrics, focusing on Italian culture and native-speaker language data.
paper_title: All-in-one: Understanding and Generation in Multimodal Reasoning with the MAIA Benchmark

description: The first systematic evaluation framework for world knowledge in text-to-video generation, covering 6 major categories, 60 subcategories, and 1,200 prompts across physics, nature, activity, culture, causality, and object domains. It uniquely tests factual accuracy and semantic consistency in video generation.
paper_title: T2VWorldBench: A Benchmark for Evaluating World Knowledge in Text-to-Video Generation

description: The first large-scale fine-grained benchmark for video caption quality evaluation comprising 5,677 videos and 109,796 question-answer pairs. It uniquely systematically annotates across 21 fine-grained dimensions critical for text-to-video generation and introduces automated evaluation pipeline with three metrics.
paper_title: VCapsBench: A Large-scale Fine-grained Benchmark for Video Caption Quality Evaluation

description: FiVE is a fine-grained video editing benchmark with 74 real-world and 26 generated videos, featuring 6 editing types and 420 object-level editing prompt pairs with corresponding masks. It evaluates precision in object-level modifications while maintaining context and temporal consistency.
paper_title: FiVE: A Fine-grained Video Editing Benchmark for Evaluating Emerging Diffusion and Rectified Flow Models

description: Movie101v2 is a large-scale, bilingual dataset for automatic movie narration with enhanced data quality. It breaks down movie narration into three progressive stages with corresponding evaluation metrics, focusing on generating video-aligned plot descriptions across multiple movie shots.
paper_title: Movie101v2: Improved Movie Narration Benchmark

description: MORSE-500 provides 500 programmatically generated video clips spanning six complementary reasoning categories (abstract, physical, planning, spatial, temporal, mathematical). Its script-driven design allows fine-grained control over complexity and can generate arbitrarily challenging instances for stress-testing next-generation models.
paper_title: MORSE-500: A Programmatically Controllable Video Benchmark to Stress-Test Multimodal Reasoning

description: ExAct is a video-language benchmark for expert-level understanding of skilled physical human activities across 11 activities in 6 domains (Sports, Bike Repair, Cooking, Health, Music, Dance). It requires nuanced, fine-grained understanding through carefully designed multiple-choice questions.
paper_title: ExAct: A Video-Language Benchmark for Expert Action Analysis

description: AutoEval-Video comprehensively evaluates LVLMs on open-ended video question answering across 9 skill dimensions covering perception, comprehension, and generation. It uses instance-specific evaluation rules and adversarial annotation mechanisms for robust automatic evaluation with GPT-4.
paper_title: AutoEval-Video: An Automatic Benchmark for Assessing Large Vision Language Models in Open-Ended Video Question Answering

description: A large-scale, multilingual event-centric video retrieval benchmark featuring news videos and 3,906 queries targeting specific world events. It requires systems to leverage visual content, audio, embedded text, and text metadata for successful retrieval.
paper_title: MultiVENT 2.0: A Massive Multilingual Benchmark for Event-Centric Video Retrieval

description: A fine-grained benchmark for video captioning and retrieval with high-quality video-caption pairs featuring manually separated spatial and temporal annotations. It introduces ReBias and CapST metrics to investigate spatial and temporal biases in video-language models.
paper_title: CaReBench: A Fine-Grained Benchmark for Video Captioning and Retrieval

description: Introduces a real-world surveillance video retrieval benchmark focused on vehicle-related actions. Defines inter-pair and intra-pair evaluation protocols to assess cross-action discrimination and temporal direction understanding.
paper_title: SOVABench: A Vehicle Surveillance Action Retrieval Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models

description: The largest open-ended video QA benchmark for surveillance domain with 589,380 QA pairs spanning 12 cognitively diverse question types. Covers temporal reasoning, causal inference, spatial understanding, and anomaly interpretation in surveillance contexts.
paper_title: SurveillanceVQA-589K: A Benchmark for Comprehensive Surveillance Video-Language Understanding with Large Models

num_samples: 180 non-rigid motion videos with 2,340 task instructions and 360 multiple-choice questions
description: First dedicated benchmark for evaluating non-rigid video editing with high-quality dataset from six physics-based categories. Introduces NRVE-Acc evaluation metric using VLMs to assess physical compliance, temporal consistency, and instruction alignment in complex dynamics.
paper_title: Beyond Rigid: Benchmarking Non-Rigid Video Editing

description: FIOVA provides a human-centric benchmark with 3,002 real-world videos (33.6s each) independently annotated by five annotators per video. It introduces FIOVA-DQ metric with cognitive weights from annotator consensus and complexity-aware analysis framework based on inter-annotator variation to evaluate human-machine alignment in video captioning.
paper_title: FIOVA: A Multi-Annotator Benchmark for Human-Aligned Video Captioning

description: SOK-Bench provides situated video reasoning with 44K questions and 10K situations featuring instance-level annotations. It combines situated knowledge extracted from videos with extended open-world knowledge for problem-solving, using automatic generation through LLM/MLLM combinations with self-prompting and manual quality assurance.
paper_title: SOK-Bench: A Situated Video Reasoning Benchmark with Aligned Open-World Knowledge

description: ChineseVideoBench is a pioneering benchmark specifically designed for evaluating MLLMs in Chinese Video Question Answering. It comprises 8 main classes and 12 sub-classes, demanding both deep video understanding and nuanced Chinese linguistic and cultural awareness, addressing the need for culturally-aware video evaluation frameworks.
paper_title: ChineseVideoBench: Benchmarking Multi-modal Large Models for Chinese Video Question Answering

description: HumanVBench comprises 16 carefully designed tasks exploring human-centric video understanding across two primary dimensions: inner emotion and outer manifestations. It spans static/dynamic, basic/complex, and single-modal/cross-modal aspects with automated pipelines for video annotation.
paper_title: HumanVBench: Exploring Human-Centric Video Understanding Capabilities of MLLMs with Synthetic Benchmark Data

description: MT-Video-Bench is a holistic video understanding benchmark that evaluates MLLMs in multi-turn dialogues rather than single-turn Q&A. It assesses 6 core competencies focusing on perceptivity and interactivity across 1,000 meticulously curated multi-turn dialogues from diverse domains, aligned with real-world applications like interactive sports analysis.
paper_title: MT-Video-Bench: A Holistic Video Understanding Benchmark for Evaluating Multimodal LLMs in Multi-Turn Dialogues

description: ExpVid is the first benchmark designed to systematically evaluate MLLMs on scientific experiment videos. It features a three-level task hierarchy mirroring the scientific process: fine-grained perception, procedural understanding, and scientific reasoning that connects experiments to published conclusions.
paper_title: ExpVid: A Benchmark for Experiment Video Understanding & Reasoning

description: Proposes the first omnimodal benchmark for interactive livestream video understanding featuring 24 diverse tasks. Incorporates audio, speech, and real-time comments modalities to evaluate perceptual, reasoning, and livestream-specific challenges through standardized semi-automatic annotation workflow.
paper_title: LiViBench: An Omnimodal Benchmark for Interactive Livestream Video Understanding

description: Introduces a comprehensive benchmark for fine-grained video motion understanding comprising 1,776 videos with structured manual annotations. Features both close-ended (8,184 QA pairs) and open-ended evaluation tasks spanning six distinct motion comprehension sub-tasks.
paper_title: FAVOR-Bench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Fine-Grained Video Motion Understanding

description: Presents a large-scale vision-language benchmark for evaluating MLLMs in high-risk traffic scenarios involving vulnerable road users. Comprises 1K real-world dashcam accident videos with 6K multiple-choice QA pairs across six safety-critical categories and 1K dense scene descriptions.
paper_title: VRU-Accident: A Vision-Language Benchmark for Video Question Answering and Dense Captioning for Accident Scene Understanding

description: A multimodal retrieval-augmented generation benchmark constructed from 599 videos covering chart-heavy reports, news broadcasts, and software tutorials. What makes it unique is its focus on fine-grained multimodal information retrieval and reasoning over long temporal video spans requiring high-density format understanding.
paper_title: CFVBench: A Comprehensive Video Benchmark for Fine-grained Multimodal Retrieval-Augmented Generation

description: ViRectify is a comprehensive benchmark to evaluate MLLMs' fine-grained correction capability in video reasoning scenarios. It challenges models to perform step-wise error identification and generate rationales with key video evidence grounding across dynamic perception, scientific reasoning, and embodied decision-making domains.
paper_title: ViRectify: A Challenging Benchmark for Video Reasoning Correction with Multimodal Large Language Models

description: CrossVid is the first benchmark designed to comprehensively evaluate MLLMs' spatial-temporal reasoning ability in cross-video contexts. It provides 5,331 videos with 9,015 question-answering pairs across four high-level dimensions and ten specific tasks, requiring simultaneous understanding of multiple videos.
paper_title: CrossVid: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Evaluating Cross-Video Reasoning in Multimodal Large Language Models

description: The first benchmark tailored for compositional text-to-video generation evaluation. Encompasses diverse compositionality aspects including attribute binding, spatial relationships, motion binding, object interactions, and generative numeracy with specialized MLLM-based evaluation metrics.
paper_title: T2V-CompBench: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Compositional Text-to-video Generation

description: Introduces a multimodal dataset with expert annotations for benchmarking MLLMs and LLMs in financial discourse analysis, focusing on stock recommendations from social media influencers. The benchmark evaluates models' ability to analyze multimodal signals including tone, delivery style, and facial expressions beyond text-based financial analysis to assess investment conviction.
paper_title: VideoConviction: A Multimodal Benchmark for Human Conviction and Stock Market Recommendations

description: First diagnostic benchmark for assessing cross-video relational reasoning across multiple videos, spanning three hierarchical tiers: cross-video object association, cross-video event association, and cross-video complex reasoning. Built from five domain-diverse video clusters, it challenges models to analyze and integrate spatiotemporal patterns from dynamic visual streams.
paper_title: CVBench: Benchmarking Cross-Video Synergies for Complex Multimodal Reasoning

description: Benchmark specifically designed to assess object state change (OSC) performance in text-to-video generation models, constructed from instructional cooking data. Systematically organizes action-object interactions into regular, novel, and compositional scenarios to evaluate both in-distribution performance and generalization in understanding transformations of object states induced by actions.
paper_title: OSCBench: Benchmarking Object State Change in Text-to-Video Generation

description: First comprehensive benchmark for evaluating Image-Grounded Video Perception and Reasoning, consisting of 967 videos paired with meticulously annotated image-text queries across 13 tasks (7 perception and 6 reasoning tasks) and 5 categories. Addresses the significant role of image context in video comprehension, an area largely overlooked by existing evaluation frameworks.
paper_title: IV-Bench: A Benchmark for Image-Grounded Video Perception and Reasoning in Multimodal LLMs

description: Novel benchmark for evaluating Image-to-Video generation models with focus on semantic understanding and reasoning across four dimensions: spatial understanding, attribute binding, category understanding, and reasoning. Uses instance-level and feedback-based reasoning pipelines with MLLM evaluation methods to assess beyond video quality and temporal consistency.
paper_title: UI2V-Bench: An Understanding-based Image-to-video Generation Benchmark

num_samples: 900 text questions + 1,701 video clips with 12,048 QA pairs
description: SPORTU provides comprehensive sports understanding evaluation through two components: text-based rule comprehension and video-based multi-level reasoning from recognition to complex foul detection. It uniquely combines sports rule understanding with visual reasoning across 7 sports with slow-motion video analysis.
paper_title: SPORTU: A Comprehensive Sports Understanding Benchmark for Multimodal Large Language Models

num_samples: 2,501 questions from 429 news groups with 1,405 videos
description: VNU-Bench is the first benchmark for multi-source, cross-video understanding in news domain. It uniquely requires comparing perspectives from different sources, aligning multimodal evidence across sources, and synthesizing multi-source information rather than single-source intra-video reasoning.
paper_title: VNU-Bench: A Benchmarking Dataset for Multi-Source Multimodal News Video Understanding

description: HumanVideo-MME provides comprehensive evaluation of MLLMs in human-centric video understanding across 13 tasks from basic attribute perception to advanced cognitive reasoning. It spans 50 distinct visual scenarios and covers videos from short-term (10 seconds) to long-term (up to 30 minutes) with diverse question formats.
paper_title: HumanVideo-MME: Benchmarking MLLMs for Human-Centric Video Understanding

description: A benchmark for multimodal deep reasoning in videos requiring long-range, multi-frame evidence analysis and reasoning over hidden information rather than direct perception. Features manually annotated tasks with extensive real-world user understanding and carefully designed distractor strategies to reduce model shortcuts.
paper_title: MMR-V: What's Left Unsaid? A Benchmark for Multimodal Deep Reasoning in Videos

description: A modern benchmark suite specifically for instruction-guided video editing assessment with diverse database spanning seven semantic dimensions and video lengths from 32 to 1,024 frames. Establishes three-dimensional evaluation protocol covering video quality, instruction compliance, and video fidelity.
paper_title: IVEBench: Modern Benchmark Suite for Instruction-Guided Video Editing Assessment

description: SeriesBench evaluates narrative-driven drama series understanding across 28 specialized tasks requiring deep narrative comprehension. Unlike existing video benchmarks focused on standalone videos and visual elements, it assesses complex continuous narratives and introduces a novel long-span narrative annotation method for understanding plot structures and character relationships.
paper_title: SeriesBench: A Benchmark for Narrative-Driven Drama Series Understanding

description: MVBench addresses temporal understanding in dynamic video tasks through 20 challenging video tasks that cannot be solved with single frames. It introduces a novel static-to-dynamic method to systematically generate video tasks requiring temporal skills from perception to cognition, using automatic conversion of video annotations into multiple-choice QA.
paper_title: MVBench: A Comprehensive Multi-modal Video Understanding Benchmark

description: Trust-videoLLMs is the first comprehensive benchmark evaluating trustworthiness in videoLLMs across five critical dimensions: truthfulness, robustness, safety, fairness, and privacy. It comprises 30 tasks with adapted, synthetic, and annotated videos to assess spatiotemporal risks and cross-modal impact.
paper_title: Benchmarking the Trustworthiness in Multimodal LLMs for Video Understanding

description: POVFNDB is a process-oriented benchmark comprising 10 tasks designed to systematically evaluate MLLMs' perception, understanding, and reasoning capabilities in video fake news detection. It contains 36,240 human-annotated QA pairs spanning 15 distinct evaluation dimensions for the entire detection process.
paper_title: Perception, Understanding and Reasoning, A Multimodal Benchmark for Video Fake News Detection

description: Multi-modal Untrimmed Video Retrieval benchmark supporting video-centric multi-modal queries through text descriptions, video tags, and mask prompts. Focuses on multi-level visual correspondence across six levels (copy, event, scene, instance, action, others) for long-video platform applications.
paper_title: MUVR: A Multi-Modal Untrimmed Video Retrieval Benchmark with Multi-Level Visual Correspondence

description: First comprehensive full-spectrum Multi-Modal Evaluation benchmark for MLLMs in video analysis spanning 6 primary visual domains with 30 subfields. Features diversity in video duration (11 seconds to 1 hour), breadth in data modalities (video, subtitles, audio), and rigorous manual annotation quality.
paper_title: Video-MME: The First-Ever Comprehensive Evaluation Benchmark of Multi-modal LLMs in Video Analysis

description: COVER is a multidimensional multimodal benchmark for counterfactual video reasoning that systematically evaluates MLLMs across abstract-concrete and perception-cognition dimensions. It uniquely decomposes complex queries into structured sub-questions, enabling fine-grained analysis of reasoning capabilities in dynamic video environments.
paper_title: Reasoning is All You Need for Video Generalization: A Counterfactual Benchmark with Sub-question Evaluation

description: VideoDR is the first video deep research benchmark for video-conditioned open-domain question answering that requires cross-frame visual anchor extraction, interactive web retrieval, and multi-hop reasoning over joint video-web evidence. It uniquely combines video analysis with open-web search capabilities for comprehensive video understanding.
paper_title: Watching, Reasoning, and Searching: A Video Deep Research Benchmark on Open Web for Agentic Video Reasoning

description: Video-Bench provides comprehensive evaluation across three distinct levels: Video-exclusive Understanding, Prior Knowledge-based QA, and Comprehension and Decision-making. It comprises 10 meticulously crafted tasks and includes an automatic toolkit for processing model outputs, targeting the ultimate goal of achieving artificial general intelligence in video understanding.
